Title: Leave blank to Auto-calculate - Blank Transaction Number - Blank Estimate Number - Blank Invoice Number

2.) Custom Numbering Might be on in QuickBooks
3.) The Transaction might be in conflict
Solution: Unfortunately, at this time, there is no easy way to customize a warning to the user explaining why their transaction is not having a number assigned.

Title: Show Customer Balance Update in Realtime - Customer / Invoice Balance Not Updating after Payment
Description: My client's balance on the invoice screen is showing 0.00 (I was the one who took his payment so I know he's all set) but his balance under the Customer Details is showing that he still owes the invoice amount. He will also see this when he logs into his portal which is incorrect. Can you please advise?
Solution:
The customer balance requires an active QuickBooks Connection to be updated at this time:

1.) The customer should check that QuickBooks is connected
2.) The customer should check if the Customer, Invoice, and Payment are not in conflict
3.) Lastly, if everything is working the Customer Balance should update in about 15 seconds.

We certainly can and should improve this experience; however, customization won't be a fool proof solution.

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Email Transactions - Option to Edit Emails before sending
Description:
Many customers have requested we have the ability to edit the template that will be sent off to the customer from a transaction screen. This will allow them to quickly modify the transaction and personalize it to each customer prior to sending it out. It should also allow them to edit the to/ cc/ bcc fields per transactions. 

Please quote how long it would take to implement:

Hello, recently we got the following feedback from account, cmisales:
'This particular issue has been raised by me since before I signed up for Method. The absolute poor integration of email services within the product. There was a ray of hope/progress on Sept 27 when the email service for invoices allowed cc and bcc fields to be accessed.

My need is very simple.
When I want to email a quote, or invoice, or packing slip from a button on one of these screens, I want to transfer control to the method email app that has

the send-to field populated from the invoice contact,

the subject line populated with some standard text along with the quote/invoice/packing slip number. ie....“CMI  xxxxxx”

and the pdf of the quote/invoice/packing slip attached.
With the email app now in control I can choose to change/add send-to addresses, bcc,cc, subject, body, and attachments.

Why isn’t this a standard feature?'

Basically, they want to be able to edit what is being sent for a transaction, before it is sent. 

this is actually a pretty common request, i've always pointed them to PS. 
Whats requested commonly actually is to change the email address the email is going to

so i proposed this feature: 

When the 'Are you sure?' pop up comes up before you send an email from a transaction, it would be cool if there was a button there to click if you'd like to make a quick edit before sending.
A button that will direct you to the send email screen, where the email that was abut to be sent is autoloaded into the fields. 

From there, you can change the fields and send the email from this screen with the changes
Thanks!!!
Solution:
Zach has graciously created a catalog entry for this request.

This allows you to select contacts to email from transactions
Edit the contacts and add new contacts.
Allows you to edit the body of the email.
Allows you to also attach documents and bcc or cc anyone you need.

Please note, that the billable time to implement this is estimate at 5 hours since there are multiple considerations and tests.
Note from Product:
Phil Cote - Sept 26, 2024
A version of this has been added to the stock flows for sales transactions. We're still in the process of gradually extending the functionality to the rest of the apps' email flows, where applicable. Proposals and Purchase Transactions next on deck.

Title: Project Management / Project Tracking / Project Tracker App
Description:
Project tracking application creation (functional for desktop and mobile)

Project List screen
i.      Need views to show Project Status (Awaiting Kickoff, In progress, On Hold, Complete and Active (any status other than complete)
ii.      Columns – Project Name, Customer, Type, Status, Start date, End Date, Resource, Hours allocated, Hours remaining, % hours remaining
iii.      Button to add new project

iv.      NOTE: the hours remaining and % hours remaining are calculated –> Hours allocated – Hours used from the Time tracking app

Add/Edit screen
i.      Project name
ii.      Customer drop down
iii.      Area for details
iv.      Type drop down (Audit, Block of Hours, Consulting)
v.      Hours allocated
vi.      Resource Assigned drop down (Employees)
vii.      Start and end dates
 viii.      Project Status

The Project Tracking app and the Time tracking app need to be connected. 
i.      Time tracking app screen

Remove the timer option – we always enter the time manually
Remove the Service Item
Add the Project to tie it to the Project App
Total hours used has to be fed to the project tracking app for the remaining hours calculation  
 

Opportunities customization:
Add a field for Gross Profit and add to reporting (we always look at Revenue and GP)
Customize Opps reports to include Totals for the Month
i.      Total Rev and Total GP
ii.      Total Rev and Total GP by account manager
iii.      Total Rev and Total GP by product
iv.      Total Rev and Total GP by customer
Solution:
In theory, we can likely re-use / re-purpose the opportunity app to do many if not all of these functions. This will also bring down the total cost of the project that is estimated.

However, for the full scale project below is a breakdown. 

Project Tracking App: 
-  This is mostly manual entry (I.e. create a table with the necessary fields, design screens for usability) 
- Estimate: 5-10 hours

Time Tracking: 
- Simply removing things from the screen however TT entires do sync with QB and from memory Service Item was required 
- Add dropdown to TT table and use for time retrieval in Project app 
- Estimate 2-3 hours

Opportunity Reports (assuming this the reports screen and not print reports)
- Would need a loop to iterate through every item and and a separate loop for every account manager and yet another for every customer to update individual revenue and GP numbers, can be timely unless run as an app routine and is only accurate as of the day prior
- Depending on what is used for the profit calculations, time can vary in difficulty. Is this simply Sum Invoices - Sum Bills or do they use other costing metrics (some of which may need manual entry or separate calculations I.e. commissions)
- Estimate 3-5 hours (really depending on how they calculate profit)

Title: Gross Profit / Gross Margin - Dynamic on Screen
Description: The customer wants to see the gross margin and profit dynamically on screen as they are creating an estimate or invoice. To make sure they're profitable when creating a transaction.
Solution:
Pretty Simple two new fields can be on the line or transaction basis:

Gross Profit = Amount − Cost
Gross Profit Margin=  ((Amount - Cost) / Amount * 100) 

Pretty easy to calculate these fields are the transaction is created.

Title: Change Sort Order - Line Order on Transactions
Description:
Ability to change sort order of items on Sales Orders

- He is aware this will be Method:CRM only sort order, and these changes will not reflect the sort order of items in QuickBooks. 

- Sometimes, when creating Sales Orders, a customer requests an item at the end of the order, but the item needs to be ordered/displayed higher up in the items list. 

- Currently, they need to remove all items up to that part, and then add the new item and re-enter the items they removed. 

- They would like to add an item to the middle of a sales order, and have this order reflected internally on the Sales Order screen, print out, as well as in the portal. 

Keywords:
Move Lines
adjust line item
rearrange items
Solution:
This is a pretty common request and quite simple to add.

1. Add MethodLineOrder to Sales Order Lines table
2. Add MethodLineOrder as a column on the New/Edit Sales Order screen
3. Adjust the Sales Order Lines editable grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
4. Adjust the Sales Order Line's grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
5. On Create Invoice screen, update the Create button's logic to add Invoice Lines following MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
6. Update their Sales Order PDF to print the Sales Order Line by MethodLineOrder and not LineOrder
7. If the customer is using Sales Orders in the Customer Portal, make sure to update the grid to sort by MethodLineOrder and not Line Order

The complex part is what the custom may expect when changing the MethodLineOrder. If they are not okay with manually changing every line's MethodLineOrder we then need to ask the following which will balloon the Estimated Time. 

1. When adding a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, do they expect the MethodLineOrder to be added in sequence? Or should they manually enter the MethodLineOrder?
2. What do they expect to happen when deleting a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, then adding a new Sales Order Line?

Title: Portal - Customer Facing Document Centre
Description:
Hello PS!

I have a customer who's looking to have document management solution in Method. She's also wants her customer to be able to access this through the portal.
Solution:
Base Requirements ( ~ 8 hours) 
 
*Internal Screens*
-	1. Add a new table to hold documents and link them to customers. 
-	2. Add a new section on Edit Entity or a brand new screen where the documents for each customer will be uploaded. This will be dynamic, which means you can add as many documents as you wish for each Customer. 
 

*Portal Screen* 
-	Next step will be to add a new guest screen  where each customer will access a list of all documents provided to them


===========================================
Additional components (This will increase your project time)
 
-	Have a preferences screen where you have a list of the most common documents that you provide to your customers( this could be a catalogue, an agreement, …) so you can select from this list (or select to add a separate document ) (~3 - 4 hours)
-	Have the ability to check if the documents have been viewed or not.  (~2 hours)

Title: Improve Admin Capabilities - Allow the Admin to edit User Settings - Allow the Admin to Edit Sign-in Emails

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Title: Email Automation - Email Drip Capability / Scheduled Email - Sequenced Emails
Description:
Hey PS!

Can we provide an estimate on how long it'll take for us to customize an email drip feature in Method. 

My customer's workflow is as follows:

Select Email drip timeline - 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 2 weeks, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months (Calendar option)
Select activity type - email, call reminder, appt reminder
Create new / Select template - email templates, call scripts 

Create a flow example
Email template 1 goes out 2 weeks from invoice date.
< complete / add step >
Email template 2 goes out 2 weeks after email 1 unless a new invoice was shipped. 
< complete / add step >
Email template 2 goes out 2 weeks after email 1 unless a new invoice was shipped. 
< complete / add step >
Phone call reminder setup repeated every 2 weeks until new invoice or toggled off.
Solution:
I would:
Create a table that would story the workflow for example:

Email Drip Category, Email Order, Email Frequency
- Invoices, 1,  14 days, Email Content
- Invoices, 2, 14 days, Email Content

I think to get this 100% right would take us 5 to 10 hours of customization.
If we needed to do more than 1 workflow - It would probably take an additional 2-3 hours after we build out the intitial skeleton.

Title: Integration with Shopify - Inventory Integration between Shopify and Method - Sales Orders with Shopify
Description:
Customer is currently using Shopify as their primary inventory management tool.

They do not have a full integration with QBooks inventory due to perceived limitations. 

They are wondering what the Zapier or API avenue could look like if they want to connect Shopify and Method directly with a focus on items.

In essence, when a item is added to a Work Order or invoice in Method, can we update Shopify inventory and verse versa.
Solution:
Shopify does not provide any information about items or item levels via zapier.

Therefore this needs to be a direct API integration.
Exploring and implementing any API is generally a large project.

A quick look at the API does look like this is possible:
https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest/2023-10/resources/inventorylevel

This wouldn't be a realtime sync but most likely a nightly or daily process.

Title: Ability to have multiple templates available on Transactions screens to choose from before sending the email
Description:

Title: Duplicate Entity - Improve Duplicate Detection - Improve Duplicate Entity Conflict
Description:
We have customers that work together in tandem with QuickBooks, in some cases, they are creating duplicate entities without even knowing. 

For a example, I've created a lead called Method Integration; however, my QuickBooks team has after the fact also created another customer or vendor with the same name Method Integration.

Because the information exists in both QuickBooks and the CRM as different records, there is no easy way to reconcile these duplicates. As a user I don't even know these duplicates exist and are problematic. 

So the CRM user is seeing the Customer Lead, who they can't convert into a customer because of a duplicate conflict. 

And the QuickBooks users is wondering why is there a duplicate. 

This is especially problematic when it comes to customization, and you try and update the 'Customer' table. Because the table will have both entries listed essentially blocking the table from any updates until the duplicate is resolved.

Archived entities further complicate these workflows as well.
Solution: We do have a data clean up catalog available to help resolve this problem. It's about 80-90% effective; however, for archived entities we do need to work with support to rename and bypass the duplicate entity issue.

Title: Sync with QB Expenses - Expense App - Expenses App
Description: I would like to use the CRM for both Vendor Management and Customer Management. Expenses are key part of my workflow so I can keep track of what I've purchased from Vendors.

2.) Work through the process payment action to refund a payment directly from the payment screen.

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

c.) Create an activity to record the account was reassigned
d.) Update the customer

Title: Email & Portal  - Tracking Email (Sent, Opened, Clicked) or Portal Last Accessed Date, Transaction Last Opened, Transaction Last Viewed
Description:
Can the estimate email status be added as a column on the estimate list.
Can the last time a customer accesses the portal be added to the estimate list 

Bo and his team often find estimates landing in Spam folders and this could be a way for them to stay on top of follow-ups and who is engaging with their estimates.

Customer looking to have a feature similar to QuickBooks that allows visibility of when customers view invoices with date and time stamps. They want the date and time visible on Invoice list as well as on Invoice screen.
OB screenshot attached

Keywords:
Estimate Opened
Invoice Opened
Estimate Viewed
Invoice Viewed
Transaction Opened
Solution:
Both as possible.

Portal Tracking is Simple - Create a new field on the Estimate, PortalLastAccessedBy and Date. Everytime the portal screen is loaded update these two fields. 

Email Tracking is Simple as well - Everytime an email is sent. Make sure to assign it a 'BatchID'. eg. TableName+RecordID - Estimate12345

Once a BatchID is set, the following webservice can be called:
https://api.method.me/email/notifications/alocetsystem/stats?

With the parameter batchname

Using the batch name above, you should be able to return the status of the email, Whether it was Sent, Opened, or Clicked.

Screenshots are attached of the webservice configuration.

Title: Invoice Paid Date Field  - Invoice Payment Summary - Invoice Template

Title: Sales Tax QBO - Improve the Sales Tax Experience for QBO
Description:
Everytime a customer hit's save on a transaction in QBO

• Sales tax is finalized in QuickBooks after transactions sync from Method to QuickBooks.
• QuickBooks uses Automated sales tax to assign taxes based on customer's location.
• Tax information selected in Method will be updated once transaction is synced with QuickBooks.
• Tax rate may change when going back to an estimate in Method.
• Tax rate may also change the county at times.

This is a problem because customers will receive emails with different numbers and tax values. Everytime someone hits save on the transactions it's reverted.
Solution:
All workarounds, aren't perfect; however, we might be able to customize a solution to help prevent this. 

On save, ask the user if they want to re-fetch the tax values for the transaction from QuickBooks or save without preparing for accounting. 

We could also adjust the save to behave similar to the print button, where we just give the user the option to print without saving before printing.

Title: Method Payments - Instant Signup & Collection
Description:
Summary
Enable businesses to sign up for Method Payments and start collecting payments immediately—without complex setup, third-party integrations, or manual configurations.

Problem Statement
Currently, new customers need to go through multiple steps to enable Method Payments, including external verification, manual configuration, and in some cases, third-party integrations. This process creates friction, delays revenue collection, and can discourage adoption.

Proposed Solution
One-Step Signup & Activation: Allow customers to enable Method Payments directly within Method:CRM with minimal input—e.g., business details, bank info, and tax ID.
Instant Payment Collection: Once enabled, customers can immediately create invoices, accept payments via credit card or ACH, and reconcile transactions within Method:CRM.
No Third-Party Requirements: Eliminate the need for external payment processors or plugins—everything should work natively within Method Payments.
Smart Defaults & Automation: Pre-configured settings for payment terms, auto-reconciliation, and deposit tracking to reduce manual setup.

Compliance & Security Built-In: Ensure compliance with PCI-DSS standards while providing fraud protection and secure payment handling.

User Benefits
✅ Faster Revenue Collection – Businesses can start accepting payments immediately, reducing time-to-cash.
✅ Simplified Onboarding – No complex configurations or integrations needed.
✅ Increased Adoption – Lowering friction will encourage more customers to use Method Payments.
✅ Improved Customer Experience – Reduces frustration by streamlining the payment process.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time; however, is up for consideration with the product team.

Title: Full Advanced Inventory Support - Multiple Warehouse

That said this only applies to inventory that is shared between warehouses.

We could customize a 'home' warehouse for each item.

Title: System Email Improvement - System Email Visbility -  Auto unblock / ability for a user to unblock users who are not receiving system emails
Description:
Today, we have a number of users contact us on a daily basis about system emails not being delivered. 

These are not emails that they're sending to customers. These are emails we (Method) are sending to their users for the following reasons:

Invite user / Inviting a new user
Password Reset / Resetting a Password

1.) Clear visibility to system emails that are departing from my account
2.) The ability to potentially action these emails or at least understand what their status is

So as an admin I can take the appropriate action to help my user.

Send grid, Email server

Title: Portal  - Sub-Customer / Jobs in the Portal / One Email Multiple Accounts
Description:
I want to be able to see sub-customers / jobs in the Method portal.

The main parent should be able to see all of their invoices and the invoices associated with their jobs. 
They should be able to also pay for invoices.

subcustomers
parent customers
Solution:
Depending on the needs of this project this could expand to be a Medium sized project. Better to PAD when possible.

1.) Recommend removing any reference to 'Customer Balance' on the portal since this won't report correctly for all outstanding sub-customers. 
- I believe this exists on Customer Home & Recent Transactions
- I would remove the ability to 'pay multiple invoices' since this could create complexties later that are not needed.

2.) On Recent Trasactions Loop through all Customers where Parent Fullname RecordID = Portal Entity. RecordID

Title: Gross Profit / Gross Margin - Reporting
Description:
I have attached a sample screen shot of the report a client wants to build. Can you provide me a rough estimate please. 

This report has a profit by item 
- Items also may also be linked to discount items which should be factored into the profit calculation.
Solution:
Our comissions app out of the box has similar elements to this.

Ideally we wouldn't create a report but show this information in a grid.

1.) We would be able to pull sales price & purchase cost from QuickBooks. 
2.) Take that information then multiply it by the volume / units sold.
3.) Then calculate the Total Cost --- Total Revenue.
4.) Based off of that information, determine the difference to calculate Total Profit.
5.) Using the Total Profit, we can then calculate % margin.

It should actually be very straight forward.

The only challenge I see in this report, is grouping an 'item' with it's appropriate discount code.

So we might need to create a relationship table to manage for 'Salmon', here are the items we need to consider for purchase and discount.
-- Then go through both these items and complete 1,2,3,4,5 and summarize it.

Title: Ability to Insert Line Items or re-sort Line Items on a Transaction
Description:
User would like the ability to insert a line item on an existing estimate / invoice.

Its important for their line items to flow in a certain order but they often forget to add certain line items during the estimate process.
Solution:
Estimate is Small (3 -4 hours) per transaction.

This is a big ask, believe it or not. LineOrder is a special field that QuickBooks doesn't really give us access too and is a super important field. LineOrder is heavily relied on for other specials features such a Grouped Items.

If they are not using Grouped Items, then this can be a rather simple request.

We would add 2 new buttons on the EstimateLine grid. One for Up, one for Down. When clicked, this will work with the neighbour's data. We would hold all the values for each line in Action Results and update the respected Record with it's neighbours data. That way the actual Record doesn't move, just the values have swapped. 

If they are looking for a solution that works with their Grouped Items, this will greatly increase the estimated time to 10 - 30 hours. The LineOrder in Method will not be the same as what they see in QuickBooks. We would have to update their Guest - View screens, PDFs and more.

Title: Sync Improvement - Sync with QuickBooks Documents - QuickBooks Attachments

Title: Whatsup Integration
Description:
On behalf of  Vikram Mehta.

The customer was intrigued by our new SMS function but was wondering if WhatsApp is a customization option for the actual delivery. 

MMS is generally smoother, has read only feedback and provides the customer the option for responses, 2-way communication and reduced cost. 

Currently Whatsup is not available via Zapier. 

Is this possible via API? 

Any Guidance for Whatsup requests in the future?
Solution:
As of my last knowledge sending messages via the WhatsApp Business API is not as straightforward as sending regular messages through the consumer version of WhatsApp. Here are some key points to consider:

WhatsApp Business Account:
To use the WhatsApp Business API, you need to have a WhatsApp Business Account. This is different from a regular WhatsApp account.

WhatsApp Business API Provider:
Businesses can't connect directly to the WhatsApp API. Instead, they need to work with an official WhatsApp Business API provider. These providers are third-party companies authorized by WhatsApp to offer access to the API.

Approval Process:
Before using the WhatsApp Business API, businesses need to go through an approval process with WhatsApp. This involves submitting an application to be reviewed by WhatsApp.

Compliance:
Businesses using the API need to comply with WhatsApp's policies and guidelines. This includes adhering to messaging rules, opt-in requirements, and other policies to prevent spam.

Message Templates:
Messages sent through the WhatsApp Business API often require the use of message templates. These templates need to be pre-approved by WhatsApp and can only be used for specific types of messages, such as transactional or customer service-related communications.

Costs:
Using the WhatsApp Business API is not free, and costs can vary based on the provider, the volume of messages, and other factors.

Technical Integration:
Integrating the WhatsApp Business API into your systems requires technical expertise. You need to set up webhooks, handle message templates, and manage the integration with your business processes.

Security and Data Privacy:
Businesses need to ensure the security and privacy of user data when using the API. Compliance with data protection regulations is crucial.

Use Cases:
WhatsApp Business API is typically designed for medium to large enterprises, and its use cases often involve customer communication, notifications, and support.

Title: Support Google Analytics Tracking / Capturing of UTM parameters on Web to Lead forms
Description:
Google Analytics Tracking ID

The ability to add a tracking ID to our web to lead forms or any public page for that matter.

UTM Parameters

The types of UTM parameters are synonymous with the five available tags you can use for tracking. 
Below you will find each tag, followed by what they mean and an example:

utm_campaign: This names the specific campaign the link was a part of. For example, you could use opening_day_deals or black_friday_sale. Example: utm_campaign=black_friday_sale

utm_source: Use this to identify where the traffic comes from. Possible sources include search engine, newsletter, or other referrals. Example: utm_source=facebook

utm_medium: This indicates what marketing channel directed a visitor to your website, such as email, social media, PPC ads, guest blog or brand partnerships. Example: utm_medium=social

utm_content: This highlights the call to action that worked. Example: CTAs include buy_now, click_here, learn_more, shop_now, or try_demo.

utm_term: Identifies keywords used in PPC ads, such as “dashboard cam” or “eye drops for allergies,” so you can see what most people are looking for when they find your ads. Links with this tag rarely show up in organic searches. When they do, this may indicate a website indexing problem. Example: utm_term=tennis_shoes or 
utm_term=utm_builder
Solution:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yIWkcMvrpC0

Title: Sales Order Portal - ECommerce, Price Catalog, Custom Order Portal
Description:
1) Our customer who are retail stores. We would like them to enter their orders on a B2B System.

It could be linked to our website or a stand alone B2B where they could enter their orders.

This is the flow:

-They enter their USER NAME and PASSWORD
-Once in, they see a picture of the products, the products code, their net price and the retail price (MSRP)
-Then they can enter the quantities of each units they want in their basket.
-Once done, they confirm their shipping address that will appear in the basket. They enter their PO number, comments if they have, etc. and then they submit their order.
-An email is sent automatically to them stating that the order confirmation will be sent to them within 24 hours.


2)For example, we have about 1,000 ship to locations and we have about 25 groups of customers.

Each group of customers have +-15 products in their price agreement, but have different net prices.

For example, attach is a buying group, there are about 70 ship to’s/customers into this group and they each pay their own invoices.

In Quickbook I have them linked together, but always invoice to individual accounts.
Solution:
First thing to keep in mind is that this will never look as nice as doing this with an actual ecommerce platform. 

1) Having them log in with username and password - I would recommend just using the portal flow for this -- No customization needed
2) Set up a screen with an editable grid that has all the items listed, have a button to view pictures of the items, and be able to change quantities 
3) Have a submit button which takes them to a separate page to enter PO number, ship address details, and displays the confirmed items
4) Insert invoice (based on the part 2 customization) 
5) Send notification email to customer (Involves creating a email template) 
----
Altogether the above is roughly 5- 10 hours I might even go higher because aesthetics might be a huge factor here

Part 2: Custom price groups
1) Set up a custom table to hold price group info
2) Display this info either on the edit entity or view contact page 
3) Modify transaction screens to check if there is a price group in place and then pull the price of that individual item 

Having done this one before it's roughly 2-4 hours of work (depending on which transactions need it)


-----

Technically an easy workflow:
1.) Make a duplicate of the Guest Sales Order Page
2.) Call it New Sales Order
3.) Create an item drodown with a quantity text box where the user can select from the grid (if the item has already been added) or select from the dropdown list of items.
4.) Create a 2 fields on the item table
4a.) Portal Safe Name: This allows the end user to name their items differently than QB
4b.) Is Item Visible on Portal: This allows the end user to control what items are visible on the portal. 
5.) Create a button to submit the order and notify the rep, if the rep doesn't exist notify the admin
6.) Create a filter on the sales order screen for 'Wait for sync approval'.
7.) Create a status field for the sales order options.

Title: Bill of Lading - Chain of Custody - Tracking Shipment Delivery
Description:
A Bill of Lading (BOL) is a legal document issued by a carrier to a shipper that serves as a receipt for the goods being shipped, a contract of carriage, and a title for the goods. It includes details like the type, quantity, and destination of the goods, and it must accompany the goods being transported. The BOL is crucial in the shipping process as it is used to ensure that the carrier delivers the goods to the correct location and to the correct party. It can also be used in disputes over shipping, as it serves as proof that the carrier received the goods as described.

A Packing Slip, on the other hand, is a document that details the contents of a shipment. It lists the items included in the shipment, but it doesn't serve as a legal contract or title for the goods. A packing slip is primarily used by the recipient to check that all items listed have been received and to verify the quantity and description of the products.

Key Differences:
Purpose:

Bill of Lading: Acts as a legal contract, a receipt, and a title for the goods.
Packing Slip: Lists the contents of the shipment for verification purposes.
Legal Status:

Bill of Lading: Legally binding document that outlines the terms of the transportation contract.
Packing Slip: Not legally binding; purely informational.
Use Case:

Bill of Lading: Used by the shipper, carrier, and consignee to manage the shipping process.
Packing Slip: Used by the consignee to ensure all items have been received.
Contents:

Bill of Lading: Includes detailed shipping information like the shipper, carrier, consignee, description of goods, and delivery instructions.
Packing Slip: Includes a list of items, quantities, and sometimes item prices or codes, but not detailed shipping or contract information.
In summary, while both documents are important in the shipping process, the Bill of Lading has a broader legal and logistical role, whereas the Packing Slip is focused on item verification.
Solution:
This can certainly be customized at a high level we need to track some additional information on the load and items:
eg. Weight and Dimensions


We'd also need to track additional information on the carrier and dates.

Title: Group Items - Support on Work Orders
Description:

Solution:
The scope of this customization is pretty large, there are a couple of key problems. 
The first is the customer workflow, if the customer starts from an estimate, the group item flow should just work - towards the work order. Then the next flow which is converting the Work Order into an invoice could result in duplicate line items which need to be cleared. 

1.) Group Item is inserted
2.) Group Item Expands
3.) Then the individual lines that were on the Estimate are added.

So a process is required to clean up and merge the duplicate line information.

If the workflow starts from the work order, we'll need to design a flow which looks at the group item, pulls all the sub items and adds them to the work order at the time of saving the transaction.

Again the first flow will be required when the work order is converted to an invoice to clean up any duplicate items that may be inserted by the Business Rule Engine. 

This logic exists today in our copy button for transactions like estimates and invoices. However, quite a bit of testing is required to make sure this workflow is functional.

Title: Bill of Materials App - Tracking Bill of Materials - Managing Assemblies
Description:
A Bill of Materials (BOM) is a comprehensive list of all the raw materials, components, and assemblies required to manufacture a product. It includes the quantities of each material needed and may also detail other aspects like the labor required, tools, and instructions for manufacturing.

Components of a BOM:
Part Number/Item Code: Unique identifier for each component.
Description: A brief description of the component or material.
Quantity: The amount of each component required for manufacturing.
Unit of Measure: How the quantity is measured (e.g., pieces, kilograms).
Cost: The cost of each component or material.
Supplier Information: Details about where the material is sourced.
Assembly Instructions: Steps or methods required to assemble the product.
Lead Times: Time required to procure or manufacture each item.
Accounting Workflow: Item Receipt + Converting to Bills and Customer Invoices
Purchase Order (PO) Creation:

You begin by creating a Purchase Order for the materials listed in the BOM. This document is sent to your supplier, detailing the items, quantities, and agreed prices.
Receiving the Items (Item Receipt):

When the materials arrive, you create an Item Receipt in your accounting or inventory management system. This document acknowledges that you have received the goods but haven’t yet paid for them.
The inventory is updated to reflect the newly received items, increasing the stock levels.
Converting Item Receipt to Bill:

After confirming the receipt of items, the Item Receipt is converted into a Bill.
The Bill reflects your obligation to pay the supplier. It records the amounts owed and the due date for payment.
This step often includes matching the Bill to the original Purchase Order to ensure consistency in quantities and prices.
Paying the Bill:

The Bill is paid according to the payment terms agreed upon with the supplier. This transaction reduces your accounts payable and the cash or bank balance.
Manufacturing the Product:

Once all necessary materials are in stock (as per the BOM), the production process begins. During manufacturing, the materials are used up, and their costs are transferred from inventory to work-in-progress (WIP) or directly to the cost of goods sold (COGS), depending on your accounting system.
Creating Customer Invoices:

After the product is manufactured and ready for delivery, you generate a Customer Invoice.
This invoice includes the final product details, quantities, and prices, reflecting what the customer owes you.
The invoice is sent to the customer, and the amount is recorded as accounts receivable.
Solution:
Customizing a bill of materials, is a multi-app customization depending on the level of integration with QuickBooks and QuickBooks workflows. 

For example, first and foremost, we would need to understand the structure of the item and it's unit of measure. How is this captured in QuickBooks.

What are are assembly instructions. 
If there are pieces missing that are required; how do POs get created and connected to the customer order.
This customization is pretty involved and should involved a dedicated consultant to review.

Title: Pledges App - Workflow from Pledge to Donation
Description:
Hi guys,

How long will it take to convert a pledge (Opportunity) to a Donation (Sales receipt)?

Key fields we'd need 
- Line Items on the Opportunity screen
- Entity
- Billing Address
Solution:
There are a few ways to tackle this. Generally when taking a pledge there is a period of time that should be considered:

1.) Create a pledge start date & pledge end date
2.) Create a donation frequency (Monthly, One-time, Annually)
3.) Create a custom table to keep track of all the line items we want on the donation - these items will be the template for each individual donation.
4.) Opportunity Amount = Total Pledge Amount.
5.) When the opportunity is saved, we would determine the number of sales receipts to create & create 12 sales receipts  (depending on the period) that are wait for sync approval. 
6.) On the donation list screen, I would create a view that would filter all donations that are due on or before today.
7.) As part of the process someone would go in and update the donation and send it to quickbooks.
8.) When the last donation that's part of a pledge is sent to quickbooks, we would then mark the opportunity and closed and suggest creating a new pledge for the next period. (Nice to have)

Title: Advanced Price Rules
Description:
Method does not currently support the advanced priced rules feature of QBDT.

Advanced Price Rules is a premium add-on that allows the user to create price rules with conditions based on customer, item, sales rep, and class. 
You can also create override rules to exclude items from a price rule.
Automatically change the prices of items on sales based on the price rules you create.
Create Qty Discounts on specific items.

Yes,

This could be a large undertaking or a simple one.
It really depends on the number of rules and how the customer wants to build or manage them.

But in reality, we're just building a lookup table,
Look up item a for this customer type b and get be the price based on quantity c.

Title: Limit the Number of Users who are able to edit a record at a single time
Description:
Currently this customer has about 8-9 different people who can edit a work order at any given time. There are a number of fields that go into each work order, so the technician or dispatcher could be up to 10 minutes on the work order order page.

The issue is when multiple people open the page, they're overwriting each others changes.  Is this a solution we could customize for?
Solution:
This is possible with a relatively simple action set based on two fields LastOpenedBy and LastOpenedDate
On focus if the last opened by and date are empty, populate it with the info
On focus if it's not empty, and the record has been locked for over 15 minutes (preference in the table) then release and update it to the current user
On focus if it's not empty, and the record has been locked for under =<15 minutes then warn the user its locked and by whom.

The record can be released by the 15 minute timer.
The record is also released if a new record is opened (a user can only have 1 record locked at a time)
The record is also released if they click (Back, Save & New, Create Invoice etc. anything that I've identified closes the screen or moves them away from the screen)

Title: Reporting - Provide a Year over Year report of every customer and how much they have purchased

2.) Calculate the total revenue from this year from that customer
3.) Provide a column that shows % growth

Keywords:
Sales by Customer
Sales Report
Sales by Customer Report
Customer Report

This is quite simple I would recommend:

2.) Add fields to keep track of customer, total revenue, difference, and % growth
3.) We could then export this as an excel sheet were the customer could do further manipulation to it. 

If the customer wishes to filter this information, we could certainly expand the workflow to use a grid instead of an excel sheet / pdf to report.

Title: Opportunity Stages - Multiple Sales Flows - Different Sales Opportunities
Description:
Customer has 2 types of sales they would like to manage via opportunities. 
The 2 type of Opportunity do not share common milestones or stages. 

Can we customize 2 separate pipelines based on the type of opportunity?
Ideally, the opportunity-by-stage report would be enhanced to include both pipelines.

eg. Product Sales - Opportunity Pipeline
       Product Installation - Opportunity Pipeline

While similar and can be related, totally different cycles.
Solution:
This is certainly customizable the recommended approach would be:

1.) Create a new Opportunity Type Dropdown this opportunity type would then be related to stages and filter which stages are shown.

2.) Create a new field to link Opportunity Stage with Opportunity Type. When you select the opportunity type, the stage list would then be filtered for all the stages related to that opportunity. 

Designing this flow allows for endless options of Opportunities. Lastly, for opportunity types that are shared, I would just leave this relationship blank. So that anything that is blank or equal to the selected opportunity type could be displayed. 

Reporting would work the same, existing reports would show everything by stage.
If we wish to create a subset of reporting by type, this would have to be a further enhancement to the opportunity reporting. The customer should plan for additional time if required. The quote above does not take reporting changes into consideration.

Title: Web to Lead (W2L) - Customization to Setup Multiple Forms with Different Fields to be Captured
Description:
The customer currently runs two websites and would like show two different sets of fields for each webform. 
Today this is very difficult to customize for the customer to do on their own.
Solution:
Customizing a new webform isn't technically difficult; however for those that are unfamiliar with customization this is a process:

1.) Create a copy of the screen (Customer Form)
2.) When based off of that customize a new screen as you wish, using the default as a starting point
3.) Make sure that 2nd page is available for sharing in a public page format
4.) General the Public page URL for that page.

Title: Import invoices in Method from Excel
Description: The customer is inquiring about the cost and expected timeframe for importing invoices into their Method account for one time, and they also want to know how to do it independently.
Solution:
Importing Invoices and any transactional data is labourious, there are multiple steps required:

1.) All required information must already exist
1a.) Customers, Leads, Terms, Items, all dropdown data must already exist in the CRM

If not all those entries need to be created

2.) Then you need to import a header (Invoice)
3.) Then you need to import multiple lines per header (Invoice Line)

Again, all required fields including Quatity, Rate, Tax must be included as part of the import. 

If not, we're going to need to design business rules around this. 
This is a large project.

How we'd customize it?
1.) Build a table to match the spreadsheet we want to import
2.) Build a button that processes all the above and makes the appropriate decisions when a certain value isn't found. 

This isn't recommended unless the customer is going to be importing on a regular basis from a source like shopify or their own website for example


Alternatives:

1.) Import using QuickBooks
2.) Find another service that support Importing (Transaction Importer)

Title: Improve the HTML Editor to provide WYSIWYG Background Color for an email - Ability to fill the entire background color in the email templates
Description:
The client is attempting to design a festive Christmas email campaign, but is unable to add color to the email body.

The background color feature only adds color behind the text. It would be beneficial to have the ability to apply a single color to the entire email body.
Solution:
This is possible using HTML. 
The HTML Below, shows a 'Grey' Background. 

<!DOCTYPE html>


   
   
  Email with Background Color 
 


  
    
      
        Hello there! 
        This is a sample email with a background color. 
        Feel free to customize the content and styles as needed. 
       
     
   

 
 

In this example, the body element has a background color of #f0f0f0, and the content is contained within a table with a white background. The inline styles are used to ensure better compatibility across various email clients.

Please note that email client support for HTML and CSS can be limited, and some clients may ignore or override certain styles. Always test your emails in different clients to ensure they are displayed as expected.

Title: Estimate App Improvement - Proposal App Improvement - Estimate to Proposal - Convert Estimate to Proposal

1.) Create an 'Estimate' type field - One time or Recurring
2.) If they choose recurring
3.) On the line items of the estimate provide the 'recurrence' options - When accepted, Recurring, or When completed
4.) At this point, we could convert the estimate to a proposal and send that off for signature.
Note from Product:
A Couple curiosities:
Do these accounts want to do both? Estimates and proposals?

Do they require all the extra septs of the proposal or are they just looking for recurring auto-invoices?

Does their flow involve Opportunities and would an immediate connection to proposals have them skip the estimate step?
- Courtney Nov 18, 2024

Title: Opportunity Email Automation - Opportunity Stage Change
Description:
User is looking to manage their sales and production workflows from the opportunities app. 

Although the actual production management process will be completed in their proprietary software, automated communication is not possible. 

Can we customize automatic templated emails based on Opportunity Stage change?
Solution:
Pretty simple in theory:

1.) Create a template (could be a different template per stage)
2.) On the opportunity stage link a stage to a template
3.) Everytime the stage changes and is saved - check to see if the notification went out. 
4.) If no then send the email notification of the change.
5.) If yes then don't do anything.

Alternatively, before the save, compare the value on screen with the value on table.
If the value is different, then get the email template and send based on the value on screen.

Title: QBDT Multiple Shipping Addresses - Customizing multiple ship addresses
Description: The end goal is to be able to choose different locations we are performing work for the same customer. Same customer, same billing, same contact person, possible different locations.
Solution:
Help centre article:
https://help.method.me/en/articles/2732968-customizing-multiple-shipping-addresses

The solution is quite simple: 
1.) We would need to create a new table  or use the ShipAddress table in QBDT
2.) We would need to create an address type field, Primary Billing, Primary Shipping, and Other 
3.) Primary Billing should reflect the Billing Address for the Customer in QuickBooks 
4.) Primary Shipping should reflect the Shipping Address for the Customer in QuickBooks 
5.) All other addresses would be listed as Other 
6.) On the transaction screens, we would still pre-fill with the defaults from QuickBooks but provide a dropdown for address where the custom addresses would be selected.

Title: Bulk Update, Delete, Manage Tags - Update Tags
Description:
Right now Tags is not available as a Table or an Object. To be able to bulk update Tag, this requires an individual to use the Import Tool, work with VLookups in Excel or some hefty customizations.

It would be great if we can interact with Tags, similar to how we can in a Grid's Advanced Search. That way it would save lots of time as it wouldn't be as complex to manage Tags in bulk.
Solution:
This could be customized:
0.) Create the tag
1.) Create a radio button on the list screen
2.) Add to options Append or Delete
3.) Add a textbox, where the user can type the tag
4.) Add a checkbox to the grid
5.) Loop through checked records
6.) Assign loop.Tags to an action result
7.) Character function the text box by including a comma before and after the tag
8.) Then if the option in the radio button is append join the new tag to the list
9.) If the option in the radio button is delete, do a character function replace for the tag and remove the tag

This should work 9/10

Title: Proposal App Improvement - Calculate tax on proposal
Description: Customer wants to  calculate tax on the subtotal of the service items before accepting the proposal and show it under Invoice Dates --> When Accepted grid (see screenshot attached).
Solution:
Taxes in Method are generally only finalized when the transaction hits QuickBooks.

In the case of proposals this is a little complex because, the invoices aren't created until the customer accepts or a recurring date occurs.  At that moment the transaction is sent to QuickBooks for tax calculations and returns the appropriate number.

For the proposal to consider tax there are a few options:
1.) Set all items on the proposal as non-taxable
2.) Create / Use a 'Service Tax' item on the proposal that represents a % of the above.

You also have the option of updating the acceptance page to include wording like, all items listed on this proposal are taxable and will be subject to 'x'% tax or 'local' state tax at the time of processing.

Title: Tax Improvement - The ability to choose between calculating tax before or after discounts
Description:
Because discounts are generally offered directly by the retailer and reduce the amount of the sales price and the cash received by the retailer, the sales tax applies to the price after the discount is applied. For example, if your normal selling price is $30 but you are offering a 5-percent discount for first time customers, the tax base is $28.50. The same holds true if you are offering a dollar discount rather than a percentage discount. If the normal price is $30.00, but you are offering a $5.00 discount for returning customers, your tax base (prior to any taxable shipping) is $25.00.

If, however, the discount is sponsored by the manufacturer or the distributor and you will be reimbursed by either of these parties for offering the discount, the sales tax base is the full sales price and not the reduced sales price. As noted below with manufacturer’s coupons, because the tax base is the amount of receipts you receive for selling the product, the states generally don’t distinguish whether the payment comes from the customer or by some third party.   As noted above in the Georgia definition, coupons that are reimbursed by a third-party are NOT treated as a sales price adjustment. This rule is pretty uniform across the country.
Solution:
Cannot be customized at this time, QuickBooks handles all tax.
Their may be a way to pass this information to QuickBooks when a transaction is created in Method so taxes can be accurately reflected.

Title: Change Order Management - Estimate / Work Order change tracking
Description: User would like to create an interface in our customer portal where their clients can make adjustments to finalized estimates or work orders in progress, notifying the team internally of the change order request.
Solution:
Change Orders, are complicated:

1.) If an estimate hasn't been accepted by the customer, you can simply just edit the estimate and the information will be updated on the Portal in realtime. 

2.) If the estimate has been approved; my recommendation would be to create a new estimate by 'Copying' the old estimate, and modifying the appropriate content. While this isn't a traditional change order, it does enable you and the customer to have a clear view of all costs associated with the job not just the changes.

3.) Last but not least, the status of the original estimate would move to 'rejected' with the new estimate taking precedent. 

4.) Since only a single signature can be captured per document this is the past of least resistance. 

5.) Alternatively, a secondary table / screen can be created to build on top of the original estimate - that can be used to sign off on new changes or keep track of changes throughout the life cycle of a project. 

6.) Changes could also be recorded to work orders as needed; where the final invoice would include the original estimate information and any additional work orders, that are used to track 'Additional Services' or 'Additional Scope' to the original project.

Title: RMA process - RMA App - (Return Merchandise Authorization) is a system that businesses use to manage the return of goods from customers.
Description:
Key Steps in the RMA Process:
Customer Request: A customer contacts the business requesting a return.
Issuance of RMA: The business reviews the request and issues an RMA number if the return is approved. This number tracks the return process.
Return of Goods: The customer ships the product back to the business, usually including the RMA number with the shipment.
Inspection: The business receives the product, inspects it to confirm the issue, and determines the appropriate action (replacement, refund, or repair).
Resolution: The business processes the return, issues a refund, replaces the product, or repairs it as agreed.
How it Fits with QuickBooks:
QuickBooks, as an accounting and inventory management software, can support the RMA process in the following ways:

Tracking Returns: QuickBooks allows you to create sales receipts, invoices, and credit memos. For RMA processes, you can use credit memos to record returns. The credit memo reflects the value of the returned goods and helps update your inventory and accounts receivable.

Inventory Management: When goods are returned, QuickBooks can update inventory levels based on the credit memo or refund. It keeps track of which products have been returned and adjusts stock accordingly.

Customer Refunds: QuickBooks can issue refunds or apply credit to the customer's account. Once the return is approved, you can create a credit memo, and QuickBooks will track the refund or adjustment.

Reporting: QuickBooks can generate reports on returns and credits issued, helping businesses track and analyze return patterns, customer behavior, and product issues.

Integration with Other Systems: If you use third-party RMA management software, QuickBooks can integrate with many such systems, ensuring that return transactions flow smoothly into your accounting records without manual input.

Incorporating an RMA process in QuickBooks makes it easier to manage the financial aspects of returns and maintain accurate records of your business's inventory and customer interactions.
Solution:
This is something that can be customized; while all aspects of the flow are complex and the scope may increase. 

One could expect a simple basic RMA app within the budget above. 
Keeping in mind anything transactional like Credit Memos and Refunds would need to also be managed and customized.

Title: API Integration - Klaviyo Integration with Method
Description:
Summary
Enable seamless integration between Klaviyo, a powerful email marketing and automation platform, and Method CRM, to enhance customer engagement, centralize data, and improve marketing campaign performance. This integration will automate workflows between the two systems and provide a unified view of customer interactions.

Purpose and Benefits
Centralized Customer Data:

Sync customer and lead data between Klaviyo and Method CRM to keep information up-to-date across both platforms.
Automatically update email preferences, purchase behavior, and engagement metrics in Method CRM for personalized follow-ups.
Enhanced Marketing Automation:

Leverage Method CRM's customer segmentation in Klaviyo for targeted email campaigns.
Trigger automated workflows in Klaviyo based on CRM events (e.g., new lead, deal closed, task completed).
Improved Reporting and Insights:

Merge Klaviyo’s email analytics (open rates, click-through rates, etc.) with Method CRM data to measure campaign ROI and its impact on sales.
Streamlined User Experience:

Eliminate manual data entry between systems, reducing errors and saving time.

Use Cases
Lead Nurturing:

A new lead is added to Method CRM. Automatically enroll them in a Klaviyo email sequence.
Customer Retention:

Identify inactive customers in Method CRM and send re-engagement emails via Klaviyo.
Purchase Follow-Up:

After a deal is marked as 'Closed' in Method CRM, send a thank-you email and product recommendations from Klaviyo.
Upselling Opportunities:

Use Klaviyo’s predictive analytics combined with Method CRM sales data to target customers with relevant offers.
Solution: Certainly possible via API integration, that said the scope of work is quite large and it's advised to review with a Customization Specialist or API Consultant.

Title: Invoice Improvement - Monthly Open Balance Summary

Customer is looking to haveDescription:

Proposed Solution:

Add a feature to generate a Monthly Open Balance Summary report directly from the invoice or reporting section.
The summary should include key details such as customer name, invoice numbers, due dates, and total outstanding balances, broken down by month.
Impact:

Simplifies the accounts receivable process.
Saves time for users by automating balance tracking.
Reduces errors and improves financial visibility.
Solution:
This feature can be customized to send an automated email on the last day of each month, providing a summary that includes:

Invoices Paid Last Month – A detailed list of all invoices settled during the previous month.
Currently Open Invoices – A snapshot of invoices that remain unpaid.
Overdue Invoices – A breakdown of overdue invoices, including due dates for better prioritization.

Title: Business Card Scanner - Integration with Free Business Card Scanning Applications
Description:

Use Case
Small business owners or sales professionals often collect large volumes of business cards at networking events or trade shows. A streamlined, cost-effective way to scan and import these contacts directly into Method:CRM would save time and reduce manual data entry errors.
By providing native support for popular third-party solutions, Method:CRM can improve user experience and cater to customer needs for automation and efficiency.
Solution:
Proposed Solution
Integrate with Free Scanning Apps:
Evaluate the feasibility of integrating Method:CRM with free business card scanning apps (e.g., Microsoft Lens, CamCard, or Google Lens) through direct APIs or intermediate platforms like Zapier.

Enhance Open API Documentation:
Update API documentation to include best practices and pre-built workflows for connecting to business card scanning solutions.

User-Friendly Workflow:
Develop a feature that allows users to upload a bulk-scanned contact file (e.g., CSV or vCard) directly into Method:CRM, mapping fields intuitively for seamless contact creation.

Zapier Workflow Templates:
Provide Zapier templates specifically for integrating popular business card scanning apps with Method, reducing setup time for customers.

Title: Sales Round Robin - Auto-Assign Leads based on geography
