Solution:
Yes, this is certainly possible. 

The actual actions behind this are quite simple, we would need to build a simple preferences screen where we would keep a list of locations and the appropriate rep. 

Depending on the complexity of the business rules, the preferences would be the most difficult part of this implementation.

Title: Bill Email - Updating on Invoices / Transactions
Description:
When a work order is created, they want to capture the billing email.
Usually this is from the contact; however, there are scenarios where it changes.

They want this information to flow to the invoice since they email their invoices from QuickBooks.
Solution:
- Retrieve bill email on Customer selection in Work Order
- Populate bill email on Invoice during creation process

Title: Quantity on-hand Warning - when inventory is low
Description: Can we customize a warning or popup when estimating or invoicing an item with limited on hand quantity?
Solution:
We can do this, but the User should still be cautious. This is a field that is governed by QB. If there are Sync conflicts, or a Sync hasn't ran for a while, the On Hand Quantity could be off.

Here are the steps we would need to take to be able to have the warning apply;

1. Add a field to Items to hold their Warning Quantity
2. Item change will looks to see if the Item is in it's Warning Quantity by comparing it to the Quantity On Hand
3. Qty change should also do this as well
4. If they are using mobile, we would need to apply #2 and #3 to the Add/Edit Item screen
5. Repeat #2 - #4 for Invoices

Title: W2L - Web to Lead - Automatically Tag Leads
Description:
This customer would like to analyze the possibility to have a tag automatically created in the contact when a lead comes from a web to lead form. 
He knows that he can see that information in the 'lead source', but he'd want to have a tag as well.
Solution:
Possible:

1.) Create the tag
2.) Update the Web to Lead for Submission on Customer Create
3.) On Contact, Update the contact field 'TagsList' to the new tag 

eg. ,Google,

Title: Ability to manage and sort Attachment
Description:
Customer is in custom shelters and have many revisions and attachments on each opportunity.

They are looking for a way to 1) Sort by date of attachment 2) catch duplicates of the same file name.

Can this be accomplished via customization?
Solution:
A custom document management system would need to be designed in the opportunity to store revisions of documents and attachments.

Every 'new' revision would have a new set of attachments that can be stored or referenced. 

Duplicate detection and date sorting could be available through this functionality however it is not foolproof.

Title: Track Miles driven by the techs when they punch in until they punch out for the day
Description:
The customer is looking to track the miles covered by their techs when they punch in for the day until they clock out. 

They want to record the miles covered by them through out the day.

Is it possibel with customization to to track it on the Field Crew App and get the track on Time Tracking?
Solution:
Yes, This is possible.

The easiest way is to add 2 custom fields

1.) Odometer Reading @ Start Time
2.) Odometer Reading @ End time

2-1 = Total Mileage.

Title: Adding Unit of Measure to Transactions - UoFM
Description: This customer would like to know if the information of UofM from QuickBooks can be synced and populated on Sales Orders or Purchase Orders
Solution:
Adding Unit of Measure to transactions is quite simple.
The process would typically require you to go into a transaction line eg. EstimateLine, and link in UnitOfMeasure from the item table.

Then place this on the grid & update when a new item is selected.

Keep in mind this unit of measure is 'read-only'.
Ideally by this point the customer will review out help-centre content on unit of measure and understand that it's best to manage Unit Of Measure by creating a separate item for each combination. 

Eg. Rather than just having a single item called Coke -- then 5 unit of measures.
It's better to have 5 items
Coke - Single Can
Coke - 6 Pack
Coke - 10 Pack
Coke - 12 Pack
Coke - 24 Pack

Title: Work Orders - Total Time - Total Duration - Summary of time on the Work Order
Description:
Total of Duration for related Time Entries: We require the total duration for all related time entries on the edit Work Order screen. I have successfully created a grid that shows the related time entries for each work order, but I am having trouble creating a calculation that totals the duration of all related records.

Keywords:
multiple time entries
Solution:
Very simple customization.

1.) With an editable grid you can add the 'total' option to the bottom of the editable grid to sum to total duration.

2.) This could also be done and updated using the retrieve value from table action where the time tracking entry is linked to the work order.

Title: Email reminders that can be automatically sent to the contact before the activity is due
Description: Right now, it's possible to send reminders to the assigned user in the Activities app, but this customer would like to have the same option for the contacts. This way, he can send automatic reminders to his contacts before the activity is due.
Solution:
This can certainly be customized; however, there are limitations. 

In general the 'morning' of the activity or at any point of the day, we can send a single notification to all contacts with an activity due on that day.

Where there are limitations is controlling the ability to say 15 or 30 minutes before the activity is due. 

So as long as the customer is ok with 24 hour notifications and same day notifications this can be customizable. 

If they need something more time sensitive like 30 minutes before the due date, this is much more complicated, and we're unlikely to achieve that level of automation at this time.

Title: Xero Improvement - Add Support for Vendor Transactions - Purchase Transactions
Description:
Please include the sync with Vendor transactions, particularly “Purchase orders”. Requested by account: “mountainshade2” who stated that not having this integration as a deal breaker since his staff would be required to continue using Odoo - their current PO tool.

Vendors and Customers, are handled differently in Xero. Since the entity is transferable, it's really just another transaction related to a Customer / Vendor.
Solution: Cannot be Customized at this time.

Title: Restore Items Records - The ability to restore items when deleted or archived - Unarchive Item
Description:
The customer contacted us because their record for an Item was deleted and could not be found in QuickBooks. The item was  deleted and cannot be found in QuickBooks. Customer attempted to recreate the record in Method but it says it already exists in QuickBooks. 

Keywords: Unarchive Customer Unarchive Lead Show Less
Solution: Support Tool Available	Cannot be customized, however, this is a very common request. We should +1 this ticket to better understand how often people are looking for deleted or the ability to restore records that are missing from the CRM

Title: Global Search - Improving what fields are used to search - Adding fields to Global Search - Fullname - Name in QuickBooks
Description:
Most of my contacts do not have a first name and I often use 'Name in QuickBooks' to store the company name.

When i search for a company, it's not coming up with the company name on the Global search. I can see the contact but not the company name, to be honest seeing 'Brad' isn't useful.

Could we add Fullname / Name in QuickBooks to the global search card?
Solution:
There is a workaround today, which is add the 'Name in QuickBooks' to the 'Company Name' field, however, this is not ideal. 

We do need to improve the global search display.

Title: Improve Work Order App - Recurring - Recurring Work Orders - Ability to add items to all sub-workorders automatically
Description:
Customer is looking to have a feature where they can add items to all sub-workorders instead of having to click the button ' Copy Job Items From Recurrence Schedule' .


Keywords:
Recurring
Recurring Work Order
Recurring WorkOrder
Recurrence
Sub-items
Children
Child Work Orders
Repeat Work
Copy from Series
Series Items
Solution:
This can be customized, in theory I would approach it as:

1.) On Screen Load if the work order does not have any items
2.) Call the copy from Series button

Behind the scenes the work order wouldn't have items, but once they load the work order, it would be auto added without any interaction.

Title: Influencer CRM - Social Media CRM - Influencer Management Tool
Description: Looking for an influencer management tool to integrate with WeChat, TikTok, and pull/push data both ways
Solution:
Two way sync of this magnitude is pretty large scale customization.

I would generally say not customizable.

Title: Communications Preferences  or Email Preferences - Choose whether or not to be BCC yourself on emails
Description:
Currently today, the signed in user is BCC'd on all outgoing emails.
Including but not limited to Estimate Email Notifications, invoice Email notifications.

Can this be a preference we turn on an off for users?
Solution:
Yes, this can be customized:

1.) The system will always add the user sending the email as a BCC
2.) The simple solution is to disable the logic that does this on each email screen as needed.

Long-term a system preference would go a long way.

Title: Ability to have a sandbox for testing
Description: The customer would like the ability to have a sandbox for testing. They are integrating with a third party Shopify to enter orders, adding customers (if new), and enter payments. And wanted to option to test it out.
Solution:
Sandbox is available. 

I believe we can create a restore of their active account and provide access to that account in a billable means. 

The sync would need to be disconnected from this account and never reconnected.

Alternatively, for QBDT customers, a backup of their QuickBooks file could be taken and connected to a new trial. The customer would then be required to pay for that trial for the life time of their needs at the minimum cost of a pro license.

Title: The ability to resize Company Logo or Portal Logo
Description:
Customer is wondering how much customizability we have on the customer portal, specifically, our Logo. 

They have a long rectangular logo and our stock interface does not mesh well.
Solution:
Cannot be customized. 

In theory the customer just needs to provide a logo that works for the provided dimensions today.

Title: Sales Order to Work Order
Description:
The ability to convert a sales order to a work order.

Generally, we'll create a sales order to keep track of what's required to complete  the work.

Once the materials are available we can then schedule and complete the work order.
Solution:
Certainly Possible

Just like other conversion buttons we would mimic looping through sales order and creating a work order.

Title: Provide Customer PO on the Invoices when converted from Work Orders
Description:
The client is seeking the capability to showcase the Customer Purchase Order numbers provided on the Work Orders, to be exhibited upon converting the Work Orders into an Invoice.

So they have them linked.
Solution:
Simple customization.

Bring Over PO from Estimate to Work Order, then bring that to Invoice. 
Remember since multiple work orders, could become one invoice.

We might need to combine / list PO numbers.

Title: Reorder / Re-order Improvement - LImit access of reordering to only some customers
Description:
Currently we sell to retail and wholesale customers.
We want all of them using the portal; however, we only want re-order to be available for our wholesale customers.
Solution:
This can be customized.

We can use the CustomerType field or a custom field. 
Based on this hide the re-order capabilities on the portal based on the contact that is viewing the portal.

Title: Reset and account to Stock - The ability to remove all data from an account preserving customization
Description:
The customer is currently working through adding a new QuickBooks file that has been created for the company.

In addition to that, they would like to do a complete wipe of all QuickBooks and CRM data while preserving their Customization. 

Today, we effectively do not have a way to do this easily. The best solution has been to create a new account, sync the new file, and copy screens over to the new account whilst wiring up all the changes and updating all the dropdown options.
Solution:
Depending on the size of the account, this is quite a bit of work. 
Naturally the heaviest lift is moving over all customizations and testing them to make sure they're operational on the new account.

Title: Vehicle App - Car App - App to Track Cars - App to Track Vehicles
Description: Customer is looking to design an app where the they can manage cars and keep track of vehicles of their customers.
Solution:
Quite an simple app, we also offer a mission that goes through the creation of a basic vehicle app. In summary:

Create A list screen for the New app and New/ Edit contact Vehicle screen to enter the details

Customized the ability to Add Edit the Model DropDown (Custom row click) which navigates to a new / Edit Make screen

Title: Integration with Apollo
Description:
The customer would like to know if it is possible for Method to integrate with Apollo.

It  helps you find emails for marketing. 
You can search by specific data attributes such as a person's name, a company name, job title, employees by department, revenue and funding, persona, location. You can also send emails using the application as well.

Link for more info: https://www.apollo.io/magazine/how-to-find-an-email-address
Solution:
API Integration Required:

This is possible; however, an API integration is required.

As with all API Integrations, it's based off of scope. Looking at the documentation, we could send apollo and email and return information about that contact. Then update it in the CRM.

Title: Gmail Widget - Automatically log an activity when an email is sent
Description:
My Customer is frustrated with the fact that the Gmail Sidebar does not automatically log an email to the Contact when the sidebar is open. She feels that the side bar is out of date and is garbage. 

My Customer would love if sending an email would automatically log it as an activity in Method.
Solution: This is a great request, cannot be customized. Thanks for submitting.

Title: Shuttle Improvement - Payment Improvement - Support for Wallets - Ability to have  Apple Pay, Venmo , and PayPal in portal as payment options
Description:
The customer is looking to see if it is possible to add  Apple Pay , Venmo , and PayPal, Google Pay as options to pay in the portal.
Use case: Apple Pay is easiest, with auto-filled content and only requiring a double tap and a face look, so that should be first. They want card and ACH to be last their customers use this the least.
Solution: At this time it does not appear these options are supported.

Title: Report Permission - The ability to hide reporting - Hide Reports from Users
Description:
User would like a way to restrict transaction reports so that snapshots into the company revenue is not available. 

They would however want to maintain the team dynamic of a small business so Restricting share tables is not a great option. 

In addition to hiding the report button on the invoice/estimate list, can we hide access to the reports via the ellipses?
Solution:
This can be customized, my approach would be:

1.) Don't hide the options, let the users know the option is there
2.) On the screen where the reports are shown, add logic to the load event to check who should have access to reporting and bounce the user back if they don't have permissions.

Other applications break out reporting as a separate permissions like Access, Edit, and Delete.

Title: Google Integration Improvement - Contacts Sync - Contacts Integration
Description:
User need Googlecontacts to sync bidirectionally with Method to ensure ones he has there is 1. at least in Method, 2. even better, if it can sync contacts both ways.

Google Contact Sync
Gmail Contact Integration
Solution: Potentially a one time import + zapier could solve this; however, updates would not sync between both systems.

Title: Work Order App Improvement - The ability to schedule a multi-day work order on non-consecutive days.
Description:
The customer contacted us for help with scheduling a multi-day work order on specific non-consecutive days. 

The current solution is to create multiple work orders.
Alternatively, they could create a 'series' that has some manual recurrences added. 

However, there is no simple solution without some duplication and manual work.
Solution:
Customization for this particular flow is possible:

1.) When a certain work order type is created, we can basically create and insert a work orders automatically. 

2.) For example, if we're doing a service of pesticides on a lawn, we know that we need to reapply every 3 business days.  We can design a customization to support this to automatically insert those work orders. 

3.) Alternatively is services happen at different intervals we can also customize that were 3 days then 10 days later then 31 days. We can certainly design rules to support that flow.

Title: Purchase Order Approvals to Admin - Prevent Transactions from going to QuickBooks	The customer would like to review all transactions prior to sending them to QuickBooks.
Description: As the admin he wants to control the flow of transactions.	Depending on the level of notifications the user wants or requires this could be done.
Solution: At a simple level, we can mark all transactions as wait for sync approval; from there create a view that the admin can use to then update that field and send the information to QuickBooks.

Title: QBO - Sync Journal Entries
Description:
Ability to sync with Journal Entries.

Some QuickBooks Online Reports incorporate Journal Entries which we do not sync, meaning we cannot create/edit/view Journal Entries in Method.

When our Method clients adopt the platform, we try to get their entire organization using our software, or at least everyone that uses a computer. This includes the Back Office / Accounting teams.

Due to this sync limitation, we cannot create customized solutions. As a side note, Method:Classic has stock screens for Journal Entries and Credit Memo apps for QBDT.
Solution: Cannot be customized, FR added.

Title: Transaction Improvement  - Ability to hide group items in the Portal
Description:
Some users do not want their customers to see all the children items that belong to a group item. This is mainly because if the customer is able to see the breakdown of items that make up that group item - there is a possibility that the customer sources these parts themselves for a cheaper price. 

Method does provide functionality to 'Print Items as Group'  on the item level. This dictates if transaction PDFs show children items within the group item or not. However, there is nothing to account for this on the customer portal. 

For example, the Guest - View Invoice screen will still show the full breakdown of all items regardless. It would help if there was an additional checkbox on the item level that dictated if group items are fully shown or not on the Portal.
Solution:
This can certainly be customized either transaction by transaction. 

Alternatively there could be a global setting.

Title: Integration of Method:CRM with Sage 50
Description:
ummary:
Request for the development of a direct integration between Method:CRM and Sage 50 accounting software to expand the CRM's compatibility beyond QuickBooks, allowing businesses using Sage 50 to leverage Method:CRM’s workflow automation and customer management features.

Business Case:
Currently, Method:CRM is tightly integrated with QuickBooks, offering a seamless experience for users who require a robust CRM with automation and deep accounting sync capabilities. However, many businesses, particularly in industries such as manufacturing, wholesale, and professional services, rely on Sage 50 for their accounting needs. By enabling a Sage 50 sync, Method can access a new market segment and provide Sage users with an advanced CRM solution that enhances their efficiency and business operations.

Key Benefits:

Expanded Market Reach: Opens Method:CRM to a broader audience beyond QuickBooks users, increasing potential customer acquisition.

Seamless Accounting Integration: Sync key financial data (customers, invoices, payments, etc.) between Sage 50 and Method:CRM.

Workflow Automation: Leverage Method’s customization capabilities to streamline sales, invoicing, and customer management for Sage 50 users.

Improved Customer Experience: Provide existing Sage 50 users with a more advanced CRM to improve business processes and customer engagement.

Competitive Advantage: Differentiate Method:CRM from competitors by supporting multiple accounting platforms.
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: API Integration - Google Reviews Request Automation
Description:
Summary:
Enable Method:CRM users to request Google Reviews from customers directly through automated workflows, enhancing customer engagement and online reputation management.

Problem Statement:
Customer reviews, particularly on Google, are essential for businesses to build trust and improve local search rankings. Currently, users must manually request reviews from customers via email, SMS, or other communication channels, which is time-consuming and inconsistent. A built-in automation within Method:CRM would streamline this process, increasing review collection and business credibility.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce an automated Google Review request feature that allows users to send review invitations based on predefined triggers and workflows.



Key Features:

Automated Triggers:

Send review requests after invoice payment, job completion, or customer interaction.

Customizable trigger options to suit business needs.

Prebuilt Templates:

Create and store email/SMS templates with personalized merge fields (e.g., customer name, service details, direct review link).

Option to include incentives (e.g., discount codes) for leaving a review.

Google My Business (GMB) Integration:

Connect Method:CRM with a business’s Google My Business account to generate direct review links.

Retrieve and display recent reviews within Method:CRM.

Review Request Tracking:

Track sent requests, pending responses, and completed reviews.

Automate follow-ups for customers who haven’t left a review within a set timeframe.

Customer Segmentation & Targeting:

Allow users to segment customers based on interactions, transaction history, or NPS scores to target satisfied customers.

Reporting & Analytics:

Dashboard to monitor review request success rates, review sentiment trends, and impact on business ratings.
Solution:
Zapier may be able to facilitate this.

Implementation Considerations:
Requires Google My Business API integration.
Permissions and user roles to control review request access.
Compliance with Google's review solicitation guidelines.

Title: Expense Reporting - QuickBooks Desktop / Enterprise  QBDT
Description:
Expense reports app description by Jason.

A full blown expense reports app that has 90% parity with our Classic app (plus new features!). Today it works for QBDT only but could be customized to be compatible with QBO.

Features: 

Expense Reports are created automatically based on the chosen schedule in the Preferences (daily, weekly, monthly) 

Predefined roles and team managers are set in Preferences. Users are set to be a Submitter, Approver, or Admin and are assigned to a team. Approvers can approve or reject expense reports from their team, and only an admin can then process them and send to QuickBooks. 

On screen reminders of what you need to do based on your permission level. This could be reports waiting to be submitted, waiting to be approved, or waiting to be processed. 

Expenses are sent to QuickBooks as either a reimbursable bill or a non-reimbursable credit card charge based on the payment method selected. Payment methods can be set up in preferences.
Receipts use the new attachment widget and are automatically grouped together in the expense report.
New email templates.
Solution:
Solution Available in the Customization Catalog
- Not available for QBO

Title: Change Calendar Color of a Work Order based on User / Status / Type
Description: Are we able to change Calendar Color of a Work Order based on User / Status / Type?
Solution: This is possible, we simply need to create a new field to hold the color of each user (or service item depending on which you would like the calendar to be colors by) and then update the calendar widget to refer to this new color

Title: Allow for Switching between CC & ACH / Limit Credit Card Processing - ACH Only, Control who can pay with a credit card.
Description:
Customer asks 'is there a way to restrict the certain customer from paying with a credit card? 
I want to block someone from doing CC, and only doing ACH.

- Example, by size of transaction
- Example, by type of customer

Alternatively give customers the option to switch between ACH and CC.
Solution:
Yes, the implementation will require:

1.) How do we identify who can and connect make CC payments
2.) Based on that criteria show them a different version of the widget.

With payments, there is always a considerable amount of testing we recommend to make sure all scenarios are covered.

Title: Work Orders - Ability to include Tax Calculations
Description:
Canadian FS user and would like taxes to be part of the WO process including the Field crew signature piece.

Can Taxes be added and auto calculated at the Field crew level so when the customers sign, they are signing a tax inclusive amount.
Solution:
Taxes can be calculated on work orders.

There are a couple of ways to do this with or without customization.

1.) You can create a new 'Service Item' called 'Sales Tax' which is temporary and calculates the total sales tax on the Work Order.

2.) When this work order is converted to an invoice, this item can be removed and replaced with the correct tax functionality.

This can be done manually (no customization)
This can be facilitated automatically with customization. 

In both cases, remember the information calculated here is only as good as the information the user inputs into the work order at the time of calculation.

Title: Leads & Customer Reportings (Contact Reporting)
Description:
Customer is looking for a print template / report of all the leads in the system.
They would also like to filter this list for leads created in the last 3 months.

In an ideal situation, the ability to know which leads were converted to customers etc.
This information will then be used to prioritize how and when they communicate to open leads which may not have opportunities or activities.

Also important for management to know how many leads are being created every month and by whom.
Solution:
Generally a pretty simple report.

This can also be generated using a grid filter and some customization.
Overall the customer should expect 1-2 hours depending on the formatting this could be a larger.

This estimate assumes a simple csv / excel sheet will suffice.

Title: Universal Tags - Automatically add tags to transactions from contacts or customer - Tag Sharing
Description:
Customer would like the ability to have all tags of an entity universal and visible in all apps.

For his business the tags are useful in all stages of the sales process and having to add tags for each app is very labour intensive.
Solution:
All Tags are universal. Unless you're referring to Classic.
In Classic this cannot be achieved.

In new all tags are universal.
If you're asking for tags to travel from customer to opportunity to estimate etc.

This can certainly be customized and pretty easy to implement. We would need the customer to identify their key workflows and where tags should always come from.

Title: Ability for the FC to block calendar
Description:
Customer is looking for a solution to block out days for his FC.

They were wondering, can we have a validation system to check if 2 WOs are assigned to the same user at the same time, a message is shown.

Customer is open to suggestions for setting 'driver availability'
Solution:
Can be customized; however, it is not straight forward and prone to error. 

This is probably better suited in a feature request as a platform functions vs. customization. 

We've customized scenarios to check if a customer has an activity in the AM, then list as a conflict; however, our recommendation at this time would be to use the the waitlist.

Title: Access or Login Report for Portal - Portal Usage Reporting - Portal Analytics
Description:
The customer is wondering if it possible to get a report for customer logins for the portal.
They would like reporting so they can understand at a high level, the usage of the portal.

Including but not limited to:
Logins
Payments Made
Documents Uploaded or Downloaded
Solution:
This can be customized,

On every portal screen, we can record to a custom table:

Date || Contact || and Screen Name || record accessed.
Providing the customer of the portal usage and the screens being used.

Depending on the nature of the request, we could also up level this to Estimate last viewed on etc. Of course, as more information is captured the scope can increase but this is certainly something we can do with customization.

Title: Job Costing - Linking Bill to Work Orders
Description:
Customer would like to know if they can link Bills directly with Workorders. 

They want to create a cost report based on workorders for the purpose of profit and margin calculations.
Solution:
The process of linking Bills to Work Orders is quite simple.
One the bills are linked they can be displayed and total to calculate profit and margin.

The bills will need to be linked using Method in order to ensure they're attached to work orders, they can then be displayed on the work order.

Title: Email Tracking - Advanced Analytics - Number of Opens or Clicks
Description: Customer is requesting that in addition to our Email status for open, clicked etc, can we track when and how many times a recipient views a specific email.
Solution:
At this time this cannot be customized; however, this is  valid feature request to understand how many times an email is opened or clicked.

Defintely a useful request, for proposals for example.
It's nice to see how often a proposal is viewed before it was signed. 

If the customer is emailing a portal link, we can certainly build this level of tracking on to portal page loads etc. To keep track of how many times the estimate or invoice was opened in the portal. There is an existing customization entry for this request that you may be able to suggest to the customer.

Title: QBO - Updating Transaction Subtotals and Totals in Real-Time (Not just on save)
Description:
Customer would like to change logic controlling estimate screens update. 

Out of the box, the line-items and subtotals are updated on save/email/print. 
Customer would like the subtotal to be more dynamic and update on any change.

The Dynamic nature of being able to update is important so we can keep track of totals as items are added. Rather than adding items then pressing save to know the total.
Solution:
Currently listing this as should not be customized; but can be customized. 
Everytime something changes on the line item for example, we call save.

Doing this will slow the screen down but can provide the customer with real-time updates. The reason is because, some of these totals and amounts are calculated by QuickBooks or our Business Rules Engine.

At the bottom of the editable grid, we also have a running total for the transaction typically under the Amount column in a light grey color.

Title: Sync with Vendor Credits - Vendor Credit
Description:
Vendor Credits are credits that you receive from your vendor as an equivalent of the amount that they owe you. With vendor credits, you can track this amount until it is paid by the vendor, refunded, or applied to other bills of the vendor.

Since I'm using this CRM for both Vendor and customer management, i need to be able to keep track of credits or open credits i have with my Vendors.
Solution: At this time cannot be customized until it becomes available via Sync.

Title: Report Designer - Merge Multiple Reports - Merge Reports - Generate Report Action - Generate Multiple Reports at Once
Description:
DevExpress has the concept of Mergin reports, by adding a subreport to a report and having it print at the end or beginning of another report. But that function is lacking in the Method version? or am i missing something?

https://devexpress.github.io/dotnet-eud/reporting-for-web/articles/report-designer/merge-reports.html

As for the consulting team doing it for me, are you sure that's a functionality they can access? I'm a pretty experienced customizer (10+ years) and I can't do this (merge reports) without calling an external API. I'd be very curious how anyone could merge reports real time in Method without an API call.

 Eg. When I Print an Invoice, I want to also print the related work order, and a tertiary inspection sheet that is linked to the work order as well. Right now as a user, I have to print these reports 3 different times, then merge them using an external utility or actually print them physically and staple them together
Solution:
Merging reports or running to consecutive generate report actions and outputting a single PDF isn't customizable today.

However, when this request does come in, a potential solution could be to create a centralized table where a single report could be created that reflects information from a variety of places in one combined, condensed format.

Title: Sync with QuickBooks Deposits - QBO Deposits
Description:
I am currently using deposits to track my donations. 
Method does not sync with deposits, so I can't see any historical information from my Donors in the CRM. 

Is this possible?
Solution: Currently, we do not have access to deposits via the API. We will need to review and enhance the API before this can be customized.

Title: Calendar Improvement - Address on Calendar - Show Address - Show Address on Calendar
Description:
Customer sales workflow is primarily face to face, especially, the key assessment/estimate activity. 

He wants to utilize Methods activity calendar and its integration with Google Calendars. 

He finds the activity screenlet limiting and needs the ability to see the shipping address in the activities apps. 

This requirement also applies on the activities list view where the physical location is a key data point.
Solution:
This is a very simple customization.

Certainly a DIY Customization, where you can:

1.) Customize the Calendar Widget
2.) Edit the details expression
3.) Add the 3-4 address fields the customer would like to see listed.

Title: API Integration - Mailchimp Integration Improvement

Grid Views

Michael - Sept 16-2024

Title: QBO Multiple Shipping Addresses - Customizing multiple ship addresses
Description: The end goal is to be able to choose different locations we are performing work for the same customer. Same customer, same billing, same contact person, possible different locations.
Solution:
The solution is quite simple: 
1.) We would need to create a new table  -- DO NOT USE THE ShipAddress table 
2.) We would need to create an address type field, Primary Billing, Primary Shipping, and Other 
3.) Primary Billing should reflect the Billing Address for the Customer in QuickBooks 
4.) Primary Shipping should reflect the Shipping Address for the Customer in QuickBooks 
5.) All other addresses would be listed as Other 
6.) On the transaction screens, we would still pre-fill with the defaults from QuickBooks but provide a dropdown for address where the custom addresses would be selected.
7.) Nightly, we would loop through the addresses and update Billing and Shipping Addresses.
8.) Transaction screens and activity screens would need to be updated to pull addresses from this new custom table rather than the customer table.

Title: Inspection App - Verification and Compliance - Assessing conditions and standards
Description:
Purpose and Focus
Inspection App: Primarily designed for verification and compliance, focusing on systematically assessing conditions, equipment, and standards. For example, a fire safety inspection app would allow inspectors to check fire alarms, sprinklers, extinguishers, and evacuation routes, ensuring they comply with regulations and function as intended.

Workflow and Usage
Inspection App: Inspections follow a structured, checklist-based format where items are assessed, marked as “pass/fail,” and documented for compliance. Users typically log details such as dates, location, condition, and photos, ensuring that every element meets safety codes.


Data Tracking and Reporting
Inspection App: Emphasizes data collection, trend analysis, and compliance reporting. Reports summarize the status of inspections, identify recurring issues, and highlight areas needing improvement.

Inspection vs. Work order
Use an Inspection App if your primary need is ensuring compliance, assessing risks, or adhering to regulatory requirements. This app is ideal for scheduled assessments of equipment or environments, ensuring everything is up to code.
Use a Work Order App if your goal is to manage and track repair and maintenance tasks efficiently. This app is best for prioritizing, assigning, and tracking corrective actions arising from inspection reports or other triggers.
Solution:
An inspection app can be customized, it should probably be coupled with an equipment app. 

This way we can manage assets, inspection history and work order history if required.

Title: Integration with Microsoft Power Automate
Description:
Summary:
Enable Method:CRM to integrate with Microsoft Power Automate, providing users with powerful workflow automation capabilities to enhance productivity, streamline processes, and create custom automations between Method and other applications in the Microsoft ecosystem.

Use Case:
Users of Method:CRM who also leverage Microsoft products such as Office 365, SharePoint, Teams, and Dynamics 365 want a seamless way to connect their CRM data and workflows to these tools. By integrating with Power Automate, users can create automated workflows triggered by Method:CRM events, such as new leads, updated invoices, or completed tasks. These workflows can interact with hundreds of supported apps within the Microsoft ecosystem and beyond.

Key Features:

Trigger-based Automations:
Allow Power Automate to trigger workflows based on events in Method:CRM, such as:

New lead creation
Updated customer records
Invoice generation or status updates
Activity completions
Data Connectivity:
Provide connectors in Power Automate that interact with Method:CRM tables, enabling users to:

Read data (e.g., retrieve customer details or invoice information)
Write data (e.g., create or update records like activities or sales orders)
Bidirectional Sync:
Allow Method:CRM data to sync with other applications, ensuring data consistency and reducing manual data entry across platforms.

Custom Workflow Creation:
Support custom workflows such as:

Notify teams in Microsoft Teams when a high-value deal is won in Method.
Automatically create a task in Microsoft Planner when a lead is assigned in Method.
Add new Method contacts to a SharePoint list for document management.
Sync Method invoices with a Power BI dashboard for advanced reporting.
Prebuilt Templates:
Provide prebuilt templates in Power Automate to simplify common use cases, such as:

Sending email notifications for overdue invoices.
Exporting CRM data to Excel for reporting.
Creating a PowerPoint presentation of top leads using live CRM data.
Benefits:

Increased Efficiency: Automate repetitive tasks and streamline workflows to save time.
Enhanced Collaboration: Integrate Method data into tools like Teams and SharePoint to improve team communication.
Scalability: Empower businesses to design workflows that scale with their operational needs.
Data Visibility: Sync Method data with Power BI to unlock advanced analytics and reporting.
Custom Solutions: Provide flexibility for users to create workflows tailored to their business processes.
Solution:
This is a complex undertaking and will likely requestion API integrations to carry the load. 
Best to work with a dedicated consultant to review their needs and develop their specialized workflows.

Title: Reporting Improvements - KPI Tracking - Revenue Forecasting
Description:
Objective: Enable consulting businesses using MethodCRM to generate accurate revenue forecasting reports. The feature should allow users to set revenue cadences, track progress-based or project-based revenue, and highlight key revenue metrics in reports.

Key Features:

Revenue Cadence Setup:

Allow users to set up recurring revenue streams (monthly, quarterly, yearly, etc.) based on customer contracts or service agreements.

The cadence setup should integrate with the existing invoicing system to automate the forecasting of recurring revenue.

Progress-Based/Project-Based Revenue Tracking:

Add functionality for project tracking that ties into revenue recognition.

Users should be able to forecast revenue based on percentage completion of projects or specific milestones reached.

Allow for flexibility in tracking various project phases and assigning revenue portions to each phase.

Customizable Forecasting Reports:

Create customizable reports that highlight key revenue metrics such as forecasted versus actual revenue.

Include visual indicators (e.g., progress bars, flags) to highlight revenue targets or shortfalls.

Allow reports to display by cadence (monthly, quarterly, yearly) and by individual project or contract.

Report Sharing & Export:

Ensure that users can export these reports into CSV, Excel, or PDF for sharing with stakeholders.

Include the ability to filter and customize what’s displayed in the report based on project or client data.
Solution:
While technically possible; this is a pretty large project.

Out of the box we do not have tools to make this customization 'simple' or 'easy' in general everything would need to be designed from the ground up to specifications.

Title: Customer Referral Program - Link a Customer to another Customer / Lead
Description:
Customer who is looking to move from CopperCRM to Method asked to get a quote on the following:

1. Ability to relate customer lead to customer. This is for tracking purposes. Their actual customers are distributors. Through Method, they want to enter end consumer as lead and link this lead with their actual customer (distributor).

2. Ability to relate one contact to multiple companies; without entering the same contacts for multiple times. i.e John Smith is a contact for Method:CRM HQ John is also a contact at Method:CRM Vancouver. A way to have a field on Contacts screen to tell users that John smith is a contact for two different locations. 
 
3. Custom field to capture customer type i.e distributor, engineer

4. Ability to limit activity type selection - whenever their user is creating an activity in Method, the activity type dropdown will only show certain values; depending on the type of customer i.e distributor, engineer, etc. 

5. Add value dropdown to activity; allowing users to quantify the importance of each one of the activity and to ultimately being able to run a report based on its value
Solution:
1.  Relating customer lead to customer  - Create a dropdown on the entity table based on customer fullname. - bring over to new contact, view contact, and edit entity screen (30-1.5 hours)

2. Create new table that's linked to the contact table have a dropdown for Entity Name. Create an editable grid and place it on the view contact screen (1-3 hours)

3. Create a dropdown on the customer table, and place it on the entity table then show it on New, view, and edit entity screens. (30-1.5 hour)

There's also a customer type dropdown in QB that comes over to method. If they use this, it will be 0 hour customization

4. Create another field on the activity type table with different values. Add a field to the entity table for what type of data they want to show. Set the field to show on the new contact, view contact, and edit entity screens. Link this field over to the activities and add selection criteria to the activity type dropdown to show certain data.  (1-3 hours)

5. Need more info here about the report. Adding a dropdown and bringing it onto the screen is 10-30 minutes. Report time depends on what you need.

Title: API Integration Trello - Creating a Lead from a Card

ZAPIER INTEGRATION -- Always requires a lot of testing, this could make the setup longer of shorter.

This integration is quite simple; however, there are a couple of pre-requisites.

Title: Shipping Rules App - Rules Based on Dimensions and Weight for Shipments
Description:
Customer would like to enhance the shipping rules system to evaluate and calculate parcel or pallet shipping costs based on item dimensions and weight for estimates/orders, ensuring compliance with shipping constraints and efficiency in packaging.

Items can live in boxes, pallettes, and other shipping Methods depending on item sku & quantities on an order.


Feature Request: Shipping Rules Based on Dimensions and Weight for Offline Work
Objective
Enhance the shipping rules system to evaluate and calculate parcel or pallet shipping costs based on item dimensions and weight for estimates/orders, ensuring compliance with shipping constraints and efficiency in packaging.

Example Implementation
- Validate shipping feasibility against defined rules.
- Dynamically calculate the number of boxes/pallets required based on item weights and dimensions.
Requirements

A. New/Edit Estimate
Enhance functionality to analyze shipping rules during the estimate creation or modification process.

B. Rules Configuration
Instant Fail Rule
Criteria: If any item’s weight exceeds 30 kg. 

Action:
Automatically adjust parcel box count to 5.
Skip over specific parcel rules (e.g., 3 and 5) that cannot accommodate such items.

Implementation:
Link item weight directly to estimate lines.
Remove outdated conditional logic (line 28) and implement revised rule sets from scratch.

Packaging Rule
Criteria: If all items weigh ≤ 30 kg.
Action:
Calculate if the entire order can fit into a maximum of 4 boxes.
Determine the exact number of boxes required if the order exceeds 4-box capacity.
Reference fncCalculatePalletShippingCost to implement cost estimation logic for pallet shipping as fallback.

Functional Details
Weight Validation
Extract item-level weight data from the estimate.
Link dynamically to estimate lines for seamless validation.
Box Calculation Logic

Use fncCalculatePalletShippingCost as a template to:
Aggregate item weights and dimensions.
Simulate packing scenarios based on preset box dimensions and weight limits.
Return the required number of boxes or trigger a transition to pallet shipping.
Rule Application

Automatically apply appropriate rules during offline work:
Assign parcel rules for orders ≤ 4 boxes.
Assign pallet rules for orders exceeding 4 boxes or containing items > 30 kg.
Solution:
This workflow is certainly customizable; however, the estimate could be larger than the above depending on the number of combinations and other rules that may be applicable to certain items. 

For example, liquids may need to be in a separate shipment than non-liquid materials etc.

Title: Item & Order Reminders - Out of Stock Notifications or Follow-up to sell new versions or upgrades.
Description:
Order reminder notifications:
Account manager receives an 'order reminder' notification based on a specified time period, e.g. 30 days after customer's last order or interaction (we started creating this on our call)
An email is sent once every day/week (account manager can choose) to the account manager listing all the customers who are past their 'order reminder' time period
The list would include: customer name, last order date, last interaction date
The account manager has the ability to snooze these notifications for individual customers for X number of days

Event reminder notifications:
Many of our customers attend events throughout the year - we'd want to be reminded X number of days before the events so we can supply them with stock. The account manager would need to enter the dates of the events, and set reminder dates

Reporting:
All customers by last order date
All customers showing average order value. Ability to filter by date (e.g. show average order value between January & October 2020)
Activities and sales by account manager

Other:
If a product is out of stock and a customer wants to purchase, the account manager could make a note and check a list once it's back in stock
E.g. a list of all customers who want to order WP40 Red
Solution:
The reminder notification pieces can be done but will take quite a bit of time. An alternate solution would be to create a custom Activities View Filter to keep track of their tasks and to-do's. Rough estimate of about 10 hours.

The reporting piece alone can take 20 hours to complete depending on details, such as what data needs to appear on the report and how it should appear. Rough estimate at this moment is about 15 hours.

The back order piece comes up once in a while and is usually well handled within QBDT. However, to create quick and dirty solution inside Method would take about 5+ hours which can include a list or basic report of customers on that list. The important part about this workflow piece is to ensure they're following their processes and correctly taking customers off this list. Title: Assigning Serial Numbers to Items
Description:
Customer is a selling of Motorola radios and sell basically a hand full of items.
Each item must have a serial number associated.

Can a generic Motorola radio be invoiced and automatically serialized with a unique number and tracked for said customer via a custom equipment app or field.
Solution:
Serial Numbers are sometimes available via QuickBooks. If the customer is currently not using QuickBooks Serial number functionality, we can certainly customize a solution to assign a unique serial number for each item sold. 

If the customer is looking for us to provide a number, I would recommend to expose the InvoiceLine's RecordID to be used for this. Easy, simple and will always be unique. This would only take about 15 mins to expose on the New/Edit Invoice, Invoice PDF and Guest - View Invoice screen.

That said, if the device has it's own number, the same process as above can be done; however, we would need to create a new field where the customer could key in this information. 

A custom app could certainly be designed to keep track of all the items sold by customer; however, more information is required:

- Should this info be accessible in the Customer Portal? 
- Are there any additional details we need to capture about the devices that is not captured directly on the invoice?
- Do User's need to send emails in this App? 
- Are there any Reports that need to be created? 
As you can see there are lots of questions about their expectations behind this App, which is why I made this a medium request of 5 to 10 hours.

Title: Landed Cost Calculator - Landed Costs Reporting - Landed Cost Summary
Description:
Landed cost is the total cost of a product on its journey from the factory floor to the buyer’s door12. It includes the price of goods, shipment costs, insurance fees, customs duties, and any other charges incurred along the way2. Landed cost is an essential way to calculate a company’s bottom line.

What is Landed Cost? - Definition | Meaning | Example
https://www.myaccountingcourse.com/accounting-dictionary/landed-cost

Landed Cost: Meaning & Calculator | Freightos
https://www.freightos.com/freight-resources/understanding-landed-cost-and-profitability/

--------------------

Suppose you are looking to import 100 units of a product from Shanghai to Los Angeles. The per-unit cost is $50. The freight cost for the shipment is $1000 and it represents one-quarter of the shipment. The duty charge is set at 5%.

Per Unit Cost + Freight Cost + Duty Charge = Landed Cost Per Unit

$50 + ((1000 * 25%) / 100) + (5% * $50) = $55 Per Unit

Now let’s throw a curveball into the equation. Let’s say your goods are held at customs for three days and you are charged $100 for each day:

Per Unit Cost + Freight Cost + Duty Charge + Additional Charge = Landed Cost Per Unit

$50 + ((1000 * 25%) / 100) + (5% * $50) + (($100 * 3) / 100) = $58 Per Unit

The unforeseen cost added an additional $3 to your landed cost per unit. With this information in mind, you may choose to adjust your pricing model.

These examples may seem like a bit of a simplification of the concept, but Freightos users enjoy this kind of no-hassle math with every shipment.

Our marketplace is free to try, but you may prefer to begin with our freight rate and import duty calculators.
Solution:
Depending on the complexity of the calculation this can take longer or shorter.

In most calculators you're able to just key in values and spit out a result. However, with Method we can do more than just calculate but also aggregate.

So as part of our calculator, we can aggregate information from invoices, bills, item and item costs to really provide a dynamic reporting function.

Title: Automation around Unit of Measure Conversion - UOM Conversion - Dynamically Calculate and convert

Title: Electronic Data Interchange - EDI - Support EDI
Description:
Organizations can save time and reduce errors by automating paper-based transactions.

In EDI transactions, information moves directly from a computer application in one organization to a computer application in another. EDI standards define the location and order of information in a document format. Sharing data is now faster and easier with this automated capability, compared to using paper documents or other traditional methods that can take hours, days or even weeks.

Today, industries use EDI integration to share a range of document types, from purchase orders to invoices to requests for quotations to loan applications and more. In most instances, these organizations are trading partners that exchange goods and services frequently as part of their supply chains and business-to-business (B2B) networks.
Solution:
There are a number of solutions, for Method, EDI would need to be established via our API. 
Implementation, depending on the complexity is XXL at a minimum. 

Our team can consultant on the matter, tables and data structure from QB and Method.
However, we do not create or facilitate the process through any technical means. 
-------------------------



For some businesses, EDI can be difficult to implement. . It is  inherently complex, as it needs to accommodate the complexities of global business needs. For example, each trading partner in a B2B network can present individual requirements. Many organizations outsource EDI solutions due to unique formatting criteria.

Whether in-house or outsourced, there are some basic conditions, capabilities and resources that are needed to implement EDI effectively. In addition to factors such as agreement on document types, secure transmission methods and necessary hardware and software, an effective EDI implementation should consider:

Translation or mapping software

This type of transformation software takes fields such as names, addresses, currency amounts, part numbers and quantities, and maps them from business application formats into standardized documents and vice versa.

Batch enveloping or de-enveloping capabilities

These capabilities support large EDI message batches by enabling senders and receivers to wrap and unwrap transactions. The transactions can then be grouped from or split into several divisions or areas of a trading associate’s business.

Message routing mechanisms

After a message is de-enveloped, routing mechanisms are required to sort the messages for different groups and deliver them to the appropriate targets. Message transformation may also be required to get the message into the correct format for its destination.

Trading partner agreements (TPA)

TPA clarifies terms and conditions, establishes standards for business documents and defines communications and business protocols between trading partners.

Title: Search Improvement - Enhance Fuzzy Search Functionality
Description:
Description:
The current search functionality in the Items app requires exact matches, including spaces, which limits its flexibility and user experience. 

For instance:
If an item is named 'test sale' and a user searches for 'test sale' (with two spaces) or adds an extra trailing space ('test sale '), no results are returned.
Similarly, in the advanced search, applying a filter using 'contains' fails to return results when extra spaces are included in the search term.
While the current behavior ensures precise matches, customers are expressing dissatisfaction and requesting a more user-friendly and flexible search experience that accommodates minor variations, such as extra spaces or typos.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce fuzzy search logic in the Items app to improve the search experience. This could include:

Ignore Whitespace Variations: 
Treat multiple spaces or trailing spaces as a single space during searches.

Partial Match Support: Allow partial and close matches to be displayed, even if there are slight variations in input (e.g., typo-tolerance).

Enhanced Advanced Search Filters: Ensure that filters using 'contains' are robust to whitespace and minor variations.
Benefits:

Improves user satisfaction by making the search functionality more intuitive.
Reduces frustration for customers dealing with minor errors in search input.
Aligns with modern user expectations for search flexibility.

Additional Notes:
This enhancement is crucial to meet customer expectations and ensure their workflows are seamless. While this may initially appear to be a bug, the current design was intended for exact matches. The proposed changes would represent an upgrade in functionality rather than a fix.
Solution: This cannot be customized at this time; however, a great request.
