Title: Takeoff Workflow for Construction Estimation
Description:
A 'takeoff' workflow in construction refers to the process of determining and quantifying the materials, labor, and equipment needed for a project based on the construction plans or blueprints. The takeoff is a critical part of the estimating and bidding process, ensuring accurate cost estimation and procurement.

Can this type of workflow be customized?
Solution:
This is a large scale project with may components and the nature of the scope is complicated. Here is a summary of key elements we would like to customize in order to develop the Takeoff Workflow.

Blueprint/Plan Import & Measurement Tools
The feature will allow users to upload construction blueprints, site plans, or technical drawings in PDF or image format. Once uploaded, users can set a measurement scale based on the drawing's reference points. This will enable accurate area, length, and volume measurements directly within the platform. The tool will support various measurement types, such as linear measurements for pipes and wiring, surface area calculations for flooring or roofing, and volume calculations for concrete and excavation work. Users will be able to mark sections, label items, and create visual annotations to streamline the takeoff process.

Material, Labor, & Equipment Database
A centralized database will store information on materials, labor rates, and equipment costs, which can be accessed and customized for each project. Users can define unit costs for common materials like lumber, steel, concrete, drywall, and electrical components, as well as labor costs based on job roles, hourly rates, and expected completion times. The system will allow businesses to import or integrate supplier price lists, ensuring that takeoff calculations reflect current market rates. Additionally, rental or purchase costs for equipment, such as excavators, cranes, and scaffolding, can be included to provide a comprehensive cost breakdown.

Automated Quantity Takeoff & Cost Breakdown
Once measurements are taken from the blueprints, the system will automatically generate a list of required materials, labor, and equipment. Users can apply predefined formulas to account for material waste, additional labor hours for complex tasks, or transportation costs. The takeoff tool will calculate the estimated costs dynamically and allow users to adjust pricing manually if needed. If a change is made to the blueprint or scope, the system will automatically update the quantity takeoff data, ensuring real-time cost adjustments.

Estimate & Proposal Generation
The takeoff results will be formatted into a professional estimate that includes an itemized cost breakdown, labor hours, material lists, and total project costs. The estimate will be customizable, allowing users to add terms and conditions, payment schedules, and client-specific details. Once finalized, the proposal can be exported to PDF, sent directly to the client from the CRM, or integrated into the company’s invoicing system.

Sync & Reporting Features
The takeoff data will be synchronized with QuickBooks to streamline invoicing and procurement. Users will be able to generate reports that analyze estimated costs versus actual project expenses, track profitability, and assess resource utilization. Custom reporting options will allow businesses to filter takeoff data by project type, material category, or labor hours, providing insights that can improve future estimates and decision-making. Additionally, a historical database of past takeoffs will be maintained, enabling users to reference previous projects for benchmarking and cost comparisons.

Item Name

Vendor name
Items quoted
Prices and terms
Any additional supplier notes

Select one or multiple vendors to approve.

An end to end solution of this scope would be quite large as multiple components and data matching is required.

Title: Vendor Event Opportunity Sharing in Customer Portal Summary
Description:
A customer in event management needs a way to share upcoming activities and opportunities with their vendors (e.g., musicians) via the customer portal. This would allow vendors to view, express interest, and potentially accept event opportunities in a centralized location.

Proposed Functionality:

Event Posting: Admin users can create and publish upcoming events with details such as date, time, location, event type, and payment information.
Vendor Visibility: Vendors (e.g., musicians) can log into the customer portal and see a list of upcoming opportunities relevant to their profile.
Expression of Interest: Vendors can indicate interest, ask questions, or accept/decline invitations through the portal.
Notifications & Alerts: Vendors receive automated notifications (email/SMS) when new opportunities are available.
Filtering & Tagging: Ability to categorize events by type (e.g., concerts, private gigs) and filter based on location, payment, or availability.
Business Impact:

Streamlines communication between event organizers and vendors.
Reduces manual outreach and back-and-forth discussions.
Ensures vendors stay engaged and informed about relevant opportunities.
Improves vendor experience and retention.
Possible Integration Points:

CRM activity tracking for event/vendor interactions.
Calendar sync for accepted events.
Payment tracking for confirmed gigs.
Solution:
1. Data Structure Updates
To support this feature, the database would need additional tables and fields:

Events Table: Stores event details (name, date, time, location, description, status, payment details, etc.).
Vendor Profiles Table (Enhancement): Expands existing vendor records to include event preferences, availability, and categories (e.g., musician, caterer, photographer).
Event-Vendor Relationship Table: Tracks which vendors have been invited, expressed interest, or accepted/rejected an opportunity.
Customization Effort: Medium—this requires defining relationships between events and vendors.

2. Customer Portal Enhancements
The Customer Portal would require UI/UX modifications:

Event Listings Page: Displays all available opportunities to vendors.
Event Details Page: Shows event-specific information and an action panel (Interested, Decline, Ask a Question).
Vendor Interaction Panel: Allows vendors to express interest, confirm participation, or communicate with the event manager.
Customization Effort: High—this would require new portal screens, role-based visibility, and dynamic UI elements.

3. Workflow Automation & Notifications
Automation is needed to streamline interactions between event managers and vendors:

Event Creation Workflow: Automates event posting and vendor notifications.
Interest Submission Workflow: Triggers an email/SMS when a vendor expresses interest.
Event Confirmation Workflow: Updates event status when vendors confirm participation and sends confirmation emails.
Customization Effort: Medium—this involves setting up action-based workflows in Method.

Treat Vendors with work orders as field crew staff and set user permissions correctly to ensure they only see their own records.

Title: Sales Order Improvement & Invoice Improvement – Displaying PO Status for Customers
Description:
Customers need a way to display the Purchase Order (PO) status for each item on a Sales Order or Invoice, particularly when multiple POs are involved. This would enable businesses to provide real-time status updates to their clients at the item level, improving transparency and reducing manual status inquiries.

Use Case:

Solution:
Note that in general, it's recommended that Sales Orders / Invoices are converted to POs in Method. 
This will allow us to establish a connection between each of the line items. 

Then we can add a PO Status column on Sales Orders and Invoices that dynamically reflects the status of the linked PO for each item.

Display this information on the customer portal, so clients can see order progress without needing to contact support.

Allow configurable settings for businesses to choose which PO statuses to display (e.g., show only “Shipped” and “Received” vs. all statuses).

Optionally include an estimated delivery date based on PO information for better visibility

Traditionally commissions apps have always been built on sales transactions; however, the same can be applied to Purchase Orders and Purchase transactions.

Title: Transaction Improvement - Link Purchase Orders (POs) and Invoices

Financial Reconciliation: Show a breakdown of invoiced vs. remaining amounts.

Impact
✅ Saves time by reducing manual cross-referencing.
✅ Improves financial tracking and reduces missed invoices.
✅ Enhances visibility into order fulfillment.
✅ Ensures accuracy in billing and accounting records.

Title: Date Function Improvement - Native Business Day Calculation Functions in Method CRM / Weekend
Description:
Summary
Introduce built-in Date Functions in Method CRM that allow users to count and add business days (excluding weekends and optionally holidays). This would eliminate the need for external APIs or complex workarounds when calculating due dates, lead times, or scheduling tasks.

Problem Statement
Currently, there is no native support for counting or adding business days in Method CRM. Users have to either:

Manually adjust dates by checking if the result falls on a weekend.
Use an external API for business day calculations, which adds complexity, potential costs, and dependency on third-party services.
This is a basic function available in Excel but is missing in Method CRM. Implementing this natively would improve efficiency and streamline workflows for users handling scheduling, invoicing, and task management.

Proposed Solution
Enhance the Date Functions in Method CRM by introducing:

BUSINESSDAYS.ADD(Start Date, Number of Days, [Holiday List]) → Returns a future date after adding a given number of business days (excluding weekends and optionally holidays).
BUSINESSDAYS.DIFF(Start Date, End Date, [Holiday List]) → Returns the number of business days between two dates.
ISBUSINESSDAY(Date, [Holiday List]) → Returns TRUE if a given date is a business day, FALSE otherwise.

User Impact
Faster, more efficient scheduling without needing external APIs.
More accurate due date calculations for invoices, tasks, and workflows.
Reduced complexity in app customization since users no longer have to build logic to check if a date falls on a weekend.
Solution: In theory this could be customized by stringing together a bunch of date checks and conditional statements; however you'd need to loop through the incremental days and validate if they're business days then +1.

Title: Migration - MindCloud Account Transfer to Method
Description:
Background:
Many customers transitioning from MindCloud to Method:CRM encounter challenges due to differences in data structures, import processes, and workflow adaptations. A streamlined migration process would reduce friction, enhance adoption, and increase customer satisfaction.

Problem Statement:
Currently, migrating data from MindCloud to Method involves manual data exports, transformation, and re-imports, leading to:

Data integrity issues with mismatched fields and missing relationships.
Limited automation in bringing over workflows, custom fields, and user roles.
High support overhead, as users require assistance from CSMs and support staff.
Solution:
Proposed Solution:
Develop a MindCloud Migration Assistant within Method that automates key aspects of the migration, reducing manual intervention and setup time.

Key Features:
Automated Data Importer

Direct CSV and API integration for bulk data transfers.
Pre-mapped fields based on MindCloud’s standard database structure.
Auto-matching for common fields like Contacts, Invoices, Jobs, and Custom Fields.
Field Mapping & Validation Tool

A UI-driven field mapper that lets users review and adjust data mapping before import.
Automated detection of missing or duplicate fields with suggestions for resolution.
Error handling and preview functionality before finalizing import.
Workflow & Customization Import

Ability to transfer key workflows and business rules from MindCloud.
Automated recreation of custom fields, forms, and saved filters in Method.
Smart prompts for users to manually confirm workflows that don’t have direct equivalents.
Migration Dashboard & Logs

Centralized dashboard tracking import status, errors, and completion rates.
Exportable migration logs for troubleshooting.
Undo/rollback functionality to revert migrations if issues arise.
Guided Setup & Support

Step-by-step migration wizard to guide users through the process.
Inline help, video walkthroughs, and a direct link to Method’s support team.
Option for CSMs to oversee or assist in high-value migrations.

Title: Estimate Customization - Art Work Approval

Where we have a project tracking elements (likely cases) that could be linked together with estimates to track the progress and approval of individual samples.

Title: Web to Lead Improvement - 2-Step Verification for Web-to-Lead Form Submissions
Description:
Summary:
Enhance the security and lead quality of web-to-lead forms by introducing optional 2-step verification using email or SMS, similar to how users verify their identity when accessing the Method Portal.

Request Details:
We would like to implement a 2-step verification process for web-to-lead forms submitted on external websites. This feature should function as follows:

Step 1: The lead submits their basic contact information (e.g., name, phone, email) via the form.
Step 2: Before the submission is finalized or saved into the CRM, the user receives a one-time verification code either via:
Text (SMS) to their mobile number
Email to their provided address
Only after entering the correct code will the submission be considered verified and passed through to the Method CRM lead list.

Business Value:

Prevents spam and bot submissions.

Improves lead authenticity and reduces sales follow-up time on fake/incomplete leads.

Aligns with security standards already present in the Method Portal login experience.

Optional Enhancements:

Admin setting to enable/disable verification by form.

Option to choose between SMS, Email, or both verification methods.

Custom messaging for the verification prompt.

Let me know if you'd like this tailored for submission in a partner portal, internal ticket system, or a specific formatting style (e.g., Jira, Monday.com).
Solution:
In theory, the SMS workflow could be customized. 

Once a phone number is entered, have the user click verify. 
We then send a text to the phone number on screen and generate a 6-digit code (Could be the last Activity RecordID + todays date, 2025-04-01) 

Then as part of the submission make the phone number and code required. 
Verify would generate the code and store it in a hidden field on screen. 

On Submit check the code if it matches.

Title: API Integration - ODBC Connection Support for Method
Description:
Request Summary:
Allow users to connect Method:CRM to external databases (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle) via ODBC connections for seamless data querying, reporting, and synchronization.

Problem Statement:
Many businesses maintain external data sources outside of QuickBooks and Method, such as ERP systems, inventory management tools, or legacy software. Currently, integrating these systems with Method requires custom API work or third-party middleware, which is time-consuming and lacks native reliability.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce ODBC connection capabilities to enable Method users to:

Query external databases directly from Method apps or tables.

Create reports or dashboards that reflect real-time or scheduled pulls from external sources.

Use external data within Method’s customization and automation tools (e.g., conditional workflows, dropdown sources, validation logic).

Benefits:

Reduces dependency on custom API work or middleware solutions.

Streamlines data access and reporting from multiple systems.

Increases Method’s value proposition for mid-sized and enterprise clients with complex data ecosystems.

Improves accuracy by reducing manual data re-entry or imports.

Use Cases:

Syncing real-time inventory levels from an external WMS (Warehouse Management System).

Pulling shipping or logistics data into Method for customer status updates.

Linking a third-party quoting system’s product catalog for use in Method sales workflows.

Generating unified reports combining QuickBooks, Method, and other SQL-based systems.

Suggested Technical Requirements:

Secure credential management for ODBC connections (connection strings).

Ability to define and save queries within Method.

Option to schedule query runs (e.g., hourly, daily).

Field mapping tools to align external data with Method fields.

Support for read-only access initially; potential for write-back in future iterations.
Solution: This could be incredibly useful for companies with larger operations and have multiple data connections.

Title: Sales Order Enhancement - Ability to see Items yet to be invoice
Description:
Customer often has to back order certain items on a sales order. 

They are wondering if we can customize a way so that they can report on how many items have yet to be invoice on the sales order app?
Solution:
For QuickBooks Desktop users, this functionality is available out of the box.

For QuickBooks Online users, this functionality would need to be customized, it is relatively straight forward however, there are some considerations to make:

At a high level, we would have a status on the order (Not Fulfilled, Partially Fulfilled, Back-Order Completed)

Then we would need to review key workflows:
eg. What happens when an invoice is deleted in Method or in QuickBooks.
eg. Is there ever a scenario where items could be exchanged, returned 
eg. What happens when a sales order cannot be fully fullfilled and manually closed.

These questions will generally define the scope of the project; however, certainly possible.

Title: Spellchecker Action Improvement - Spellcheck for HTML Text Editor
Description: The Customer drafts sections of contracts in HTML editors in Method for display on their Printouts. While the HTML editor will mark words that are spelled incorrectly, it does not suggest corrections. Having a spellcheck function could enhance the tool further.
Solution:
This cannot be customized at this time; however, this is something that is certainly possible as previously available in the classic platform. 

At this time, it's strongly recommended to use browser spell check or related plugins like Grammarly to assist with email and spell checking.

Title: Integrate Method with AccountEdge
Description:
Description:
We are requesting a native integration between Method:CRM and AccountEdge accounting software (developed by Priority Software), supporting both Mac and Windows versions.

Problem Statement:
A subset of our customer base currently uses AccountEdge for their accounting needs instead of QuickBooks or Xero. These customers are unable to take advantage of Method’s real-time sync capabilities, resulting in manual data entry and disconnected workflows. This limits our addressable market and creates friction for potential users evaluating Method as a CRM platform.

Proposed Solution:
Develop a secure, bidirectional sync between Method and AccountEdge that supports:

Customer and vendor record syncing

Invoices, payments, and estimates

Product and inventory data

Time tracking and job costing (if applicable in AccountEdge API)

Benefits:

Expand Method’s compatibility with accounting systems used by SMBs

Attract customers in industries or regions where AccountEdge is preferred over QuickBooks

Reduce churn risk for multi-platform customers or prospects switching from QuickBooks

Technical Considerations:

AccountEdge Connect (cloud) and AccountEdge Pro (desktop) offer APIs via ODBC, SOAP, and REST (depending on version)

May require collaboration with Priority Software for full API documentation and certification

Target Users:

Small to medium-sized businesses using AccountEdge for accounting and job costing

Consultants and resellers in Australia, Canada, and the U.S. who currently support AccountEdge deployments
Solution:
Zapier is available; however, this type of integration should not be supported as it's core functionality required to use our system in many ways.

Best to go native for this type of integration.

Title: Email Campaign Improvement - Newsletter Templates & Guided Campaign Paths
Description:
While the Method Email Campaigns app allows us to send emails, there is no support for newsletter-specific templates or guided campaign structures. This limits our ability to efficiently send branded, recurring content such as company newsletters, product updates, or promotional digests.

Feature Description
Introduce built-in newsletter templates and recommended campaign flows within the Email Campaigns app. This would include:

Pre-designed, mobile-responsive newsletter templates (e.g., Monthly Update, New Feature Release, Promo Highlights)

Ability to drag-and-drop or easily edit layout blocks (images, headers, articles, CTAs)

A 'Create a Newsletter' guided flow with steps like: Select Template → Add Content → Choose Audience → Schedule → Review & Send

Default sections for 'What's New', 'Tips & Tricks', and 'Customer Spotlight'

Support for saving custom templates

Benefits
Increases marketing velocity by reducing setup time

Helps non-designers create polished, branded newsletters

Encourages consistent outbound communication with customers

Reduces the need to rely on third-party email tools for this purpose

Success Criteria
At least 3 pre-built templates available within the first release

80% of users able to create a branded newsletter in under 15 minutes

Adoption by CSM and marketing teams for monthly customer communications
Solution: In theory this could be customized; naturally the complexity of the newsletter / email template will become a factor when scoping.

Title: Work Order Improvement - Auto-Populate Workflow from Scanned Paper Tickets (OCR + Workflow Trigger)
Description:
Summary:
A prospective customer requires a workflow that allows paper-based supervisors to submit scanned job tickets (e.g., work completed forms) and have Method automatically extract the data (via OCR) to populate a corresponding screen or transaction, enabling the next person in the process to review and approve for invoicing.

Use Case:

Supervisors refuse to use electronic systems directly.

They complete jobs on paper and submit scanned copies (PDFs, JPEGs, etc.).

System should read these scanned tickets and:

Extract structured data (e.g., customer name, job ID, service performed, materials used, hours worked).

Populate the relevant Method screen (e.g., Work Order or Time Tracking).

Trigger a workflow or approval flag for admin/office staff to review and approve for invoicing.

Customer Quote:

“If the system can’t read scanned tickets and populate the fields, I can’t move forward.”

Impact:
This feature is a potential dealbreaker for the prospect and could be a competitive differentiator for paper-heavy or field-first industries.
Solution:
Recommendation:
Explore feasibility of OCR + field mapping + trigger workflow capabilities via:

3rd party OCR integrations (e.g., Microsoft Azure Form Recognizer, Google Cloud Vision)

Custom parser logic to convert scan → JSON → Method record

UI configuration to allow for manual override/review before approval

Priority: Medium-High (High if similar field-based prospects emerge)

Next Steps with Lead:

Advised prospect to trial Method + sync with QB Desktop if interested in further evaluation.

Prospect agreed to “do some digging” and may revisit if a workaround or future solution is possible.

Title: Daily KPI Summary Email for CRM Activity (User + Account Level)
Description:
Request Type: Feature Request

Summary:
We would like to introduce a daily KPI summary that provides insight into user and account-level CRM activity. This summary should be delivered via email and optionally accessible within the app as a dashboard widget or downloadable report.

Key Features Requested:

User-Level Activity:

Number of activities created (calls, meetings, emails, tasks)

New leads or contacts added

Opportunities created or moved through stages

Notes or comments added

Account-Level Summary:

Total number of activities across the account

Pipeline movement (deals advanced, closed-won/lost)

Open vs. completed tasks ratio

Most active users

Delivery Options:

Daily email digest (sent at a configurable time)

Option to receive summary by user, manager, or admin

App setting to enable/disable email

Customization:

Ability to configure which KPIs are shown

Filter by role/team

Export to CSV or PDF

Problem This Solves:
Users and managers lack quick visibility into CRM engagement and team productivity. This summary enables proactive coaching, encourages CRM adoption, and helps identify trends or gaps in sales motion on a daily basis.

Benefit:
Improves CRM usage, aligns team around daily habits, and enables better sales and activity tracking without requiring manual report pulling.
Solution:
Certainly possible.

The first part is defining the KPIs in question and then surfacing them up in a screen or a email - where the users could take action.

Title: Opportunity - Item App Improvement - Public Item Catalog Page
Description:
Use Case:
Customer wants to embed a real-time item list from their Items App onto their public website. The goal is to:

Showcase available inventory to customers

Improve SEO by having a crawlable catalog

Keep item info (stock, price, description) in sync automatically

Proposed Functionality:

Add a setting to expose an Item Catalog page publicly (read-only)

Display item name, description, price, and available stock

Include filtering/sorting options for better UX

Auto-update with data from the live Items App

Embed via iframe or provide a direct link

Benefits:

Improves SEO by making product info indexable

Provides customers a self-serve product view

Reduces manual inventory updates on customer websites
Solution:
Workaround Today:
Customized web-to-lead forms (requires consulting hours)

Title: Work Order Improvement - Notification for New Work Order Entry
Description:
Request Summary:
We would like the ability for users (or selected users) to receive a notification each time a new Work Order is created in the system.

Use Case:
Team members such as dispatchers, operations managers, or field technicians need to stay informed in real-time when new jobs are added. This is critical for coordination, planning, and timely response, especially in high-volume service environments.

Proposed Features:

Trigger: On creation of a new Work Order.

Notification Options:

In-app notification (top bar or bell icon)

Email alert

Optional: SMS or push notification

Configuration:

Ability to toggle this notification on/off per user

Optional filter to only be notified for specific Work Order Types or assigned regions

Benefit:
Improves team responsiveness, reduces communication gaps, and ensures no new work orders are missed by relevant personnel.
Solution:
This is pretty simple to customize.

The bulk of the work would be setting the rules as to when the notification goes out, as there may be multiple periods where the save button is clicked / work order adjusted.

Title: Transaction Improvement - The ability Reorder Group items on a transaction
Description: Wants the ability to change the order of the group item within a transaction line table.  - keep the group together and move the entire group between other line.  Can change the order within the group in quickbooks already.
Solution:
In general this should not be customized; as there are complex accounting rules that apply to group items. 

Instead the recommendation would be to build your own set of Method Group Items. 
- These group items can be designed to look and feel similar to group items 
- In QuickBooks, items would appear individually
- However, this will give you more flexibility when it comes to moving items around.

Title: Attachment Widget - Download All Attachments
Description: The customer is looking to download all of the attachments from the attachment widget at the same time. This would make it easier for them to understand all of the process documentation that is being sent by there clients, which could include dozens of attahchments. Currently, they do this by individually downloading each, but this can prove to be time consuming.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time, all attachments can be however emailed at once then from email that can be grouped.

Title: App Routine Improvement - Ability to call another app routine from other routines
Description:
Currently, we can’t call one app routine from within another app routine. This creates a problem when you’ve built multiple app routines for different functions and then need to reuse them inside a new routine that combines their functionality with additional logic.
Of course, I could replicate the actions directly in the new routine, but if I need to reuse the same routine multiple times (for example, three times in one flow), it’s really cumbersome to repeat the same actions over and over just because I can’t call the original routine.
Example: I have an app routine that converts fractions to decimals. Now, I have another routine that calculates pricing for items and needs to convert fractions to decimals for at least three parameters. Since I can’t call the conversion routine, I have to duplicate that logic three times in the pricing routine, which isn’t efficient.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Transaction Improvement - Dirty Transaction - Unsaved Changes Warning on Transactions
Description:
When a user is editing a transaction they move to another screen and lose all their work. Having the ability to prompt the user that leaving the site will cause changes to be lost will be valuable.

This says on transactions but all screens would be improved using this feature.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Transaction Support - Multi-Branding / Multiple Templates for different services / Companies
Description:
Requested Capabilities:
Multiple Template Sets per DBA:

Allow users to configure different sets of:

Email templates (e.g. branded subject line, sender details, messaging tone)

PDF invoice templates (e.g. logos, headers, disclaimers, color scheme)

Portal settings (e.g. welcome message, branding, terms)

Automatic Template Selection:

When interacting with a customer (sending an email, generating an invoice PDF, or granting portal access), Method should automatically select the correct templates based on:

The customer’s Type or Class (used to identify the appropriate DBA)

This ensures each customer receives communication consistent with the correct brand.

Use Case Example:
A customer with Class = “DBA: East Coast Services” receives an invoice with:

East Coast branding on the PDF

A tailored email message from an east@company.com sender

Access to a portal customized for East Coast customers

Benefits:
Supports businesses operating under multiple DBAs without needing separate Method accounts

Reduces manual errors and improves customer experience through consistent branding

Saves time by eliminating the need to manually select templates each time
Solution:
Given the nature of the work, and extent.

RE: Emails & Print Templates
- Certainly possible to drive templates and choice via classes

RE: Portal
- This is a little more difficult; as the home branding should be the umbrella organization. 
- The emails and pdfs generated from the portal could be updated to reflect all the different businesses
- However, the portal header must be the same.

Title: Email Campaigns  - Ability to Click on Email Campaign KPIs to Update Grid View Filter
Description: Each KPI on the view campaign screen corresponds to a view filter on the grid. Users often think that clicking on the KPI would set that view filter, but it does not.
Solution:
Quite a simple customization.

On caption click, trigger view change.

Title: Email Campaign Improvement - Stop Active Email Campaigns, Pause Active Email Campaigns
Description:
Enhance the Email Campaign tool with the ability to:

Pause or Stop an Email Campaign After Launch

Currently, once an email campaign is sent, there’s no way to halt remaining sends. Users should be able to pause or stop an active campaign to prevent further emails from going out.

A customer realizes post-send that an error was made in the campaign content and needs to stop further sends immediately.
Solution: Cannot be customized

Title: Transaction Improvement - Reporting Improvement - Enhanced Currency & Regional Reporting
Description:
Current reporting in Method lacks native tools for multi-currency aggregation and consistent regional reporting. Sales teams working across different markets face difficulty producing unified performance dashboards without manual conversions or external tools.

Proposed Solution:

Add multi-currency support in all native sales reports and dashboards, with automatic conversion to a base reporting currency.

Allow on-demand currency toggling for users to view results in local or global currency.

Enable regional roll-up reporting for territories, countries, or sales regions, aggregating both revenue and deal count metrics.

Impact:
Improves the accuracy and accessibility of global performance insights, reduces manual conversion work, and supports consistent reporting for leadership and regional managers.
Solution:
This is a bit of a complex task to customize. 

In theory we would need to work through all the data and normalize it. 
In general it would be best to standardize all reports to the home currency that is assigned to the account. 

In this case if the customer was Canadian but sold to the US. 
It would be important for us to normalize all says to a single currency CAD.

However, there may be more advanced needs like tax reporting by state that should be reported in USD. 

Additional scoping is required as Method reporting is currency agnostic.

Title: Reorder Improvement - Re-order - Approved Inventory Display for First-Time Orders
Description:
Summary:
Currently, the reorder process in Method only allows customers to view and purchase items they have previously ordered. This limits sales opportunities for new or alternative products and prevents customers from discovering approved items they haven’t purchased before.

Proposed Solution:

Add the ability for admins to define a list of approved inventory items that will always be visible in the customer portal, regardless of prior purchase history.

Maintain current reorder functionality for previously purchased items, while expanding product visibility to these approved items.

Optionally allow filtering, tagging, or highlighting of approved items in the portal for easier discovery.

Impact:
Enables upselling and cross-selling to existing customers, improves product visibility, and supports the introduction of new or seasonal inventory without requiring a prior purchase.
Solution:
This is certainly customizable. 

We would likely need to curate a list of items available for sale / order via the portal. 
This could be done on the items app with a checkbox. 

Then serve that up to the end users.

Title: Shopify - Lead Conversion - New Converted Customer report
Description:
Summary:
Enable a reconciliation workflow that aligns Shopify/QuickBooks customer records with MethodCRM leads, ensuring a seamless lifecycle from lead capture in Method through order fulfillment in Shopify/QuickBooks.

Problem Statement:

Customers using Shopify + QuickBooks + Method encounter duplicate or fragmented records.

Leads originate in Method long before first purchase.

First purchases are completed in Shopify, which automatically creates a customer in QuickBooks.

When the lead is later converted in Method, a new QuickBooks customer may be created, causing duplicates and breaking continuity of the customer history.
Solution:
Yes, we can certainly help with merging information automatically and track success.

There is a condition to ensure there is some matching data. 
So if we have a unique identifier (eg. email / phone) that can be trusted.

We can merge and then create conversion reports.

Title: Estimate Improvement  - Ability to Calculate Linear Feet on Estimate
Description:
Today, users who price work by linear feet need to:
Measure the length on-site
Manually calculate total linear feet (sometimes room by room)
Enter a flat quantity on an Estimate line item
Recalculate and update the quantity every time the estimate changes

This creates unnecessary friction and introduces errors, especially in trades like painting, flooring, carpentry, fencing, and HVAC/electrical where linear feet is the primary unit of measure.
Solution:
A. Line Item-Level Configuration
Add support for a “Linear Feet” measurement mode on Estimate line items.
When enabled, the line item exposes:
One or more “length” inputs (numeric fields, in feet/meters depending on company setting)
A calculated “Total Linear Feet” field (read-only)
A Rate per Linear Foot field
A Quantity field that is auto-set to “Total Linear Feet”

B. Calculation Behavior
Total Linear Feet = sum of entered length segments
Example: (Wall 1 + Wall 2 + Wall 3 + Wall 4)
Line item Amount = Total Linear Feet × Rate per Linear Foot
Quantity on the line item = Total Linear Feet (for consistency with QuickBooks/Items mapping)

Title: QBO Sync Improvement - Support to Sync Sales Order Transactions
Description:
We use Sales Orders in QBO to track orders.

We would like this information available in Method.
Solution: The sales order module is in (Beta) in QBO and is not available via the API yet (November 2025).

Title: App Routine -  Automated One Year Reminder
Description: Can we currently set an app routine, for example based off of last invoice date, put in a validation to say if last invoice is a year old to send a re-engagement(Email Template) email ?
Solution:
1. Build a function that loops through customers then retrieves last invoice date (1-2 hours per transaction)
2. Create a new Email template and default preference in order to utilize it for the above app routine (30 minutes)
3. Testing and iterating based on user feedback (1 hour)

This shouldn't take too long, maybe 2-4 hours. We should discuss details with the client.

Title: Estimate Approvals
Description: Estimate Approval is a way for customers to approve or reject their estimates within the customer portal. This consists of status fields and approve and reject buttons. By approving or rejecting the estimate, a decision email will be sent to the sales rep the estimate is assigned to. If there is no sales rep assigned to the estimate, the email will be sent to the admin user. Then, a completed activity will be created within Method where the comment field will include the updated status. Lastly, the status fields on the estimate will be populated within Method. Lastly, a rejection reason must be entered into the feedback box in order to 'Confirm Reject'.
Solution: Solution Available in the Customization Catalog

Title: Deposit from Estimate Approval
Description:
They would like to be able to send an estimate to their customers, and have their customers approve the estimate in their portal. 

Once the estimate approved, they would like to redirect the customer to a screen where they can make a deposit on that estimate. 

Thanks!
Solution:
This workflow has many solutions depending on accounting considerations regarding recognition of revenue. The recommendation below may take less than 5hrs but portal & payment testing is very important and more time consuming than your average customization.


An easy solution would be to customize the Guest - Payment screen (or Sales Receipt screen) to create & apply the deposit amount to a Sales Receipt.

This amount could then be automatically inserted into the Estimate as a discount line item. 

This way when the estimate is converted, the invoice will only have the appropriate balance remaining and reflect the deposit as well.

Guest - Payment screen
- Do not insert payment, instead insert a sales receipt
- Upon confirmation that payment is processed, insert discount into estimate

ESTIMATE DOES NOT INCLUDE TIME FOR ANY APPROVAL PROCESS RELATED CUSTOMIZATIONS

Title: Automate Transaction Follow-ups
Description:
Follow-up 3 days after an invoice is created.
Follow-up 7 days after an estimate is sent.
Solution: We can add an approutine to run on a daily basis for invoices that are coming from QB to create follow up activity. Probably 2- 3 hours if no additional criteria is needed.

Title: Automate Proposals from Web to Lead (W2L) - Creation
Description:
2 proposals Options: Monthly and Annual
(Digital marketing firm)

- Created through web to lead
-be able to capture contact details within contact app
- ability process payment and store credit card information for recurring charge based off their selection
- proposal it self are simple, recurring on either monthly basis or annual
- not a huge criteria on engagement letters, just simple notification email to customer on confirmation of subscription.
Solution:
This sounds like recurring invoices based on subscribption end/renewal date. Pretty simple on paper but may require a decent amount of work depending on the details as well as any lists/reports they may want. (2.5 to 5 hours)

The recurring invoice piece will require an app routine to help automate this. The payment piece can be handled via regular gateways or maybe even the new Payment interaction assuming they intend on using only have 1 gateway. (2 to 3 hours)

The notification email will be a basic email template we create based on their requirements and maybe even a PDF report (0.5 to 3 hours).

Title: Custom Permissions - The ability to create a transaction but not edit them
Description:
Main goal:
- Non-admin users, can create new invoices and 
- Non-admin users can access past invoices without having the ability to edit them after a certain date.
Solution:
This will require a custom permission setup that we can keep track of on the User Level, however, if they want to be able to manage this themselves in the future then we can create a custom app to help do so.

The custom permissions will drive this requested functionality.

Title: Web to Lead (W2L) - Customization to Process Payment on Web to Lead
Description:
1.) Have the customer make a selection on a form and show a price. In this case a course. 

2.) Once the payment is accepted and processed.

3.) Log everything into the CRM about the course and payment.
Solution:
Assuming the workflow is: 
- Potential/Existing customer completes web to lead form on which they are given the option to select items to purchase

Customizations to add:
- Validate that if it's an existing customer, balance is already 0, otherwise direct them to portal to pay existing balance
- Create invoice based on customers item selection from web to lead for and redirect them to the invoice in the customer portal to make payment

Relatively straight forward, should be somewhere in the 3-4 range

Title: Email a report of all Transactions / Donations entered from QuickBooks.
Description:
Currently, their donations and transactions are created in QuickBooks. 

She was wondering if an email can automatically be sent about once a day (or possible changed to once a week), that has a list of all of the donations entered in Quickbooks (and synced to Method:CRM) since the last email was sent.
Solution:
We can definitely do this but would need to define the business  rules quite thoroughly since not all Sales Receipts are Donations. Keep in mind that if any data relevant to the business rules change, that would require additional customization to ensure their emails are being correctly sent out as expected.

Rough estimate of about 5-10 hours.

Title: Proposals - The ability to reject a proposal with a suggestion / feedback
Description: The ability to reject with admentment and notify the user to edit and resend the proposal.
Solution: This is something PS is familiar with through the Estimate Approval customization catalogue. We would need to display the 'Cancel' button for the signature widget and a 'Rejection Reason' text object to capture the reason then display this in the View Proposal screen

Title: Ability to do calculations / build calculators
Description:
Customer has a number of excel sheets they use to calculate rental prices / loan amounts.

Ideally this would work into the overall Purchase Calculation on Invoices.
Solution:
Most calculations are small to medium-sized job with custom math calculations. 
Anything in excel can certainly be transposed into Method.

That said, there is more to calculations than just a calculator.
Reporting and App design might be required to define the entire workflow from end to end. 

For example, before we can quote a customer, we need to make sure they meet a certain criteria based on the calculator.

Title: Ability to provide better organization of Activities (folders, groupings)
Description:
Customer has lots of activities/opportunities stages that go into a sale. i.e permit send, received, approval, denied.

They were wondering if its possible to customize a folder system in the activities ribbon where activities can be grouped into Permits for example.
Solution:
This can be done by creating a custom app based on the Activities table, then setting up a custom app ribbon to filter for those specific Permit Activities. That shouldn't take longer than 30 minutes.

However, it may involve some additional work, e.g. creating a new field or Activity Type then mass updating all relevant Activities. This may take several hours (3-6 hours) depending on what Activities need to be updated and how to locate them.

Title: Automatically update rate based on when time tracking entries are logged
Description:
Customer is a dispatching user and would like the ability to alter the rate of a service item, or the actual service item itself based on their system time for the purpose of upcharging after hours.

For example, if a technician goes to a site at 11pm and starts a time tracking entry the billable rate here should be 1.5x the default hourly rate.
Solution:
I'm assuming the user themselves will be creating/editing the Work Order/Txn during afterhours work, is this safe to assume?

If so, we can implement logic to check the current date/time. If the current time is let's say past 9pm then a new after hours rate will be used for all items. This is something we should probably setup as a Preference on the Work Orders app. One preference for After hours time threshold, i.e. 9pm, and one preference for the After hours rate, i.e. 1.5x normal item rates.

The customer may want to have custom rates for each item which is also something we can discuss. If we're talking about implementing this on one app, i.e. Work Orders, then it shouldn't take longer than 4 hours assuming there aren't any additional details we're missing.

Additionally, we would need to test the time comparison/threshold works in Method as we would expect it to.

Title: Reminder on Field Crew to Submit Time to Work Order
Description:
Is it possible to customize a popup reminder on the Field crew app to manually added a start and end time if job is completed without the start timer.

Ideally, we never want a work order to be completed unless there is at least one time tracking entry associated with it.
Solution:
This can be done via customization fairly quickly but within the confines of a Method screen, meaning no app notifications on the device itself. It will basically be one of our show message modals. For example go click the Send Email button on a transaction screen. The user gets to preview the email before clicking Okay to send it, that's the using our standard show message modal.

We should probably discuss details and thresholds with the client first. For example, what if the start and end timer difference is only 1 minute. Or what if it's days apart? If there's an internal reconciliation process at their organization now then that will catch these types of scenarios. If not, I would recommend some form of manual reconciliation on their end.

Title: Customization - YesNo / True False / Changing the way the checkbox value is displayed
Description:
On  a Grid, when displaying a checkbox - it appears as either TRUE or FALSE.
Can we change that to reflect different options like Yes or No, or show the physical checkbox.
Solution:
This cannot be customized and cannot be done.

I've linked a feature request here to +1.

Title: Provide a full list of contacts but limit their ability to view the contact based on permission
Description:
My customer has a very agressive sales team.
Lead poaching and stealing is very likely (reminds me of the CSM Team) :)

They want the ability to search the contact list and see all customers & leads; however, we want to restrict their ability to View Contact or Expand a Contact.

This is important because, the view contact page will be customized to contain sensitive information about a deal that only the assigned sales rep should have access to.
Solution:
This customization should be simple.
1.) Peer to Peer permissions should be set.
2.) We will then remove any peer to pee permissions on the contacts list
3.) We will restrict what information is visible on the contacts list
4.) Lastly, on View Contact, before the page loads, we need to validate if the user has access to that contact. If they do not send them to the page they came from.

Title: Updating Amount field on Opportunities based on Estimate Link
Description:
Customer asks 'Can we get the 'Amount' field in Opportunities to update any time we adjust the amount in the Estimate that is attached to it? Right now we are forced to manually go in and edit the amount field in the Opportunity.'

Is this possible with customization?
Solution:
Yes this can be easily done.

- Note that an opportunity can be linked to multiple estimates.
- If the estimate is updated does it update the opportunity?
- If the opportunity is updated how do we know what to update on the estimate.

Those are some key questions i'd ask.

Title: Ability to create 'in-between' filter for postal codes.
Description:
Customer is looking to use the Email campaign for mass emailing.

They are looking for a way to filter a range of postal codes.
Solution:
1.) Zip codes are currently stored as text 
Eg. How can you get every customer between A and C 
- Difficult to understand programmatically, we'd need to identify how this could be done.

2.) Being able to get a list of zip codes within a should be possible; however testing will be required.

Marking as Small as this will likely require trial and error.

Title: Ability to edit a transaction email
Description: Customer would like the abilty to edit  the estimate notification email in the estimate app.
Solution:
Shane the scope of this one can vary from very small to very large.

We currently have a catalog in testing that might be able to accomodate the core needs requested by the customer.

Title: Pull numerical text within a string
Description:
Customer is asking : How do I find a number inside a string?

Currently within their Activity comments field, they have 'vehicle count' added as text (screenshots attached)

they want to somehow pull that number and store it somewhere

Is this possible? any easy way to pull this value?
Solution:
Pulling a number in a string is possible; however, it does require some trial and error.

We would need to first find the first occurrence of t a number (0-9) within a string.

Then use a combination of character functions including (Left, Mid,Right, and Length) to identify the end of the number.

Title: building Zapier Integration for WO SMS
Description:
customer would like to have automatic SMS message send to customer when Fc is on route and after Work Order completion, for review.

Any idea on minimum monthly expenditure for zapier+SMS would be greatly appreciated as well.
Solution:
Note that SMS functionality is in the works within the platform. 

 Yes, I've done this with RingCentral. When a payment is made through the portal, a set of users are notified via text of the amount paid by the customer. The zap is triggered by an activity of a certain type (custom type I created for just this purpose), which is created when a customer pays on the portal.

For Zapier, they will at least need the Professional pack (49 USD monthly) because we need 'custom logic with paths' to separate Activities based on activity types as triggers for our zap.

For RingCentral, the Core pack might work ($30) because all we need is the SMS feature.
Other than that, we might take 2-4 hours to get this done.

Title: Excel Activities Report Export
Description:
Customer would like the ability to customize the activity report excel export function.
The customer uses activities reports as a client interaction summary and prefer excel over PDF as it offers their controller the ability to edit before sending.

If we are able to customize the excel export to be 1) one file per contact or 2) one tab per contact, this would greatly improved their end of month processes.
Solution:
1.) Customizing a customer per tab is not an option
2.) Exporting an excel file is certainly possible in stock via grid export.
 - The stock export tool can also be used with filtering to export activities of a certain type or within a date range.


For example - Activity Type - Month-End Invoice
- You can filter for this and export this for use as excel / csv

Title: The ability to have a Work Order Linked to an Invoice / Invoice Linked to a Work Order
Description:
It's often difficult to find what invoice relates to what work order.
In some cases, since people can change the InvoiceStatus on a work order, some work orders never get invoiced. 

Ideally, i would love to see what invoice the work order is on.
and vice versa what work orders are associated with what invoices.

Today it's just too many clicks if i start from the invoice or work order app.
Solution:
Pretty Straight forward customization.

Work Order = 1 Field for Invoice Reference
Invoice, we probably would just create a simple grid, which is filtered for all work orders with the current screen active recordID

Title: Ability to Assign Work Orders to Rooms vs Users
Description:
Customer runs a recording studio and at the moment do not use any management tool for booking.

They are interested in Method as a CRM but was wondering if room booking can be centralized.

Although they will be a multi-user workflow, they would like the WO calendar resources to be based on a  custom 'Room' Table.

These would ideally sync with Google Calendar, showing which room a specific booking is for.
Solution:
Use users for rooms.

The same concept is applied for thing like trucks and other resources that 'group' people into teams or a location.

If we need to keep track of room (primary)
- and participants (users in the room) we could explore that 

However, just the basic requirement here, create rooms as users.

Title: Ability making the cursor  default to the 'activity type' box.
Description: Customer is wondering if we can make the cursor have the ability to  default to the 'activity type' box instead of the comments box as it does now.
Solution:
I believe this is simple, FYI anyone can customize it via actions:
https://help.method.me/en/articles/2623136-set-focus-to-control-action

I would probably set on screen focus.

Title: Send Email / Approval Button - For Internal User (vs. Customer which is Stock)
Description:
How can I notify someone on my team of a receipt or invoice.

The reason for this email is a matter of approval / just reviewing and awareness.
Solution:
This is a relatively small project (1-2) Hours per screen / button. 

1.) Create an email button
2.) Create an email template
3.) Allow the customer to select a 'user' to email
4.) If it's always the same user we can pull from the template
5.) We can likely log an activity for a paper trail
6.) On the email generate a URL that links to the invoice / receipt in question.

Title: Ability to select date of monthly donation occurence.
Description: Customer would like to know if it is possible to add a date option to the monthly donation option, so that donors can choose the date the transaction will for the amount to be withdrawn.
Solution: Depending on the customers requirements we may need to customize the entire workflow OR just parts of it. So this is most likely a small customization request. We can give access to the donor so they select when this recur's, for example, every 15th of the month. Then we'll modify how the app routine uses this information for the scheduled recurring donations. AND if need be we can also provide access to internal CRM users to select the recurring date of each donation.

Title: Proposals -  When a client has last viewed a proposal
Description: Customer would like to know if they can have a functionality to see , when a client views a contract sent over for signing.
Solution:
Very possible, under an hour.

Create a field to store last viewed date, update it when ever the proposal is viewed on the portal.

Title: Multi-Tenancy - Allow the Main Tenant to control Transactions for all Tenants
Description:
Customer is  looking to have the main account used to manage both QB accounts, basically looking for the main Method account to have the ability to send jobs and invoices for both accounts Also the main Method account should send their NC jobs/invoices to our NC QuickBooks and our SC location invoices go to our SC QuickBooks.

They are multi tenancy account and have the customization done earlier in the main account.
Solution:
Multi-Tenancy customizations all have a high difficulty floor.

Customizing something of this nature is certainly possible within the new or classic platform that said it has to be reviewed with a consultant.

If the goal is to do it for 'cheap' this is not the customization to complete it.

Considerations include:
1.) How app ribbons work
2.) How duplicate / similar customers are handled
3.) How are payments processed 
4.) How are portals used
5.) Do different tenants leverage different brands of templates.

Title: Ability to mass update itemline  for past invoices.
Description: Customer would like to update line items for past invoices for all active customers. Does not want to manually going reselect items to reflect new pricing.
Solution: This may be an extra small project however, we should try to avoid updating transactions en masse as this may impact the clients books in negative ways. Recommend the client to first speak with their account for guidance before updating anything en masse.

Title: Separating Lead and Customers on Reports
Description:
User ulitizes a reportto track customer leads by status.

This report currently includes customers alongside customer leads.

Based on previous conversations with our team, the user is under the impression there was nothing we could do to only show prospects and hide the customers on this report based on how it is built.  

They would like to build a report that is exactly like the existing 'customer lead by status count' but does not show customers.  Only open, qualified and unqualified prospects.
Solution:
This seems like a very niche request pertaining to an already customized report. It could be as simple as filtering the report for only Customer Leads but that depends on the data in the account and how the report is built.

If we need to rebuild the report or any of the data then that will take much longer. Will need to be scoped out in detail before a rough estimate can be provided.

Title: Automatic changes in Invoice if changes are made in Estimate even after it is converted from an Estimate to Invoice
Description: Customer wants to know if we convert an Estimate to an Invoice and make changes to the Estimates later, Is it possible to get the changes on the Invoice as well? Is it possible with customization?
Solution: Yes this is possible but we should scope this workflow out a bit more. There's a better way to do it and it may not require customization. For example, why are Invoices being created at all if it's possible that the Estimate will be updated. Why not wait until the Estimate is finalized before creating the Invoice?

Title: Ability to Automatically Assign A User To Invoices Created in QuickBooks
Description: Customer would like to know if it is possible to have a user automatically assigned to an invoice synced from QuickBooks rather than manually assign a user.
Solution:
Could be done on a daily basis via a scheduled app routine.

If the invoice has a blank assignedto, retrieve from the customer.
If customer doesn't have an assignedto, pick someone from a preference for default.

Title: Ability to Link and report Time Tracking Entries to Proposals
Description:
Customer is a bookkeeping workflow and would like to utilize our Proposal app.

They are looking for a way to link time tracking to a proposals as some of their clients have multiple engagement at the same time.

Ultimately, they would like a report to compare proposals allotted hours with  timetracking entries linked to that proposal
Solution:
This should be pretty straight forward,

The workflow would be to add a proposal ID to the time tracking screen.
Then on the proposal show a list of all the time associated with that proposal and summarize it in a grid.

Should be a quick turn around.

Title: Ability to keep track of both Sales Rep & who created a transaction.
Description:
The sales rep field currently defaults to the Sales rep linked to the customer account.

Our user would like the ability to default the sales rep to the user who is creating the invoice.

eg. Shane is the CSM, I always want him to be listed on the Invoice.
Nelson is the PS Person, I need to keep track that nelson created the invoice but he's not the rep in charge of managing the account.
Solution:
A custom field can be used to manage this information.

Depending on the relationship of the non-sales rep, this information could also be associated with the customer / account or just simply the current person signed in creating the transaction.

Title: Customization changes on tenant account
Description:
They have a customization on sales order app on the head account(datamemorysales) which can also be seen in the tenant account(DMM21 and DMS2021 ).

They have an email warehouse button which is sending email to shipping@greenlightingwholesale.com and user wants to send email to shipping@data-memory.com for those two tenants accounts.

Just wanted to confirm if we update the email addresses in the tenant accounts does it affect the main account?
Solution:
Yes,

With multi-tenancy by default changing an action will have an impact on all tenants. 
Unless there is a conditional statement that seperates how the action behaves based on each tenant. 

Please keep in mind to make sure the customer tests all accounts thoroughly. 

Multi-tenancy customization should be avoided by anyone not on the Professional services team to ensure no other workflows are disrupted. No matter how difficult the customer may be. 

PS Team, this should be customized as a preference, vs. hardcoded into an action.

Title: Ability to have the calendar view display up to 3 months.
Description: Customer would like to know if it is possible to have the work order calendar display up to 3 months at a time. Also would like entries in calendar to show the user assigned to work order without clicking on it.
Solution:
Not supported not customizable today, outside of maybe agenda view.
Create a FR, make sure to list the:

Account
The request
The why - Once done, reply here so we can link this request to the FR for future +1s.

Title: Automating Sales Order from Estimate Approval
Description:
User would like the Portal approval button on any estimates to automatically creates a sales order. 

This automation should be accompanied by a Sales order email to the customer
Solution:
Possible. 

1-2 hours of customization.
Feature request would be valuable here to improve how the 'acceptance' flow on Estimate could flow automatically into an order.

Title: Proposals - The ability to add multiple customer recipients
Description: Customer would like to know if they can send proposal as well as the invoice generated for the proposal to second recipient. They are looking to pull  an email for the additional contact of that customer.
Solution:
Easy to customize; however, keep in mind that there is a reason why only 1 contact is on the list.

The moment multiple people are listed, we can no longer control who signs the contract and it could be problematic for understanding who is allowed to sign or not. 

If this is an internal team member, that needs to be CC'd or BCC'd, consider updating the email template.

Please create an FR to provide the use case.

Title: Field Services - Work Order Map Screen - Add Additional Fields
Description: The customer would like to know if it is possible to add the customers email and phone number when on the work order map screen when they have selected their routes.
Solution:
This screen just uses a grid based on the Activity Table.
To show the information it's using an expression column, we should be able to add and remove the expression column as needed.

So yes, we can certainly add more information to the Work Order Map Screen, including Phone and Email associated with the Work Order.

Title: Create a hyperlink or button that navigates to an external page outside of Method
Description: Customer request: We give our customers access to a Method portal. On that portal we display a 'Case' button. I would like to replace that with a link to our current ticketing system. We use Zamad for our ticketing system.
Solution:
Pretty simple customization:

1.) This is the action they would use called Go To Web Page
https://help.method.me/en/articles/2583879-go-to-web-page-action

2.) The user could wire up the URL directly in that action.

That said, more complicated solutions could request customization to build a URL to merge information together, and personalized the link. For example, we use Jira. In Jira every ticket has a number, in theory we use a character function to merge the URL & the Ticket Number, to build a link that takes us directly to the web page we want.

Title: CFR 211 Compliant / Compliance - Pharmaceutical / Drug Manufacturing
Description: 21 CFR Part 211 is a regulation issued by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) that outlines the Current Good Manufacturing Practice (CGMP) requirements for finished pharmaceuticals 12. The regulation specifies the minimum requirements for the methods, facilities, and controls used in the manufacturing, processing, and packaging of a drug product 1. Compliance with 21 CFR Part 211 ensures that a business has a safe, effective, consistent, and quality-managed drug manufacturing operation in place 2.
Solution:
Method at this time is not compliant at this time. 

However, if a customer is currently undergoing an audit for this compliance. The primary software component is the following:

Lastly, as we touched on in the Personnel requirements of 21 CFR 211, robust and repeatable training are indispensable for your cGMP operation.

Training on both internal SOPs and broader cGMP requirements helps put your entire team on the same page, standardizing work and minimizing risk of both drug contamination and worker mistakes and injuries.

Consider simplifying and strengthening your training processes with training management software, ditching manual back-and-forth document-signing with cloud-based centralized training records that you can easily show to an FDA inspector.

Title: Date Picker Improvement - The ability to go back more than 5 years
Description: The customer has a custom workflow in their portal > Contacts App >> Guest - Upload Screen where they have a date of birth field. The year only goes to 2018 and they were wondering if they could have the ability to scroll to any year. As now they are just typing in any year that is beyond 2018.
Solution:
At the moment this is a platform limitation of the date picker.

The customer at this time only has one option, which is to type in the year.
This has been previously documented as a feature request.

Particularly around the Birth Date use case.

Title: Calendar - The ability to customize the tile in List Month & List Week View
Description:
Currently, the customer was able to customize the event and wait list view using expressions. 

However, there doesn't appear to be an easy way to customize the title that comes up in the List Month and List Week View.

Is this something that can be customized?
Solution:
This is something that cannot be customized at this time.
This should be logged as a feature request for our development team.

Title: Payment Gateway - The ability to Import Payment Details (Credit Cards, Bank Information)
Description:
Customer is currently using another CRM / Payment solution.
They have all the customer payment information stored in excel; however, they don't have the ability to bring it into Method.

Is this something they can import?
Solution:
At this time, this cannot be customized or imported into our system.

Our current implementation of shuttle requires all payment information to be keyed in to the widget and saved. 

In order to help facilitate this entry, we could customize a button on the Contact Page, to launch a pre-authorization flow for their selected gateway and manually enter in payment information into the customer profile and use it in the future. 

Customers may see a small charge of $1 in this process as the payment information is validated when saving. However, this charge is immediately refunded.

Title: Limit User Ability to Create Customers or Vendors - Do not allow users to Create Customers or Vendor
Description:
We have 6 users that aren't the smartest people in the world. 
They're creating a ton of customers that are going into QuickBooks, is there a way to force everyone to just create leads and then have them convert on the View Contact page as normal

We do not want users to create customers or vendors.
Solution:
Simple Customization on the New Contact Page:

1.) We should remove the radio button option for Customer & Vendor
2.) Check the references to the radio button in the action list
3.) Make sure that all conditions default to lead.

Title: Payments App - Improve the Internal Process of taking a partial payment
Description:
Customer recieves partial payments often and noticed that fields like payment method and Memo do not save when they click to change the 'amount to pay'  for the purpose of creating a partial payment against an invoice. 

Can we customize this so that the partial payment amount is editable from the view payment screen or even have the 'amount' field automatically update the 'amount to pay' field when the amount field is changed from the invoice total?

There are too many clicks and it's confusing because you can update the Amount to Pay but the amount field on the Receive Payment header doesn't update, sending a user a message that the amount's do not match.
Solution:
I believe this workflow is possible using an editable grid to create a better experience. 

1.) Replace the receive payment transaction table with a Editable Grid.
2.) On lose focus of the Amount field, we should be able to update the header with the total amount that should be paid on the transactions. 
3.) Note that 1-2 saves might be required to test this.

At the time of writing this, no one has reportedly completed this customization - so some testing will be required to verify if there are any conflicts created in the process. That said, this would be a massive usability improvement for anyone who uses the payments app for partial payments.

Title: Contacts App - Limit Users from Editing Fields that Sync with QuickBooks
Description:
The Primary Contact and Alternate contact, as well as the billing address and a few other fieldsare typically used for Accounts Payable. We don't want anyone but accounting staff to be able to change this information via QuickBooks. 

CRM users, can add additional contacts and edit contact information that does not sync with QuickBooks.
Solution:
This is a pretty simple customization depending on the complexity the user requires:

1.) If the contact is the main contact or alternate contact, hide the edit contact button
2.) Always hide the edit entity button on view contact
3.) This is typically done on load by checking the edit permission, we can likely check to see if the user is an 'Admin' based on this criteria lock out their ability to edit anything that is customer related that sync's with QuickBooks.

Title: My Data Policy - EU - European Union My Data Policy
Description:
Currently it does not seem like we have any integration with EU's 'My Data' policy under their Data Act - Is this something we plan to integrate with as we branch out to more European countries? TL;DR - The My Data policy is where a business' invoice to a customer gets filed with the government (their customer files it their own way).

Creating a centralized data source for economic growth across the EU. 

https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_23_3491
Solution:
Depending on the scope of the process that the customer follows, Method could certainly customize a solution to help aid the activity of sharing. That said, it would be interesting to evaluate how this policy and GDPR work together. 

Since the data that is being submitted to the government is the invoice.
Invoices today, are primarily managed by QB, so I would review how QB handles invoice data in like with the EU My Data Policy. This seems like a optional service were information is shared and then distributed.

Title: Donor Improvement - Support for Deposits - QBO Deposits
Description:
Customer wishes to remain on the current workflow they use. Which is collecting & recording donations as deposits inside of QuickBooks. 

They do not wish to change their workflow to sales receipts as it will likely cause a lot of confusion in the organization when generating letters or historical reports.
Solution: At this time, we're currently limited by what's available from the sync API. There is no support for deposits and therefore, this cannot be customized.

Title: Limit Sync History - Bring in only customers from a certain period of time
Description:
We want to continue using QuickBooks Desktop; however, we have over 10 years worth of data. We were only wanting to transition our current customer base into the CRM, not 10 years worth of back log.

Can this be done?
Solution:
When the customer syncs with QuickBooks, they can state how far back they wish transactions to sync. By default this is the last 30 days of transactions or anything open. 

That said, the full customer list will come into the CRM.  Using the transaction history and the customer list, we can determine the last time a customer purchased a product and then filter out that customer from particular views or workflows in order to only show the 'current' customer base.

Remember that this goes beyond the contact list, but also any dropdowns that refer to the contact. One risk is that duplicate information, might not be readily visible to the users (eg. searching for a contact or creating a contact where a duplicate might exist) but if that duplicate was a customer before that filter date, the team will not have any mechanism to find that customer.

Title: Attachments Improvement - Differentiate between documents and photos on the attachments widget
Description:
The customer is looking for a solution where he can easily differentiate between a document and a picture uploaded as an attachment.

They mentioned that 'The attatchments have to be all clicked on to open the photo. customers don't label their photos so it is a pain to go thru them. Is there an option to make icons or something so it is easier to tell which attachment/ photo is which'
Solution:
Implementation time 30 minutes / transaction

A catalog entry is available for this. 
Basically what the catalog does it it summarizes all the documents and shows them in a list, and shows all pictures in a thumbnails.

Title: Outlook Gadget - The ability to view Purchase Orders
Description:
I am using Outlook Desktop app. The Gadget works, but I would like to be able to view Purchase Orders in the gadget, but I don't see that option. 

This can help me with viewing and looking up vendors when I email them
Solution:
A simple customization could be made availabel to support the outlook gadget.

That said some testing is required to make sure this works well. 
It's really just two screens:

1.) PO List
2.) View PO 

Ideally we would keep editing to the core Method App.

Title: Hyperlink / Link - Support to hyperlink URLs automatically in Text Input Objects.
Description: The customer has a text input field on their new/edit work order screen called ' Links' where he pastes links for crew to view. He would like to know if the links he pastes into that field be active rather than plain text so they can directly go to the link without having to select the text, right-click, and opening in a new tab.
Solution:
Customization / Work Around Available
Depending on the type of Field.
The customizer could create the field as an HTML text box. 
With the HTML Text box, he should be able to format text and links. 

Alternatively, they could format the object as a link using this helpcentre article:
https://help.method.me/en/articles/2475003-text-object#settings

However, to have input boxes automatically detect URLs and format this as a link - this is a feature request. 
eg. The Memo Field on Transactions or any simple text input field.

Title: Audit Trail Improvement - Track when changes are made to Portal Navigation - Portal Settings
Description:
This morning, this customer reported that Invoices and Payment lists were available on the portal. At this time, we are unable to determine who and when this change was made.

In particular an admin on the account made this change, it's a pretty serious one; however, given the size of the account we don't know who to approach about this change.

  6 users out of 21 are admin, I have a meeting w this customer today so who do I bring it up to!
Solution:
Cannot be customized.
Portal settings changes should written to some log in the system for scenarios like these.

Especially when accounts could have multiple admins.

Title: Import Improvement - Allow users to define what field to match for when looking for duplicates - Identify Duplicate Emails
Description:
While fullname duplicate catch is great for 'QuickBooks' in reality customers could be importing leads with the same emails. Email might be something customers wish to use to determine if there is a duplicate in the system. 

The customer contacted us to find a way to prevent duplicate leads during import in Method. Summary: The customer is looking for a way to prevent duplicate leads during import in Method. The teammate suggests selecting the Unique Field to Fullname when importing records to check for duplicates.
