Proposed Integration Features
1. Sync Jetbuilt Proposals & Estimates to Method:CRM Opportunities
When a new proposal or estimate is created in Jetbuilt, automatically generate an Opportunity in Method:CRM.
Sync key details such as customer name, estimated revenue, project scope, and estimated close date.
Keep updates in sync between both platforms.

2. Customer Data Sync
Automatically sync clients and contacts between Method:CRM and Jetbuilt to maintain an up-to-date customer database.
Allow businesses to create new customers in either platform and have them reflected in both systems.

3. Convert Won Proposals into Invoices in Method:CRM
When a proposal is marked as 'Approved' in Jetbuilt, create an Invoice in Method:CRM (linked to QuickBooks).

Sync line items, pricing, taxes, and project details to avoid manual data entry.
4. Status Updates & Task Sync
Enable a two-way sync for tasks, notes, and project milestones, ensuring Method users and project teams in Jetbuilt are aligned.
Notify the CRM team in Method when a project reaches critical milestones in Jetbuilt (e.g., “Engineering Complete,” “Installation Started”).

5. Payment & Financial Data Integration
Allow financial teams using Method Payments to track deposits and progress payments within Jetbuilt.
Enable automatic payment reconciliation between Jetbuilt, Method:CRM, and QuickBooks.
Solution: Jetbuilt offers an API that we can explore and integrate with; however, it should be noted that depending on the scope of the integration the work required is quite large.

Title: Investment App Customization & Reporting
Description:
Create a phased investment tracking app within Method to centralize investor data, automate quarterly payouts, and enhance reporting capabilities.


Phase 1 - Data Structure & Customer Setup
Define investment-related custom fields within Contacts and Customers.
Separate static customer data from Quarterly Payouts, which need to be dynamically calculated in the future.

Phase 2 - Investment Data & Calculations
Develop a structured investment data model based on manually entered records.
Support importing investment data (currently tracked in PDFs & spreadsheets).
Automate cash total calculations based on investment types.

Phase 3  - Reporting & Investor Portal
Generate on-demand investment reports per investor request.
Pull relevant data from Transaction Charts for accurate reporting.
Support RSP/TFSA records tracking.
Discuss role permissions with Customer Success Manager to ensure data security.
Solution: This app is certainly possible; however, given the scope of customization this is a large project as it encompasses, internal and external reporting via portal and email communication.

Title: Opportunity Reporting Improvement -  Weekly Opportunity Reports by Rep to Management via Email  - Email Reports
Description:
Description:
We need a weekly automated report summarizing open, won, and lost opportunities by each sales rep, delivered to management via email or dashboard view.

Requirements:

Automated Report Delivery – Scheduled weekly emails with a summary of:

Total open opportunities
New opportunities created
Won/lost opportunities and their values
Follow-ups due this week
Pipeline health (e.g., deal stage breakdown)
Customizable Filters – Ability to adjust report scope by date range, rep, and opportunity stage.

Dashboard View – A live, interactive version within Method:CRM for real-time tracking.

Export Options – Ability to download as CSV/PDF for further analysis.
Solution:
This can certainly be customized, the PDF or CSV format will add complexity. 
Naturally the scope will increase with complexity but this is certainly possible.

We should do our best to provide a live interactive view on the platform that references the same data as the printable report / email.

Title: Contacts App Improvement - Enable Title Field (make Title Visible)
Description:
Currently the Title field is not available when adding new contacts.
Ideally, when creating a new contact we would like our team to capture this information.
Solution:
Very simple customization. 

All you need to do is customize the New Contact Page, and make Title 'Visible'.
All the logic for this field is wired up and no other modifications are required.

Title: Items App Improvement - The ability to batch update price increases
Description:
Customer is looking to announce and update price increases. Every year, in general prices increase from 1-5%. 

We would like the ability to batch update all our prices.
Solution:
This is something that is quite simple:

1.) Add a checkbox to the item grid
2.) Create a text box or radio button to select percentage or fixed amount
3.) Create a button to then calculate the increase:

Original Sales Price * 1.(Percent sale Increase) = New Price 
Original Sales Price + Fixed Sale Increase = New Price

Title: Purchase History Improvement  - Ability to access last sold price on Estimate/Invoice Grid
Description:
Summary
Customers in the wholesale industry often deal with custom pricing and need a quick way to reference past sales data when creating estimates and invoices. Currently, they must manually check past transactions to find the last sold price of an item for a specific customer. This feature request proposes adding the ability to view the last sold price directly in the Estimate/Invoice grid when adding items.

Use Case & Business Impact
Custom Pricing Challenges: Wholesale businesses frequently negotiate pricing based on customer history, purchase volume, or previous agreements.
Inefficient Workflow: Without quick access to past pricing, users must search historical invoices or maintain external records.
Risk of Pricing Errors: Manual checks increase the risk of inconsistent pricing, potentially leading to revenue loss or customer dissatisfaction.
Solution:
Proposed Solution
Display Last Sold Price in Estimate/Invoice Grid:

When adding an item, show the last sold price for that customer as an additional column - for that specific combination of both Invoice & Customer.  Ideally, we should also see review the last time the item was sold - that way we can correctly set a price. 

Data should be pulled from the most recent invoice where the item was sold to that specific customer.

Allow users to click on the last sold price to quickly apply it as the new unit price, saving manual entry time.


Configuration Options:
Enable/disable the feature per user role (e.g., restrict access to pricing history for certain employees).
Allow users to define how far back the system looks for pricing history (e.g., last 6 months, 12 months, etc.).

Title: Item Attachments - Attach Additional Files to Items and Provide Customer Access via PDFs and Customer Portal
Description:
Summary
Customers need the ability to attach additional documents (e.g., spec sheets, instructions, and manuals) to their Items in Method:CRM. These attachments should be accessible when sending out estimates and invoices, either as part of the generated PDF or through a link in the Customer Portal.

Use Case & Business Impact
Many businesses, especially in manufacturing, wholesale, and service-based industries, provide detailed specifications and instructions for their products/services.
Customers often request supporting documents alongside estimates and invoices to better understand the product before purchasing or using the service.
Currently, users manually attach files outside of Method when sending estimates/invoices, leading to inefficiencies and lost productivity.
Automating this process would improve customer experience and reduce back-and-forth communication regarding missing documents.
Solution:
Proposed Solution

Attach Files to Items:
Add the ability to upload multiple attachments (PDF, images, Word documents, etc.) to each Item in Method:CRM.
These files should be stored in Method and linked to the corresponding Item.

Include Attachments in Estimates/Invoices:
Provide an option to automatically attach the Item's files to the estimate/invoice email when sent to the customer.
Alternatively, include a link in the email that allows customers to view/download the attachments.

Customer Portal Access:
When customers view an estimate/invoice in the Customer Portal, provide a downloadable link for each item's attached documents.
Option to enable/disable attachment visibility per item or document.

Customer Portal Access (via Catalog):
Simple option would be to provide a list of all items & supporting documents and have the customer review the documents associated with each item.

Title: Estimate to Invoice Improvement – Handling Both Discounts & Deposits
Description:
Summary:
Currently, users cannot apply both a discount and a deposit within the Estimate screen. The existing workflow requires them to add a discount as a line item, rather than using the Discount(%) field in the total section. This setup causes confusion when presenting estimates to customers.

Use Case:

A business wants to provide a percentage-based discount while also collecting a deposit upfront.
The Discount(%) field in the Total section leads to confusion when combined with deposits, requiring workarounds such as manually adding a discount line item.
This makes the Estimate screen less intuitive and increases the likelihood of errors in final pricing.
Solution:
This is a complicated one as it does involve elements that sync with QuickBooks. 
While the Line item is a good work around; another alternative would be to hide the discounting option all together to not create confusion.

Ideally, we should Allow users to apply both a percentage-based discount (via the Total section) and a deposit within the Estimate screen.

Ensure proper calculations so the deposit is taken after the discount is applied, maintaining an accurate final amount.

Provide a clearer UI distinction between Discounts and Deposits to reduce confusion.

Title: Work Order Calendar Filtering – Exclude Certain Job Types
Description:
Summary:
A customer with a high volume of regular cleaning services would like the ability to filter out specific job types from the Work Order (WO) calendar. Specifically, they want to exclude recurring Work Orders from the daily view, while still displaying them in the weekly and monthly views.

Use Case:

The customer manages a large number of recurring cleaning services, which can clutter the daily calendar view.
They need the flexibility to focus on one-off or high-priority jobs in the daily view while still keeping recurring work orders visible in the broader weekly/monthly planning views.
Solution:
Introduce a filtering option (Maybe a dropdown or Checkbox on Screen) on the Work Order calendar that allows users to exclude specific job types (e.g., recurring work orders) from certain calendar views.

Allow users to configure which job types appear in daily, weekly, and monthly views independently.
Ensure filters are user-specific so different team members can customize their views as needed.

Title: Import History Tracking – Visibility into Imported Data
Description:
Summary:
Currently, there is no way to track the history of imports in the system, making it difficult to verify what data was successfully imported vs. what was not. Users must manually compare system records with the original Excel sheet, which is time-consuming and prone to errors.

Use Case:
Users frequently import leads, contacts, and other records into the system but lack visibility into what was successfully imported and what failed.
If an import encounters errors or skips certain records, users must manually compare the system’s data with their original import file to identify discrepancies.
A built-in Import History Tracking feature would save time and improve data accuracy.

Business Impact:
Saves time by eliminating the need for manual comparisons between system data and Excel sheets.
Reduces errors by providing clear insights into why records may not have been imported.
Enhances data integrity by ensuring users can track and verify all imported records.
Improves efficiency by allowing users to troubleshoot failed imports and reattempt them easily.
Solution:
In theory, this can be customized - however, the best solution would be a product improvement. 

As a best practice we already recommend creating two fields on every import sheet:

1.) Import Date
2.) Imported By

These fields enable use to then track what was imported and by whom.
That said, when you're doing multiple imports in a single day you may want to add an import number as well and track your sheets accordingly.

Title: API Integration - MethodIntegration with Clinicient
Description:
Summary
Requesting an integration between Method:CRM and Clinicient, a leading physical therapy practice management and billing software. This integration would enable businesses to sync patient records, track insurance billing, and streamline scheduling workflows between Clinicient and Method:CRM.

Business Case
Many healthcare and therapy businesses using Clinicient for practice management also require a robust CRM for patient engagement, lead tracking, and retention. Currently, no direct integration exists, requiring manual entry of patient data, appointment details, and billing information between systems. This process is time-consuming, error-prone, and inefficient.

By integrating Clinicient with Method:CRM, businesses can automate data synchronization, improve patient relationship management, and streamline billing and scheduling workflows.

Key Features & Functionalities
1. Patient & Lead Sync
Automatically sync new patients from Clinicient into Method:CRM.
Update patient profiles, treatment plans, and insurance details between platforms.
Maintain a unified view of patients, referring physicians, and treatment progress.
2. Appointment & Scheduling Integration
Sync appointment schedules from Clinicient into Method:CRM calendars.
Send automated reminders via email/SMS for upcoming therapy sessions.
Allow front-office staff to manage cancellations and reschedules from either platform.
3. Billing & Insurance Workflow
Track patient invoices, copayments, and outstanding balances in Method:CRM.
Link insurance claims & reimbursement status between Clinicient and Method.
Generate reports for revenue tracking, outstanding payments, and claim follow-ups.
4. Marketing & Patient Retention Tools
Enable automated follow-up emails & SMS reminders for post-treatment check-ins.
Use Method:CRM’s lead management tools to convert prospects into patients.
Run targeted campaigns for re-engagement, referral programs, and wellness reminders.
5. Workflow Automation & Customization
Automate patient lifecycle tracking—from lead, active treatment, discharge, to follow-up.
Set triggers to notify staff when a patient’s insurance requires renewal or re-authorization.
Use custom Method apps to capture patient intake forms & treatment notes.
Solution:
Clinicient API Access: Check API capabilities for patient data, appointments, and billing sync.

Data Compliance: Ensure HIPAA-compliant integration for secure patient data transfer.

Two-Way Sync: Implement real-time updates for patient records and financial transactions.

Webhooks & Triggers: Enable automated actions based on Clinicient events (e.g., appointment changes).

Title: API Integration - Method and Midway Plus Integration - Aftermarket Automotive Ecommerce
Description:
Summary
We propose integrating MidwayPlus - Sell Direct, a leading aftermarket automotive eCommerce platform, with Method:CRM to streamline order management, customer interactions, and backend workflows for automotive businesses.

Problem Statement
Businesses using MidwayPlus - Sell Direct for aftermarket auto parts eCommerce currently lack a seamless way to manage customer relationships, orders, and post-sale engagement within Method:CRM. Without an integration, they face:

Manual data entry between platforms, increasing errors and inefficiencies
Limited visibility into customer orders, returns, and inquiries in Method:CRM
Disconnected workflows between eCommerce, customer support, and sales teams
Missed opportunities for upselling, order follow-ups, and service reminders
Solution:
Proposed Solution
Develop a native integration between Method:CRM and MidwayPlus - Sell Direct to enable seamless eCommerce data flow and workflow automation. Key features could include:

✅ Order Sync – Automatically pull customer orders from MidwayPlus into Method:CRM for better tracking and follow-ups.
✅ Customer Data Sync – Ensure customer profiles, purchase history, and contact details stay updated in both systems.
✅ Automated Follow-Ups – Trigger emails, SMS, or CRM tasks based on order status (e.g., abandoned carts, order confirmations, shipping updates).
✅ Inventory & Pricing Visibility – Sync product details, pricing, and stock levels between platforms.
✅ Returns & Support Automation – Log return requests, issue refunds, or escalate tickets in Method:CRM for streamlined post-sale support.
✅ Integration with QuickBooks – Ensure financial data, invoices, and payments stay aligned for accurate accounting.

Business Impact
Saves time by eliminating double data entry
Improves customer experience with proactive order updates and support
Boosts sales by enabling automated follow-ups and upselling opportunities
Enhances operational efficiency by connecting eCommerce with CRM and accounting

Next Steps
Identify API endpoints from MidwayPlus - Sell Direct for order and customer data
Define data mapping between MidwayPlus and Method:CRM
Gather feedback from automotive eCommerce businesses to refine integration needs
Develop a phased implementation plan

Title: Sales Order Reporting - Sales Orders at Cost by Rep
Description:
Enable reporting in Method:CRM that allows users to pull sales order data from QuickBooks, including cost information, segmented by sales rep.

Business Case:
Currently, sales reports focus primarily on revenue but lack visibility into costs at the sales order level. Adding this reporting capability would allow businesses to:

Measure sales profitability per rep.
Understand cost trends and improve pricing strategies.
Enhance commission calculations based on profit margins.
Improve forecasting and inventory planning based on sales at cost.
Solution:
Possible, should consider some reporting on Margins or Profit as well. 


Technical Considerations:

QuickBooks stores cost data at the item level, so this will need to be pulled from the Item List or Purchase History.
Sales orders don’t inherently store cost values, so integration will need to map items to their respective costs.
Cost values should update dynamically based on FIFO, LIFO, or average cost, depending on the QuickBooks settings.

Title: Improve SMS Experience - Standardize Phone Number Formats across the platform (E 164)
Description:
Current SMS Integration Challenges:

Our current SMS platform is not optimized, presenting the following issues:

Phone Numbers in QuickBooks: Customers must first migrate all phone numbers stored in the Phone Number field of QuickBooks to the Mobile Number field, as SMS pulls data only from the Mobile field.
This mean SMS will always trigger an error out of the box. 

Incorrect Number Format: Once migrated, many of these phone numbers will not be in the correct format for SMS (E.164 format).

Suggested Solutions:

Automated E.164 Formatting: Implement a customization to automatically format all phone numbers to the E.164 standard, ensuring numbers are always SMS-compatible without requiring manual reformatting.

Flexible Mobile Number Formats: Customize the platform to be more accepting of various phone number formats in the Mobile Number field. This would allow greater flexibility for users, and the system would handle any necessary conversions during the SMS sending process.
Solution:
In theory, we can potentially customize this; however it's complicated. 

1.) First replace all special characters including +, (, ),- etc.
2.) Validate that the resulting phone number is numeric (this ensures that the number is complete)
3.) Depending on the region the customer is from make sure that the minimum number of digits is met: eg. 10 if they're in NA.
4.) If the number is less than 10 but 9, add a preceding nine

Customization can definitely help to correct this and enforce number formatting on the platform.

An alternative might be to just export, recordID on the contact and the phone number, drop it into an AI tool and have it try to format the phone number correctly. 

The mass import the updates to the system.

It's important to note that QuickBooks, allows for anything to be entered into the Phone Number field, so managing this will need to be a regular requirement - but we can certainly do better to inform the user the incorrect format is used.

Title: Real-Time Collaboration & Change Notifications for Customization Edits
Description:
Summary
Enable real-time collaboration tracking and change notifications within Method:CRM to prevent multiple users from unknowingly overlapping or conflicting with each other's customizations.

Use Case
Currently, multiple users can edit screens, workflows, and customizations simultaneously without visibility into ongoing changes. This can lead to:

Overlapping edits, where one user unknowingly overrides another's work.
Loss of modifications, where previous changes are unintentionally undone.
Lack of transparency, making it difficult to track who made what change and when.
This has resulted in issues such as missing 'pay your invoice' options, duplicate emails, and confusion regarding past customizations.

Proposed Solution

Real-Time Editing Awareness

Display an 'Active Editors' indicator when multiple users are working on the same screen or workflow.
Lock specific fields or elements when they are being modified by another user.
Change Notification System

Alert users when another teammate makes changes to a customization they recently edited.
Send email or in-app notifications summarizing changes made by others.
Version Tracking & Undo Functionality

Implement an 'Edit History' log showing which user made modifications and when.
Allow users to roll back to previous versions if unintended changes occur.

Title: App Dashboard Improvement - Enhanced App Categorization and Organization on Dashboard
Description: Customers would like more flexibility in organizing apps on the dashboard, similar to how apps can be arranged on a mobile device. Specifically, they want the ability to categorize apps into groups (e.g., Accounting, Custom Apps, Seasonal Use) and navigate through multiple app screens. This would improve efficiency, especially for users with many custom apps, by making frequently used apps more accessible while keeping others organized for occasional use.
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: Localization - Japanese Language Support
Description: The Evolgear account is for a company branch that operates in the USA, but the company's head office is in Japan. Several users from the Japanese office were interested in viewing information on the US Method account, but encountered a language barrier.
Solution:
In general there are a couple of approaches; however, since Japanese is a completely different character set (alphabet) successfully implementing this will be difficult. 

At a high level, we could create duplicate versions of apps in different languages and captions that share the same data. 

However, the way customer names are entered and how they're formatted may be a problem.

Title: Transaction Improvement - Option to Disable Auto-Save on Transactions
Description:
Summary:
Customers want the ability to turn off auto-save for invoices and estimates, requiring users to manually click ‘Save’ before a transaction is stored. This prevents incomplete or accidental entries from being saved automatically.

Problem Statement:
Currently, invoices and estimates auto-save as soon as a user selects a customer or makes an edit. This can lead to a list of incomplete, unintended invoices or estimates when users get interrupted and leave the screen before finishing. The customer wants to ensure that only intentional, completed transactions are saved, preventing clutter and confusion in their transaction lists.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a setting that allows businesses to disable auto-save on transactions and require a manual ‘Save’ action. Possible approaches:

Global Setting in Preferences – Admins can enable/disable auto-save for all users.
Per-User Customization – Allow each user to decide if they prefer auto-save or manual save.
Warning for Unsaved Changes – If a user attempts to leave without saving, prompt them to confirm before exiting.
Key Functional Requirements:

Toggle to disable auto-save on invoices and estimates.
Require users to manually click ‘Save’ to commit changes.
Option to warn users before leaving a transaction with unsaved changes.
Ensure the system doesn’t create incomplete transactions in the background.
Solution:
This is potentially possible; however, it's important to know that child tables may require a save to happen in order to preserve changes as this will need to be tested.

For example, in order to add an invoice line item, we may be required to save the Invoice header.

This is an important workflow.

Title: Grid / Editable Grid Improvement -  Enable Middle Mouse Click to Open Records in a New Tab
Description:
Summary
Enable the ability to use middle mouse click (mouse wheel click) on records in Method CRM grids to open them in a new browser tab. This would improve user efficiency and navigation across multiple records.

Problem Statement
Currently, when users attempt to middle-click a record in a grid, it does not open in a new tab as expected. Instead, they must manually right-click > open in a new tab or use workarounds such as creating MIURL-based customizations. This slows down workflows, particularly when working with multiple records at once.

Proposed Solution
Implement standard browser behavior for middle-clicking on links within grids to open records in a new tab.
Ensure compatibility across major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari).
If direct changes are not possible, introduce an “Open in New Tab” action in the record options.
User Impact
Faster navigation between records.
Improved multitasking efficiency for power users.
Reduced need for custom workarounds.

Title: Stock App Improvement - Ability to Customize Stock App Icons
Description:
Summary:
Customers want the ability to change certain stock app icons within the platform to better align with their preferences or business needs. One specific example is the Purchase Order icon, which a customer feels does not suit their liking.

Problem Statement:
Currently, stock app icons are fixed, meaning users must use the default iconography regardless of their personal or business preferences. Some customers find certain icons unclear, inconsistent with their branding, or not representative of their workflow. The inability to update these icons limits customization and usability.
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: Work Order App Improvement - Submit Work Orders via Portal Page
Description:
We would like portal users to create and submit work orders via the portal page. 

As part of this we could also share the attachment widget capacity to show the customer attachments linked to their work order.
Solution:
This is customizable for work orders.

-----

We could also recommend using the stock Cases app for submissions, then issue work orders associated with cases.

This allows internal teams to review and validate requests before dispatching a tech. For some issues multiple site visits / work orders may be required to resolve the issue.

Title: Payment Gateway Support for Cryptocurrency Payments
Description:
Summary:
Customers are inquiring about the possibility of accepting cryptocurrency payments through Method or any of our supported payment gateways. This feature would enable businesses to offer crypto as a payment option, expanding flexibility and accommodating a growing demand for digital currencies.

Problem Statement:
Currently, Method’s payment processing relies on traditional gateways that primarily support credit cards, ACH, and other fiat transactions. As cryptocurrency adoption grows, businesses—especially those with international customers or operating in industries where crypto is commonly used—seek alternative payment options. The lack of crypto support may limit payment flexibility and create additional conversion steps for customers who prefer using digital currencies.

Proposed Solution:
Investigate the feasibility of integrating cryptocurrency payments via:

Existing Payment Gateways – Determine if any of Method’s current payment partners (e.g., Authorize.net, Stripe, Shuttle Global) support or plan to support crypto payments.
New Crypto Payment Processors – Explore partnerships with crypto payment providers such as Coinbase Commerce, BitPay, or OpenNode to enable seamless crypto transactions.
Direct Crypto Wallet Integration – Consider allowing businesses to connect their own crypto wallets (e.g., MetaMask, Ledger, Coinbase) to accept payments in supported cryptocurrencies.
Key Functional Requirements:

Support for major cryptocurrencies (e.g., Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDC, Solana).
Auto-conversion to fiat (optional) to mitigate volatility risk.
Integration with Method’s existing invoicing and payment workflows.
Security and fraud prevention measures to ensure transaction validity.
Compliance with regulatory and tax reporting requirements.
Benefits:

Expands payment options for customers who prefer digital currencies.
Reduces transaction fees compared to traditional credit card payments.
Enables faster, borderless transactions without currency conversion delays.
Positions Method as an early adopter in an emerging fintech space.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Work Order Enhancement – Customer Scheduling Capabilities
Description:
Summary:
Enhance the Customer Portal work order workflow by allowing customers to 1) request a work order and 2) schedule a work order within available time slots.

Problem Statement:
Currently, customers using the portal can view work orders but do not have the ability to initiate a request or schedule services at their convenience. This limitation results in manual back-and-forth communication between businesses and their clients, leading to inefficiencies in scheduling and service fulfillment.

Proposed Solution:
Enhance the Customer Portal Work Order Workflow to allow customers to:

Request a Work Order – Clients can submit a work order request with relevant details (e.g., service type, location, priority).
Schedule a Work Order – If the business allows, customers can select from available time slots based on predefined availability settings, technician schedules, and job constraints.
Key Functional Requirements:

Work Order Request Submission

Customers can submit a request for service from the portal.
Businesses can set required fields (e.g., service type, urgency, location).
Requests appear in the work order queue for approval.
Self-Scheduling Option

Customers can choose from open time slots based on business-defined availability.
Businesses can control scheduling rules (e.g., lead time, working hours, blackout dates).
Customers receive confirmation and notifications (email/SMS).
Admin Controls & Configuration

Ability to enable/disable customer scheduling.
Define available time slots dynamically based on technician availability.
Optional approval process for requested work orders.
Solution:
Certainly customizable:

1.) Work Order Submission is simple.
2.) There should be an approval process, that finalizes the requested schedule in case appointments need to be moved around.

Title: QBO Sync Improvement - Shipping Date field not available on Estimate table
Description: In QBO, field Shipping Date do not get synced to Estimate table this field is only available on invoice table. Is it possible to make this field available on estimate table as well. Customers want the shipping information entered in these fields in QBO to sync over to Method on Estimates. This is necessary for their workflow and business.
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: Transaction Improvement -  Preventing Duplicate Document Numbers in QuickBooks (e.g., Invoice and Estimate Numbers)
Description:
Customer Concern:
A customer reported issues with duplicate estimate and invoice numbers appearing in either Method or QuickBooks.
Solution:
To resolve this, adjust the 'Custom transaction numbers' setting in QuickBooks Online:

If the setting is ON, turn it OFF to allow QuickBooks to auto-assign numbers.
Perform a sync with Method. This ensures QuickBooks automatically assigns unique numbers to affected transactions.
If duplicates persist, contact the Method team for further assistance.
If the setting is already OFF, QuickBooks alone will control number assignments, preventing duplicates.

Title: API Integration  - Direct Integration with Skylight Calendar
Description:
Summary:
Customers using Skylight Calendar want to sync their Method:CRM Work Orders directly without relying on Google Calendar as an intermediary.

Problem Statement:
Currently, Method:CRM only integrates with Google Calendar, meaning customers using Skylight Calendar must first sync Method Work Orders to Google Calendar and then import them into Skylight. This creates unnecessary complexity and potential sync issues.

Proposed Solution:
Develop a direct integration between Method:CRM and Skylight Calendar to allow seamless syncing of Work Orders, Tasks, and Appointments.
Solution:
Alternative Workarounds (Current State):

Sync Method:CRM with Google Calendar, then import Google Calendar into Skylight.
Use a third-party automation tool (e.g., Zapier) to sync Work Orders between Method and Skylight manually.
Priority Level: Medium-High (dependent on customer demand)

Next Steps:

Assess feasibility with Skylight Calendar’s API.
Determine if a Zapier/Make integration can be a quick solution before a full direct sync.
Gather interest from customers to evaluate demand.

Title: Estimate Improvement - Remove Show More from Item Grid List on Portal
Description:
Issue Summary:
Customers have reported an issue when signing Estimates, where the 'Description' field text is truncated with a 'Show More' button. This truncated view is then captured directly into the signed PDF, rather than displaying the full text.

Current Behavior:

Long Estimate descriptions are truncated in the grid view, showing a 'Show More' button.

When Estimates are signed digitally, the resulting PDF mirrors the truncated grid view, capturing the 'Show More' button rather than the complete description.

Manual expansion ('Show More') is necessary to view or capture the full description, even in customized grid views.

Expected Behavior:

The signed PDF should automatically display the full Estimate description text, irrespective of length, without requiring manual expansion.

The PDF generated upon signing should reflect the complete details clearly and accurately, maintaining full integrity of the agreed-upon content.

Reason for Improvement:

Signed documents must reliably represent the complete and accurate terms agreed upon, without additional actions from the user.

Including a 'Show More' button and truncated descriptions negatively impacts clarity, professionalism, and user confidence in document integrity.

Impact:

Enhancing this behavior will significantly improve document accuracy, clarity, and overall user experience.

It will positively impact portal usage and document handling efficiency, benefiting both customers and internal teams.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Xero Improvement - Add Support for Inventory Management Tables
Description:
Summary:
Customers using Xero for accounting and inventory management want to access and sync inventory-related data within Method:CRM. Currently, Xero's inventory tables are not supported in Method, limiting visibility into product stock levels, costs, and availability.

Use Case:
Customers using Xero for inventory management need real-time access to product stock levels, cost, and availability within Method:CRM.
Sales and operations teams require accurate inventory data to avoid overselling and manage stock efficiently.
Businesses leveraging Xero and Method together want seamless integration of their financial and inventory data to improve workflow automation.

Proposed Solution:
Enhance the Xero integration in Method:CRM by:

Syncing Key Inventory Tables from Xero, including:
Items (Product Name, SKU, Description, Cost, Sales Price)
Stock on Hand (Current Quantity, Available Quantity)
Inventory Adjustments (Stock Movement, Adjustments, Purchase Orders)
Ensuring Bi-Directional Sync (if feasible) so that inventory updates in Xero reflect in Method and vice versa.

Providing Visibility in Method's UI, such as:
Inventory levels appearing on Estimates, Invoices, and Sales Orders.
A dedicated inventory dashboard or view.

Expected Impact:
Reduces errors from manually checking stock in Xero before creating sales transactions in Method.
Improves efficiency for businesses that track inventory through Xero.

Title: Sales Receipt Improvement - E-Signature Workflow for Sales Receipts
Description:
Summary:
Enable an E-signature workflow for Sales Receipts, similar to how it currently functions for Estimates, allowing businesses to collect customer signatures directly on Sales Receipts for authorization, confirmation, or proof of purchase.

Use Case:
Currently, Estimates in Method support an E-signature workflow, allowing customers to digitally sign off on estimates before approval. However, Sales Receipts do not have this functionality, even though businesses often require signatures for sales confirmations, proof of service, or delivery approvals. Adding an E-signature workflow to Sales Receipts would provide:

Legal confirmation of transactions for businesses requiring sign-offs.
Improved customer experience by reducing the need for paper-based signatures.
Better record-keeping with signed receipts stored directly in Method.
Efficiency gains by allowing customers to sign electronically instead of requiring manual signatures or additional paperwork.
Proposed Functionality:

Add an E-signature button to Sales Receipts, similar to Estimates.
Customers can sign electronically via a desktop, tablet, or mobile device.
Once signed, the signature is saved to the Sales Receipt record.
The signed Sales Receipt can be emailed or stored for reference.
Solution:
Yes, this is possible.

1.) Add a signature widget to the Transaction
2.) Make it available on the portal
3.) https://help.method.me/en/articles/5360166-add-a-signature-to-a-transaction-template

Depending on changes to email notifications, this is relatively small customization.

Title: Transaction Improvement - One-Click Shortcut to Access the E-Sign Portal from the Transaction Screen
Description:
Summary:
Customers using our E-sign features in a storefront setting want to minimize the number of clicks required to access the portal. Since most transactions will be accepted on the spot rather than emailed, they need a more streamlined workflow to initiate the signing process directly from the transaction screen.

Problem Statement:
Currently, accessing the portal for signing requires users to go through Email + View Estimate, adding unnecessary steps when the transaction does not need to be emailed. This extra friction slows down in-store transactions and reduces efficiency for businesses that rely on face-to-face interactions.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a 'Sign Now' button on the transaction screen that directly opens the portal for signing, bypassing the email step. Potential implementations include:

Dedicated 'Sign Now' Button

Clicking this button immediately opens the portal with the relevant transaction loaded for signing.
Enable Automatic Portal Redirection (Optional Setting)

A setting that allows users to auto-launch the portal for in-store transactions, skipping the need to email.
Solution:
This is a pretty simple customization.

1.) Create a button on the screen called 'Preview in Portal'
2.) Remove all security requirements (eg. has to signin to view transaction)
3.) On click, open a new tab, to the miurl and have the contact sign.

Title: Ability to Convert a Bill into an Invoice
Description:
Objective: Provide users the ability to seamlessly convert existing Bills into Customer Invoices. This will allow easy tracking and invoicing for subcontracted or vendor-related work, maintaining clear visibility into items billed and ensuring accurate customer invoicing.

Business Case: Our customers frequently subcontract tasks or projects. Currently, these customers manage their subcontracted work via Bills received from vendors. They often need a streamlined way to convert those vendor Bills directly into Invoices for their customers. This feature helps ensure all vendor charges are appropriately and quickly passed through to customer invoicing, preventing missed revenue opportunities and improving workflow efficiency.

Proposed Workflow & Requirements:

Initiating Conversion:

Users select an existing Bill (or Bill line item) to convert directly into an Invoice.
Vendor & Customer Identification:

System identifies the original vendor from the Bill.
System prompts user to select or confirm the associated customer for whom the Invoice will be created.
Invoice Line Details:

Automatically populate Invoice line item details from the original Bill lines, including:
Item/Product details (matching the vendor Bill items).
Quantity, description, and pricing.
Allow editing/adjusting line item details on the Invoice before finalizing, if necessary.
Record Linking & Tracking:

Maintain clear linkage/reference between:
The original vendor Bill and the newly created customer Invoice.
Provide a visible indication or linked reference in both directions (e.g., clickable link or reference IDs on both Bill and Invoice), clearly showing that a Bill has been converted to an Invoice, and vice versa.
Billing & Purchase Order Flow Clarification:

Allow users to indicate whether a Purchase Order step is required before the Bill-to-Invoice conversion or whether the Bill can directly translate into an Invoice.
Clearly define and manage this flow, giving the user flexibility based on their internal processes.
Solution:
Certainly possible to customize. 

It's important to make sure the information on the Bill includes all the detail we would like to see on the invoice.

In some cases, the items and descriptions used on bills (Purchase Transactions) vary from Sales Descriptions (Sales Transactions).  Additional time may be required to ensure taxes are correctly applied in the conversion process.

Title: Import Sales Orders in Method from Excel
Description:
The customer is inquiring about the cost and expected timeframe for importing sales orders into their Method account for one time, and they also want to know how to do it independently.	Importing sales orders and any transactional data is labourious, there are multiple steps required:

1.) All required information must already exist
1a.) Customers, Leads, Terms, Items, all dropdown data must already exist in the CRM

If not all those entries need to be created

2.) Then you need to import a header (Sales Order)
3.) Then you need to import multiple lines per header (Invoice Line)

Again, all required fields including Quatity, Rate, Tax must be included as part of the import. 

If not, we're going to need to design business rules around this. 
This is a large project.

How we'd customize it?
1.) Build a table to match the spreadsheet we want to import
2.) Build a button that processes all the above and makes the appropriate decisions when a certain value isn't found. 

This isn't recommended unless the customer is going to be importing on a regular basis from a source like shopify or their own website for example
Solution:
Alternatives:

1.) Import using QuickBooks
2.) Find another service that support Importing (Transaction Importer)

Title: API Integration - Integration Between Podium and Method
Description:
Summary:
We’d like to request an integration between Podium and Method:CRM to streamline customer communication and improve visibility into interactions across both platforms.

Use Case / Problem:
Many of our customers use Podium to communicate with their clients via text, collect reviews, and manage messaging across different platforms. However, there’s currently no native integration between Podium and Method:CRM. This creates challenges, such as:

Customer messages received in Podium aren’t visible in Method, leading to fragmented communication records.

Duplicate data entry is required when syncing contact or activity info between systems.

No centralized place to manage leads and ongoing conversations.

Proposed Solution:
We’d like a bi-directional integration that allows:

Customer syncing between Method and Podium (new leads or customers created in Method appear in Podium and vice versa).

Message visibility — messages sent/received via Podium are logged on the corresponding Method contact/activity timeline.

Automated triggers — e.g., a new Podium review or incoming text could trigger a follow-up task in Method or create an opportunity.

User Persona:

Benefits / Success Metrics:

Reduced time spent copying data between systems.

Increased lead response times due to centralized visibility.

Improved customer experience through seamless messaging follow-up.

Higher review collection rate via automated prompts.
Solution:
API Integrations are generally larger customizations. 

Please keep in mind the scope and access to API end points.

This would reduce risk, friction, and churn when switching payment processors or during gateway outages.

Manual outreach to re-collect payment info.

Customer frustration and potential churn.

Delays in billing and loss of revenue.

For larger accounts or prepay customers, this creates a significant business disruption and a negative onboarding or support experience.

Proposed Solution:
Implement a gateway-agnostic token storage layer within Method, leveraging Shuttle’s capabilities (or a parallel system), to
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: API Improvement - Miurl Generation, Method Link Generation
Description:
Summary:
Enhance the Method API to support programmatic generation of Miurl.cc deep links and Method record links, allowing users to create shareable, deep-linked URLs to specific records within Method CRM.

Current Challenge:
Currently, users must manually generate Miurl.cc links or construct record-specific URLs. This process is inefficient, especially for automation, bulk actions, or integrations with external applications.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce API endpoints that enable the automatic creation of:

Miurl.cc links – Shortened deep links that direct users to specific records.

Method record links – Full URLs that can be embedded in external applications.
Solution:
In theory, this could be customized:

1.) Create a field on the table to store the url
2.) Create an app routine to generate a miurl for the record 
3.) Save it to the field
4.) Using the API then get the url and use it for other applications.

Title: User Managment Improvement - Username on Invitation, Set username when inviting
Description:
Summary:

Current Challenge:
Today, when inviting a new user, Method automatically assigns their email username (e.g., john.smith from john.smith@email.com).

This can cause confusion or conflicts if multiple users share similar names.

Some organizations prefer standardized usernames that do not rely on email addresses.

Proposed Solution:
Modify the User Invitation flow to include an optional 'Username' field when sending an invite. If left blank, the system will continue using the current default behavior.

Example Workflow:

Admin navigates to 'Invite User' form.

New field: 'Username' (optional, pre-filled with suggested default based on email).

If a custom username is entered, the invited user logs in with that username instead of the system-generated one.

Title: CRM Improvement - Clicking Email Address generates different results

Depending on the location of the email address, we may be able to open the send email app.

The challenge will be when using grids - we cannot control how the platform handles these emails, since they're processed by the web browser. 

In general these links will trigger the default email application on the computer.

Title: API Integration - TrueCommerce / True Commerce
Description:
