Title: Activity Improvement - Work Order Improvement  - Ability to create work orders for custom dates - AI to help select certain days
Description:
Summary:
Users need the ability to create work orders with custom durations that go beyond standard options like 'one week' or 'two weeks.' Specifically, they want to schedule work orders based on a custom number of business days, such as “10 days from now,” while excluding weekends and recognized holidays.

Current Limitation:

The platform does not allow users to specify custom durations like “1 week and 3 days” or “10 business days.”

Scheduling currently lacks flexibility and can’t dynamically account for weekends or holidays.

Proposed Enhancement:

Add a “Custom Duration” option when creating a work order.

Allow the user to input a number of business days (e.g., 10 days), and automatically calculate the end date excluding weekends and holidays.

Optionally, allow the start date to be set as a relative value (e.g., “3 days from now”) and calculate the schedule forward from there.

Include visibility of the calculated end date during work order creation so users can confirm accuracy.

Benefits:

Improves scheduling accuracy for jobs that don’t fit traditional week-based durations.

Helps service businesses provide realistic timelines to customers and teams.

Reduces manual calculations and errors when planning over extended but irregular periods.

Use Case Example:
A customer wants to book a work order that spans 10 business days. They want to start next Monday and expect the system to automatically skip weekends and factor in any holidays to calculate the correct end date.
Solution:
Some of this could be customized; some it it not.

In general if there is a lot of flexibility to be had it's quite difficult. 
First and foremost, we can determine if a day is a weekday or weekend - serve up a warning to the user the selected date is a weekend. 

We could further enhance this by pulling in a holiday schedule, and validating against that. 

However, to increment based on 45 workdays. 
That's where things might get a little difficult and we would benefit from some actions or AI improvements.

Title: Grid Sort Improvement  - Ability to Sort Contacts by Numbers Associated with Customer Names
Description: They would like to be able to sort using the associated numbers with the customers and sub-customers, right now it is only sorting based on alphabets. He is saying this is possible with other platforms.
Solution:
Yes this is possible; there are a couple of solutions:

1.) Custom Grid Views, Custom grid views allow you to set sort priorities
- Sort by Customer Full Name
- Sort by Contact Name thereafter. 

2.) Customization could also accommodate this, in addition to default sort criteria, we could re-order the expressions to better match the header sort when you click on the headers.

Title: Multi-Tenancy Improvement - Share Leads Across Tenants in a Multi-Tenant Method Environment
Description:
Enable the ability to share lead records between two or more Method accounts (tenants), particularly when the businesses are related (e.g., same owner, supply chain partners). This would allow a lead created in one account to be viewable, editable, or actionable in another, based on permission levels.

Why it Matters:
Many Method users own or operate multiple businesses that are connected. For example, a wholesaler and retailer under the same owner may want to:

Collaboratively manage a shared sales pipeline

Avoid duplicate outreach to the same contact

Seamlessly route opportunities or POs between businesses

Currently, there’s no native way to sync or share leads between tenants without duplicating data or relying on external automations.

Proposed Functionality:

A lead sync setting that allows tenants to opt-in to sharing

Permission-based control over which fields are visible or editable

Automatic linking of related customer/vendor records between tenants

Audit trail of changes synced between accounts

Priority: High (especially for businesses with shared supply chains or holding company structures)

🧠 Use Case (e.g., to explain to support or a partner):
I own two companies that both use QuickBooks Online.

Company 1 sells to consumers and purchases inventory from Company 2.

Company 2 is a wholesaler and sells to Company 1 and other retail outlets.

Ideally, I’d like a workflow where Company 1 can issue purchase orders to Company 2, and Company 2 can automatically generate a corresponding sales order or invoice—without any manual entry.

Since both companies use Method, this should be achievable with shared data or a form of cross-tenant sync. Does Method’s multi-tenancy roadmap include this kind of lead/opportunity/customer sharing?
Solution:
This certainly could be customized. 

Where once a purchase is made in one tenant, a PO could be created for the second tenant - the second tenant would then have a Sales Order Created for them referencing the PO and dispatch the items.

While not rocket science, there are a lot of moving pieces. 
This could certainly be managed via Multi-tenancy and customization.

Title: Transactions Improvement - Restrict Sales Price Editing to Managers/Admins
Description:
Feature Overview:
Restrict the ability to edit the Sales Price field on transactions (e.g., Estimates, Sales Orders, Invoices) to only users with Manager or Admin level permissions.

Current Behavior:
All users with access to transaction forms can currently modify the Sales Price field.

This opens up the risk of unauthorized discounts, pricing inconsistencies, and margin erosion.

Proposed Behavior:
Sales Price field is view-only or locked for standard users.

Only users with a Manager or Admin role (based on role-based access control) can:

Manually adjust the Sales Price.

Override default pricing logic.

Optional: Include a log or audit trail of any Sales Price changes (who, when, what value).

Scope:
Applies to: Estimates, Sales Orders, Invoices, and any other transaction types where Sales Price is present.

Applies to: Web app interface and any mobile/tablet versions used in the field.

Business Justification:
Prevents unauthorized discounting or accidental price overrides.

Ensures pricing strategy compliance and protects margins.

Reduces need for after-the-fact price corrections or escalations.

Empowers managers to control exceptions while maintaining standardization.

Optional Enhancements (Phase 2):
Add an approval pop-up or request flow when a non-manager attempts to change pricing.

Surface a tooltip to standard users explaining why the field is locked and who to contact.
Solution:
Certainly customizable. 

The scope of the work here includes making this changes across multiple transactions and multiple workflows (Mobile vs. Desktop)

1.) Create a list of people (preferences) who are able to edit the sales price on a transaction (This could be transaction specific or global)

2.) On Add Item Screens (Mobile) and editable grids (Non-Mobile) make sure those fields are set to read-only or restricted.

Title: Work Order Improvement - Collaborative Work Order Notes with Notifications
Description:
Summary:
Enable users to post notes to a work order and automatically notify all associated resources. As additional notes are submitted, all linked resources should continue to receive updates, fostering real-time collaboration.

Problem Statement:
Currently, notes added to a work order are not proactively shared with all relevant team members. This creates communication gaps, particularly in cases where multiple people (e.g., field techs, project managers, subcontractors) are working on the same job and need to stay updated on the latest information.

Proposed Solution:

Add the ability to tag or auto-assign multiple users to a work order.

When a note is posted to a work order, send a notification (in-app and/or email) to all associated users.

As replies or new notes are added by any of those users, the same group receives the update.

Optional: allow @mentioning specific users within a note for more targeted notifications.

Benefits:

Improves field-to-office and peer-to-peer communication.

Keeps all stakeholders aligned on work order updates.

Reduces delays caused by missed or siloed information.

Use Case:
A project manager adds a note to clarify scope on a work order. Two field technicians and a subcontractor associated with the work order are instantly notified. Later, the subcontractor adds a follow-up question, and all parties are notified again, enabling faster resolution and fewer errors.
Solution:
Certainly customizable; however, this does have a few pre-requisites.

1.) Multiple Work Order Assignments
2.) Then using that information create a workflow in both the Work Order and Field Crew app and notification.

Title: Integration Improvement – Import LinkedIn Leads into Method
Description:
Request Summary:
A customer is requesting a native or streamlined integration between LinkedIn and Method:CRM to automatically bring in new leads captured via LinkedIn Lead Gen Forms or LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

Use Case:
The customer frequently uses LinkedIn for outbound and inbound lead generation. They want the ability to:

Automatically sync lead data from LinkedIn into Method CRM

Map LinkedIn fields (e.g., name, email, company, job title) to Method lead/contact fields

Trigger follow-up workflows within Method (e.g., lead status update, auto-assign to sales rep, initiate a drip campaign)

Current Challenge:
Currently, LinkedIn leads need to be manually exported and imported into Method. This creates extra work, delays in outreach, and introduces the risk of data entry errors.

Desired Outcome:

Support for direct LinkedIn Lead Gen Form integration via LinkedIn’s API or a native connector

Option to push leads directly to the Contacts/Leads table in Method

Automation triggers based on new lead entries (e.g., create activity, assign rep, send email)

Customer Value:
This feature would:

Improve response time to high-intent leads

Eliminate manual data entry

Enable faster conversion and more efficient lead nurturing
Solution:
There are a few options to help enable this functionality:

1.) Easy - LinkedIn Offers the Ability to export and import via CSV, this information can be added to Method CRM through our stock functionality. 

2.) Medium - Zapier can act as partial workarounds but lack native support for mapping or automation within Method.

3.) Hard - LinkedIn does offer API Access, which we can tap into to help automate some of the workflows directly with Method.

Title: Field Crew Improvement - Ability to change or create new contacts from the Field Crew app
Description:
Customer has field crew members that deliver to construction sites and require a point of contact and a sign off upon completion of delivery. 

Can we customize a way for the field crew members to edit or add new additional contacts from the field crew app.
Solution:
Certainly Customizable. 

In theory, could be a simple flow where the technician has the ability to create new contact
First Name
Last Name
Title / Role

This info could be captured and attached to the customer / job with a simple activity to describe when that contact was added and why.

Title: Migrations - Odoo to Method - CRM Migration from Odoo to Method
Description:
1.) There are a number of custom fields and information they want to capture
2.) Most of their data lives in QuickBooks; however, some importing is required.
Solution:
Expectations should be set that migrating to any new system; will never be seamless and will take time and energy from all parties. 

While this estimate is high, the objective is to educate the user on the process and why it takes time. In particular the impact it can have on their operations since we're so tightly integrated with QuickBooks.

Technically, 

First and foremost, the schema needs to be setup. 
- What information is coming from Odoo
- What format
- How much of it is duplicated by information in QuickBooks and is it necessary

Importing is generally laborious because 
- Multiple imports to link records
- Analysis to remove duplicates

Once the import and data transfer occurs we have to explore how the data will be suraced up to the user.
- Conditionally displaying fields depending on other values
- Special permission considerations

Training on new features and integrations

Title: Activity Calendar Improvement - Ability to Waitlist an activity
Description:
Customer is a solopreneur in the art industry and prefer to have one calendar to central all sales and jobs. 

The Work order calendar has many of the tools she needs such as a waitlist and the ability to invoice an activity. 

Can those features be translated to the activities app?
Solution:
This seems possible. 

Since both the Work Order and Activity are based on the same table this should be achievable. 
We should make sure the waiting status

Title: Calendar Improvement  - Integration with ICalendar
Description: For the Apple ecospace, many business use Icalendar. is there the possibility of creating an integration with Icalendar to ensure a SMBs who are looking to have their personal and business schedule in one place is able to do so?
Solution:
Direct Icalendar integration is not possible. 

However, you can link a google calendar to Method and then link that into Ical and have a two way sync.

Title: Work Order Improvement - Ability to update the service item based on Time Tracking
Description:
Customer is a handyman service and bill based on time spent. 

Can we customize a way to update the quantity of primary service item on the work order based on the time spend on the time tracking entry?
Solution:
Definitely possible. 

Ideally if there are multiple items on the work order, we would then capture what service we would track against.

On complete, we would then update the work order quantity with the time captured.

Title: Opportunity App Improvement - Automatically Log Activity on Opportunity Stage Change
Description:
Automatically create and log an activity (e.g., note, task, or timestamped update) every time the stage of an opportunity is changed within Method:CRM.

Use Case:
Sales reps and managers need to maintain a clear audit trail of changes to opportunity stages for better tracking, accountability, and pipeline reporting. Currently, changes to opportunity stages are not tied to logged activities, making it difficult to see when changes occurred or who made them without checking the record history.

Proposed Functionality:

Trigger an activity creation when the Opportunity.Stage field is updated.

The activity should include:

Date/time of change

Name of the user who made the change

The old stage and the new stage (e.g., 'Stage changed from Proposal to Negotiation')

Link to the opportunity record

Activity type can be customizable or default to something like “System - Stage Update”

Should work with both manual updates and workflow-based updates
Solution:
Certainly could be customized. 

Ideally on stage change, we would automatically generate an activity where the user could then update their notes on the change and why that change is made. 

This could be done as part of the workflow, where when a stage is changed, we take information in the a Note Box, and automatically log that as an activity behind the scenes - while keeping the user's focus on the opportunity page.

Title: Dropdown Improvement - Font size adjustment for dropdown values
Description:
Customer is looking to increase the font size for the dropdown values.

Currently, we are able to modify the dropdown caption font; however, there is no option available to adjust the font size of the actual records that appear in the dropdown list.
Solution: Cannot be customized

Title: Email Analytics Improvement  - Email Campaign Enhancement - Click Tracking
Description:
Customer is wondering if we can customize a solution where the click status can highlight what was actually clicked on the email campaign. 

This would help them identify the type of click and also which CTA is most effective.
Solution:
In general it's best to minimize any and all CTAs to focus on one clickable area or source. 

Multiple CTA tracking in general has been discouraged. 
Emails should be pointed enough to drive to one clear CTA; however, in recent years the trend to multiple CTAs has become more popular.

Title: Invoicing Improvement - Rolled-Up Invoicing (Summarized Invoice Generation)
Description:
Problem Statement:
Customers who issue multiple invoices per client within a billing period find it inefficient and confusing to send each invoice separately. Clients often prefer a single, consolidated view of all charges — especially when reconciling accounts or making payments.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a Rolled-Up Invoicing feature that allows users to:

Select multiple invoices for a customer (based on date range, tags, or projects).

Generate a summary invoice that:

Lists individual invoice numbers, dates, and totals.

Provides a combined balance due.

Optionally includes a PDF attachment or link to the individual invoice details.

User Benefits:
Reduces client confusion and back-and-forth over multiple emails/invoices.

Streamlines monthly billing workflows.

Supports a more professional and consolidated customer experience.

Helps customers align with procurement or accounts payable processes at larger organizations.

Key Functional Requirements:
Ability to group invoices by:

Customer

Date range

Project/Job

Generate a new invoice marked as 'Rolled-Up' that includes:

Summary line items or sections for each invoice

Reference to original invoice numbers

Option to send the rolled-up invoice by email or download as PDF.

Option to retain original invoices in QuickBooks / Method for record-keeping.

Toggle to enable/disable rolled-up invoice generation per customer.
Solution:
Certainly possible; essentially, we would just create  a simple invoice report, that lists all the line items. 

As part of the line items we would include the date they we're invoiced on or grouped based on Invoice date as a single pdf.

Title: Opportunity Enhancement - Calendar
Description: Customer would like to customize a calendar view for the opportunities to help  illustrate status and deadline for their Freight Services business
Solution:
We cannot customize opportunities per say; however, what we can do is make sure every opportunity has an activity that represents the opportunity close date. 

Then we can filter these activities to show in the activity calendar. 

When the activity is 'updated' the close date on the opportunity is also updated.
Furthermore, when the activity is 'Clicked' the user is navigated to the opportunity. 

Basically, we just use the activity as a vehicle to navigate and update the opportunity.

Title: Chart Customization Improvement - Customize Hover Points
Description:
It would good to be able to customize what values we can see when we hover over the data points on our charts; very similar to cards where we can show an expression similar to how the Work Order is able to show the event cards when we click on it.

The reason for this is because sometimes we want to just hover over the data point and not click into the graph. It saves one click and is convenient for the user to see.
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: Work Order Improvement - Scheduling & Optimizing Routes - Create a work order with only a date option
Description:
Customer wants to create a work order  with date only but not a time. Use case- 'We are a landscape contractor. I want to put in all the daily work orders to create a mow list. The mow list is a list of clients the crew will visit in one day. I want Method to create the most efficient route. Then I want to send that route to the crew so they can track their day'

Would it be possible to implement this workflow through customization?
Solution:
Yes removing the times are certainly possible. 

That said, when you go to optimize the route, all the appointments are setup in chronological order based on the optimized route. 

In general, you would create a list of work orders on a date, then create an optimized route, which will then setup the calendar in order (using time) as a way to organize things.

Title: Editable Grid Improvement - Click Once when entering information in an Editable Grid
Description:
Currently you have to click twice to enter information in an editable grid, my customer finds that annoying because they'll click into it like they click into our other fields not in an editable once and assumed that they are typing into the field only to realize after attempting typing in the field that they are typing into nothing.

It'd be great if you only had to click once.
Solution: Cannot be customized.

Title: Basic Match Action Improvement  - Math Function Action (Add different base for 'log')
Description:
Currently, when we select the 'log10' option in the Math function, we get base 10. If we select 'log', we get base 'e'. There is no convenient way to have base 15 for example. You have to do something like: log10(x)/log10(15) = log15(x). If we had an option to select the base to do the calculations, it would allow us to do complicated math functions without the need to create more actions.

The use case here is to be able to determine whether water is corrosive, balanced, or scaling using the LSI (Langelier Saturation Index).
Solution:
Cannot be customzied.

Great request.

Title: Email Improvement  - Ability to put the unsubscribe link to the desired email temaplates
Description:
The customer looking to replicate the unsubscribe functionality found in Method’s email campaigns within their own custom email templates. Specifically, they want to provide recipients with a visible “Unsubscribe” link in their standard email communications—similar to what’s automatically added when marking an email as a marketing message.

Currently, there doesn’t seem to be a straightforward way to manually insert an unsubscribe link or shortcode into custom templates outside of the marketing email workflow.

Feature Request / Questions:

Is there a way to manually add an unsubscribe link (e.g., a merge field or URL token) to email templates?

If not, can we consider adding a dynamic tag or function like {{UnsubscribeLink}} that generates a compliant opt-out link, which users can insert into any email body?

Alternatively, could we expose the same logic used in the marketing email checkbox as a reusable block for templates?

This would help users offer opt-out options consistently across all outbound communications—not just campaigns—and stay compliant with email best practices.

Looking forward to your thoughts or a suggested workaround.
Solution:
This is available in stock via customization.

There is an additional setting on the 'Send Email Action' that differentiates the type of email that is being sent. 

Anything that is set as a Marketing email, will automatically have an unsubscribe link. Anything marked as transactional will not. 

There are use cases for each - available via our help centre under send email action.

Title: Transaction Improvement - Display Customer Balance Across All Transaction Types
Description:
Summary:
Add the ability to view a customer's total outstanding balance across all transaction types — including Estimates, Sales Orders, and Invoices — in one consolidated view.

Request Details:
Currently, users must check each transaction type individually to assess a customer's balance or financial standing. This creates friction when trying to understand the full scope of a customer’s pending and confirmed financial obligations.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a unified 'Customer Balance Overview' section within the Customer Profile or a dedicated dashboard widget that displays:

Total value of open Estimates

Total value of open Sales Orders

Total value of unpaid Invoices

Optional: Include Credits, Payments, and Deposits on file

A Net Balance Summary that reflects total receivables (invoices) minus payments and credits

Why This is Valuable:

Provides sales and finance teams with a 360° financial view of the customer

Helps assess customer credit risk before approving additional orders

Reduces manual work and toggling between modules

Enables more informed decision-making during sales and support calls

Bonus Enhancements:

Add filtering by status (e.g., open, closed, overdue)

Include aging info on unpaid balances

Allow drill-down into specific transactions from the summary view
Solution:
Certainly possible.

On customer change / on screen load, we would simply update the screen with the current customer balance. 

Implementing just the visibility is simple; however, if we wanted to wrap this together with other business logic - the scope would need to be better defined.

Title: Contacts App Improvement - Ability to Select Contact or Customer for Activities
Description:
Right now, Activities in Method are tied primarily to Contacts. However, many users prefer or are accustomed to working at the Customer level, especially when:

The contact person isn’t known or relevant to the activity.

The interaction is with the business as a whole rather than an individual.

They're replicating workflows from QBO, which allows logging details at the Customer level.
Solution:
Technically possible via the UI. 
All of these relationships exist already, it's just a matter of adjusting the appearance of the screens and how the information is surfaced up.

Multiple apps / changes may require more time.

Title: Locked Transactions - Estimates After Submission – Manager Control Enabled
Description: Sales team can create estimates → but cannot edit or change after submission → only admin/manager can modify or approve → all integrated with QuickBooks.
Solution:
Certainly possible. 

Ideally we would track what 'Submit' means and when a 'Transaction' is no longer in 'Draft'. 

Based off of that, we can lock down the estimate / invoice / or anything.

Title: Ability to filter the report by customer type.
Description:
The customer is looking to know the approximate time and money it might take to get tah feature added to the Invoice report where they can filter the report based on the customer type they have set in their account. 

Please see the attachments for reference.
Solution:
This is certainly possible.

That said there are about 9-12 reports on this page that will need to be updated and configured. 

There are a few options:

1.) Provide a filter at the top to reflect the information across all charts (total of 12+ charts)
2.) Provide a simple chart by customer type for each option above (total of 3 charts)

The second option is likely best and able to provide customers with the insights they need to summarize data.

Title: Mobile App Enhancement - Ability to edit email for transactions
Description:
Current Behavior:
Certain URL-based pages (e.g., Edit Email Details) do not load within the Method mobile app. Instead, users are redirected to the web portal, which creates friction for mobile users.

Issues Identified:

Redirection often prompts users to log in again, disrupting workflow.

Users are sometimes logged out of their desktop session as a result.

There’s often no clear way to return to the mobile app post-redirect, especially for less tech-savvy users.

Request:
Can we explore the possibility of customizing an additional mobile screen specifically for the Edit Email Details functionality?
This would greatly improve the in-app user experience and reduce friction for users who primarily engage with Method through mobile.
Solution:
In theory, this should be something that is explored by the core development team.  

Could it be customized? 

Yes the advanced email catalog entry, supports editing via  a separate button instead of doing it in the popup preview. 

Consider using the Advanced email catalog when the environment is 'Mobile' continue using the default flow 'on Desktop'

Title: Invoice Enhancement - The ability to generate multiple reports together with the invoice
Description:
The customer operates in the transportation of dairy products and is required to generate a multi-document invoice package for each delivery.

Requirement:
A custom workflow or document output that includes the following three documents per transaction:

BOL (Bill of Lading)

Order Sheet

Invoice
Solution:
Certainly possible to customize this; depending on the nature of the data structure.

Multiple page reports based on 2-3 tables and datasets are certainly possible. 
Additional work may be required to make sure everything is contained a single page appropriately.

Title: CRM Improvement - Transaction Improvement - Clarify (Deleted) Status for QuickBooks Records
Description:
QuickBooks Online appends “(deleted)” to names of customers, vendors, and sometimes transactions, but doesn't hard delete them. This 'soft delete' can cause confusion in your app when these records still appear, especially if users expect them to be fully removed.

Tooltip or Hover Text

Add a hover-over tooltip on any name that includes “(deleted)” that explains:
“This record was deleted in QuickBooks but is still stored for historical and audit purposes. You cannot interact with it further.”

Iconography + Status Tag

Show a trash can icon or a greyed-out label like [Deleted] beside the name.

Consider a greyed-out style (e.g., dimmed text, italics) to indicate the status visually.

Optional: Add a “Deleted in QBO” status tag on the record’s detail page.
Solution: If needed this could be customized; however, I wouldn't say it's a pressing issue for many users though it is a source of some confusion.

Title: Ability to change Google calendar sync preferences time
Description:
Currently, Google Calendar automatically trigger sync every 20 minutes, however, customer want to have an ability to change that to every 5 minutes. 
Can there be button or settings to change the sync time? Could this be possible through customization?
Solution:
At this time the google calendar sync cadence cannot be changed. 

However, this could be something worth exploring if there is a more timely need for appointments to appear in calendars. 

I'm certain the technology around calendars has improved since this was originally created.

Title: Ability to use Stock Apps permissions, e.g. Create permission on txn's.
Description: Customer wants users to be able to create transactions but not edit them. Ideally controlling this with the disabled create preference in user permissions
Solution:
The create permission is complicated as create has multiple definitions for certain people. For example, if someone creates a transaction, but has not sync'd it to QuickBooks, should they be able to edit it?

When does create end and edit start?

Today these definitions are incredibly vague and overlapping. As a result we've made the explicit decision to not support them. 

That said, this could certainly be customized to adjust for your clients needs.

Title: Auto-Creation of Sequentially Named Sub-Customers When Opportunity is Won
Description:
Customer is using QuickBooks Projects and values the ability to have a project number assigned sequentially (e.g., 001, 002, etc.) as the Project Name.

Request:
Can we build a custom workflow in MethodCRM where:

When an Opportunity is marked as Won,

A Sub-Customer is automatically created.

The Sub-Customer Name is auto-assigned the next sequential number (e.g., if the last sub-customer was Project 105, the new one would be Project 106),

This number becomes the Project Name and potentially the Customer Name if preferred.
Solution:
Absolutely possible. 

There are some edge-cases we would need to review. 
eg. What happens if a customer was manually created in QB etc. 

Which may throw off the sequence; however, certainly customizable.

Title: Line Item Customization - Preserve Custom Descriptions When Changing Items
Description:
Customer has a unique workflow where the item on a line (e.g., 'Railing') is switched to 'Custom' when converting an Estimate to a Sales Order.

Current Behavior in QuickBooks Desktop:
In QuickBooks, when the item is changed at the Sales Order level, the custom description previously entered by the sales rep is preserved and not overwritten by the default item description.

Request for MethodCRM:
Can we customize Method so that when a user changes the item in the line item grid, the existing Description is retained rather than replaced by the default item description?

Use Case:
This change would support sales reps who use descriptive text to communicate scope, ensuring continuity and reducing the need to retype custom content when switching item types.
Solution:
This is quite a light-weight customization.

In theory on the item dropdown change, we would make sure that the description column on the line item grid is not updated. 

Alternatively, we could give the user the option to reset it to the default or keep the existing description. 

Both light-weight customizations.

Title: Intuit Enterprise Suite - Ability to Integrate with Dimensions (QBO)
Description: Can we integrate or customize a way to integrate the new QBO feature, dimensions, to our current workflow?
Solution:
At this time this cannot be customized; however, it is something we should revisit. 

Dimensions are basically custom fields at the line item level that can be used for reporting.

Title: Accounting Integration - FreshBooks Integration
Description:
Description:
Requesting the ability to integrate Method:CRM with FreshBooks to support customers who currently use FreshBooks for accounting.

Key Goals:

Sync customer, invoice, and payment data between FreshBooks and Method

Enable users to create and manage FreshBooks invoices directly from Method

Support for FreshBooks Classic and FreshBooks New (depending on API availability)

Use Cases:

Businesses currently using FreshBooks want a CRM layer on top of their accounting

Users want to eliminate double entry between FreshBooks and CRM

Field service teams want to manage customers and send invoices from a single interface

Value to Users:

Expands Method’s addressable market beyond QuickBooks/Xero

Helps FreshBooks users adopt a true workflow automation CRM

Reduces friction for customers switching from QuickBooks to FreshBooks

Dependencies:

FreshBooks API (authentication, rate limits, available endpoints)
Solution:
While a solution could be customized with APIs / Zapier.
In general accounting integration is important for us and an integration of this magnitude should not be taken lightly.

Title: Migrations - Migrate Customer Data from FreshBooks to Method
Description:
Description:
Requesting a migration utility or one-time import process that enables customers to move from FreshBooks to Method:CRM (and QuickBooks).

Key Goals:

Import customers, contacts, invoices, and payments from FreshBooks into Method

Map FreshBooks data to Method fields with minimal data cleanup

Support both Classic and New FreshBooks exports

Use Cases:

Customers transitioning away from FreshBooks to QuickBooks + Method

Method partners assisting in onboarding new customers

Reducing friction and time spent manually re-entering accounting data

Value to Users:

Simplifies onboarding for former FreshBooks users

Opens up Method to a new group of users transitioning away from FreshBooks

Reduces support effort and improves data accuracy
Solution:
Depending on the size of the dataset this is certainly possible:

Easy
- Leads, Vendorts, Contacts, Customers and Customer Information

Hard
- Transactions and Payments
- We need to make sure all the data exists for mapping / remapping (eg. Items, tax codes etc.)

Title: Payment Preference Improvement  - Ability to enforce Save payment
Description:
Customer is looking to use the Method Proposal app to management her field services subscription business. 

Can we customize a workflow where auto pay (save payment details) is automatically enforced when proposals are accepted?
Solution:
Yes, this is a setting that is available on both the gateway and widget side.
We can enable automated saving of payment information.

By default, this is an option provided to the end user.

Title: Grid Improvement - Zebra Striping - Alternate Row colors -  Bring Classic Grid Striping Into Method New Editable Grids
Description: In Method Classic, Grids have an odd and even striping to easily read the rows. It would be great if this was an available feature for our new Editable Grids in Method New
Solution: At this time, there are no display settings for zebra striping.

Title: Editable Grids - Be Able To Adjust Row Height
Description:
I was on a call with Tyler and he pointed out that you are able to see more records in a Grid in Method Classic, compared to the Editable Grids in Method New. The reason behind this was the row height was smaller in the Grids in Method Classic. 

It would be great if you are able to adjust the row height in Editable Grids to either be smaller or bigger
Solution:
The padding on grids does seem quite large in some cases where you're looking to scan and review lists. 

Part of this is potentially a function of expressions and how we use them. 
Consider removing expressions and multiline datasets from grids, to tighten up the experience.

Title: Editable Grids - Column Searches At The Top Of Column
Description: I was on a call with Tyler and he said he really liked the experience in Method Classic where Grids are able to be searched by each row at the top of the row. He said the Advanced Search in Method New requires more clicks and the experience is slower
Solution:
This mirrors our classic search experience.

Though it makes the search experience a little more complex, it is something simple when building multi-part queries. 

We should consider using the new editable grid with 'Chips' enabled for advanced search for this client and understand the short comings there.

Title: Field Crew Improvement - Centralized Internal Notes Management with Read/Resolved
Description:
A customer has requested improvements to the Internal Notes feature on Work Orders, specifically around visibility and tracking. The goal is to enhance efficiency and reduce missed or repeated communication.

Current Challenge:
Internal notes are scattered across individual work orders.

There’s no indication of which notes have been reviewed or addressed.

The customer currently needs to manually open each work order to check and respond to notes, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

Requested Enhancements:
Centralized View: A dedicated screen or dashboard (e.g., a 'screenlet') displaying all internal notes across work orders.

Status Management:

Ability to mark notes as 'Read' or 'Resolved'.

Once marked as resolved, notes should be optionally hidden or filtered out.

Sorting/Filtering:

Filter notes by status (e.g., Unread, Resolved)

Optionally filter by customer, date, or assigned user

Customer Use Case:
The customer prefers to review internal notes once at the end of the day, and quickly mark them as resolved from a single interface. This helps avoid navigating through each individual work order and keeps their workflow organized and time-efficient.

Benefits:
Streamlined communication tracking

Reduced time spent managing notes

Improved accountability and follow-up

Aligns with productivity-focused user workflows

Example Statuses:
Viewed
Acknowledged
Blocked
Completed
Solution:
At this time, this cannot be customized since this table and information is hidden. 
However, this is something the product team should continue to explore.

Title: Import Estimates into Method from Excel
Description: The customer is inquiring about the cost and expected timeframe for importing estimates into their Method account for one time, and they also want to know how to do it independently.
Solution:
Importing estimates and any transactional data is labourious, there are multiple steps required:

1.) All required information must already exist
1a.) Customers, Leads, Terms, Items, all dropdown data must already exist in the CRM

If not all those entries need to be created

2.) Then you need to import a header (Estimates )
3.) Then you need to import multiple lines per header (Estimate Line)

Again, all required fields including Quantity, Rate, Tax must be included as part of the import. 

If not, we're going to need to design business rules around this. 
This is a large project.

How we'd customize it?
1.) Build a table to match the spreadsheet we want to import
2.) Build a button that processes all the above and makes the appropriate decisions when a certain value isn't found. 

This isn't recommended unless the customer is going to be importing on a regular basis from a source like shopify or their own website for example


Alternatives:

1.) Import using QuickBooks
2.) Find another service that support Importing (Transaction Importer)

Title: Dashboard Improvement | Grouping Apps Together Based on App Pack
Description:
Ability to Group Apps together into either their respective App Packs or a custom grouping.

Pain Point:
1. On Team PS, working with multiple clients, the Invoice App may be on the top of the screen for 1 account, and then at the bottom for another account and then in the middle in a 3rd account. Keeping tracking of where the Invoice is on which account you're on is time consuming and can be a bit frustrating.
2. On Mobility City (Franchised Account); you can have a master admin that overseas all issues with Method; however they will face the same issue as laid out in (1).

Benefit:
1. By grouping select items into either their App Pack or custom groups; yes, it's 1 more click, but it saves more time than having to sift through all the different apps on different accounts.
Solution: Great suggestion!

Title: Calendar Improvement - Provide a search bar / the ability to search outside of waitlist
Description:
Description:
Currently, there is no native search functionality for the calendar in Method. Users must manually scroll through months to locate a specific work order, which is inefficient—especially for high-volume accounts.

User Impact:

Users often want to find a specific work order on the calendar using details like address or customer name.

A workaround using a custom search bar helps filter matching records, but the calendar widget does not auto-navigate to the relevant date.

This forces users to manually scroll through months, even when the match is found, impacting productivity and user satisfaction.

Proposed Solution:

Add a native search bar to the calendar widget (similar to the waitlist search).

Enable users to search by:

Work order number

Address

Customer name

Upon search, the calendar should:

Highlight or isolate the matching work order

Automatically scroll to the appropriate date on the calendar view

Benefit:
Improves efficiency for scheduling teams and field service users who need to quickly locate and manage work orders on the calendar.
Solution:
A work around is kind of available by using a textbox on the screen and filtering events based on the content of that textbox; however, this could generate mixed results and probably not ideal or as responsive as people expect.

1.) Add unbound text object to screen
2.) Add a filter to calendar
3.) On textbox lose focus, refresh the calendar object / show a duplicate calendar object that's filtered.

Title: API Integration - Maridian Link
Description:
Background:
We’ve briefly confirmed that Maridian Link offers an Open API, which may allow for integration with Method. While the feasibility appears promising, further technical scoping and discovery are required to understand the exact capabilities, endpoints, and authentication methods available.

Request:

Initiate a technical review of Maridian Link’s Open API documentation.

Assess compatibility and potential use cases (e.g., data sync, workflow automation).

Define a rough scope of effort based on initial integration goals.

Impact:
Could enable real-time data exchange between Method and Maridian Link, streamline customer workflows, and expand support for financial/lending organizations.
Solution:
MeridianLink® helps financial institutions of all sizes deliver faster, smarter, and more personalized lending and account opening experiences. Our highly configurable digital platform—MeridianLink® One—unifies your lending operations into one solution, placing people at the center of every interaction.

With an open API we're able to certainly make connections between both systems; however, this will need to be further explored by a consultant.

Title: Public Page Improvement - Enable Public Pages to Be Searchable by Google
Description:
Use Case:
Currently, public pages created in Method (e.g., item listings, web forms) are not indexed by Google due to restrictions on indexing. Customers who embed these pages for SEO or discoverability are not seeing them show up in search results.

Proposed Functionality:

Add a toggle setting for each public page to allow/disallow indexing by search engines (via robots.txt or meta tag control)

Include optional fields to customize page metadata (title, description) for SEO

Benefits:

Empowers users to control discoverability of their content

Enables real SEO value from embedded or linked Method pages

Supports lead gen and organic traffic use cases
Solution:
Workaround Today:
None available without customization and manual SEO embedding

Title: Invoice Improvement - Generate Order Confirmation Notifications PDFs in Method
Description:
An Order Confirmation is a document sent to the customer after an order is placed—but before payment or fulfillment. It acts as a formal acknowledgment that the order has been received and outlines the agreed-upon details.

Key Characteristics:
Timing: Sent before the invoice and before fulfillment.

Purpose: Confirms the customer’s intent to purchase specific products or services under agreed-upon terms.

Contents: Typically includes:

Customer and company info

Order number and date

Itemized list of products/services

Pricing and estimated delivery

Terms and conditions

Why Customers Request It:
Internal Approval: Their teams may need documentation to approve payment or schedule delivery.

Procurement Process: Many B2B clients require an Order Confirmation to match with purchase orders.

Clarity: It ensures all parties are aligned before payment and delivery.

Why It’s Useful for You (Method CRM):
Reduces reliance on external templates or emails for pre-invoice communication.

Helps avoid disputes by confirming the scope of work or goods early.

Improves professionalism and customer experience.
Solution:
Certainly customizable.

In general I would recommend this to be customized along side an 'Order Status' or 'Invoice Status Field'. 
Based on the Invoice status, we can then generate PDFs  & Automated emails to support the customer's fullfillment process.

Title: Accounting Integration - Wave Accounting - Support for Wave Accounting
Description:
We’re requesting a native integration between Method:CRM and Wave Accounting to support small businesses and freelancers who rely on Wave for their accounting needs but require robust CRM and workflow capabilities.

🎯 Use Case
Many small businesses using Wave do not have a scalable CRM that connects with their accounting system. Method:CRM could be a powerful front-office solution if it synced contacts, invoices, payments, and estimates with Wave in real-time or on a schedule. This would allow:

Automated sync of customer and vendor data between Wave and Method

Creation and management of Wave invoices directly from Method

Visibility into payment status and revenue insights inside the CRM

Enhanced reporting and forecasting with combined CRM + accounting data

🔗 Integration Requirements (Initial Scope)
OAuth authentication with Wave

Sync of Customers/Vendors

Sync of Invoices and Payments

Read access to Chart of Accounts, Taxes

Write-back ability for creating invoices, estimates, or contacts from Method

💬 Customer Impact
This integration would open the door for startups, solopreneurs, and service businesses using Wave to adopt Method:CRM, increasing reach beyond the QuickBooks ecosystem. It supports market growth and improves product accessibility for cost-conscious users.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Zapier Improvement - Support Prepare for Accounting or Automatically Trigger Prepare for Accounting on New Transactions
Description: Ensure that all newly created transactions in Method automatically call the 'Prepare for Accounting' function upon creation. This will allow the transactions to be immediately eligible for syncing with QuickBooks without requiring manual intervention.
Solution:
At this time this direct functionality cannot be customized.

That said there is a work around:

1.) As part of the zapier integration, create a new field on the transaction table 
2.) Also create a field called last modified by zapier
3.) Then create an app routine to loop through everything that was created by zapier in the last 24 hours and prepare for accounting.

Title: Zapier Improvement - Service to Create Short URL
Description:
Enable the ability to generate short, shareable URLs for internal Method screens (e.g., Estimates, Customers) when using Zapier integrations. This would allow users to include direct links in automated emails triggered by Zaps—for example, creating an Estimate and immediately emailing a user with a link to view the newly created transaction or customer record within Method.

Use Case:
A customer uses Zapier to automate Estimate creation and wants to simultaneously send an email with a clickable link to view that specific Estimate or related Customer within Method. Short URLs would simplify navigation and improve internal workflow efficiency.
Solution: Cannot be Customized at this time

Title: Report Designer Improvement - Support for Watermarking
Description: Customer want to be able to make their logo semi-transparent to create a watermark on report pdfs
Solution:
This functionality is available in stock.

1.) Go to Report settings (click anywhere outside of a band)
2.) Under Appearance and Design
- There are relevant settings for Watermark design and uploading 
- Including Text & Images.

Title: Opportunity Improvement - Enable Printing of Individual Opportunity Records in Printable Format (Similar to Estimates)
Description:
Currently, users can print Estimates in a structured, printable format that includes key details and branding. However, there is no equivalent functionality for printing Opportunities from the system. Users need a way to print Opportunity records for client meetings, offline review, or documentation purposes.

We can use the print opportunity button on the reports to print multiple.
Solution:
This is something we can certainly do that said a template would need to be designed.

If we want to mirror the estimate experience, we can likely make opportunity and opportunity lines to a copy of the estimate pdf.

Then create a generation process.

Title: Convert Vendor Leads to Customer Leads
Description:
Summary:
Enable users to seamlessly convert a vendor record or vendor lead into a customer lead without needing to re-enter information.

Description:
Many businesses work with companies that start as vendors and later become customers, or vice versa. Currently, Method requires manual duplication or data re-entry when this transition occurs. This can lead to:

Data inconsistency

Wasted time

Errors during re-entry

We are requesting a feature that allows users to convert a Vendor Lead into a Customer Lead (or a full Customer) with the following details preserved:

Company Name

Contact Information

Notes and Activity Logs

Custom fields and tags

Benefits:

Reduces manual data entry

Maintains full lead-to-customer lifecycle in one system

Improves reporting and historical accuracy

Saves time for teams managing dual-role accounts
Solution:
Customizing this is still pretty complex, even when we're working with CRM Only information like leads.

- Add a “Convert to Customer Lead” option within the 'Vendor Lead Record'
- Insert a Customer Lead, update all the activity and opportunity references
- Delete the original vendor lead

Title: Transaction Lines - Ability to Load pictures to Line Items
Description: Customer sells custom designs and was wondering if they can upload, or paste  a picture(s) into the Line Items grid, making it visible on the customer portal and PDF.
Solution:
Technically, this is quite simple; however, how this information is then presented to the customer will increase scope. 

In general there are a couple of ways to show information:

eg. Customer can see the # of attachments associated with a line
- When they click the line, the attachment can be expanded
- This could be done on screen or in a popup.

Title: Estimate Approval - Deposit and Tax Collection
Description:
Customer takes 100% deposit at the estimate level and prefers Method's Estimate+ Deposit workflow. 

However, taxes are excluded from deposits normally. 

Can we customize a work around where the full amount, including taxes, are taken in as a deposit?
Solution:
Yes; this can be customized. 

However, it's important to consider that taxes generally are finalized in QuickBooks. 
So changes will need to be saved and sync'd before finalized taxes are visible for deposit flows.

Title: Support All-Day Events for Recurring Work Orders
Description:
Enable the 'All-Day' option for recurring work orders, similar to the functionality already available for one-time work orders.

Current Limitation:
While one-time work orders can be marked as 'All-Day,' recurring work orders require manual entry of start and end times (e.g., 12:00 AM to 11:59 PM), which is tedious and error-prone.

Proposed Solution:

Extend the existing 'All-Day' checkbox to recurring work orders.

When selected:

The system should default to a 12:00 AM – 11:59 PM time span.

Time fields should be hidden or locked for clarity.

Ensure compatibility with calendar views and dispatch features.

Benefits:

Streamlines setup of recurring full-day work orders.

Reduces friction for teams scheduling daily or multi-day site visits.

Keeps scheduling consistent across one-time and recurring events.
Solution: This is customizable; as all day appointments are just a checkbox on the activity - we can likely update all activities in a recurrence to be all day.

Title: Email Improvement - The ability to delay the sending of emails / queue up emails / cancel emails in progress.
Description:
A customer accidentally triggered a routine that attempted to send over 10,000 emails, many of which had the subject line “A New Web Lead.” These emails immediately entered the Processing state in the email queue. The customer reached out hoping to stop them, but by the time the issue was identified, all emails had already been picked up for delivery. There was no option to cancel, delay, or manage the queue, resulting in a significant issue.

Introduce enhancements to email handling within Method to provide better control over email sending, especially in high-volume or accidental send scenarios. Requested capabilities include:

Email Delay Option: Ability to introduce a delay (e.g., 5–30 minutes) before emails are processed and sent, allowing time to review or cancel.

Email Queuing: Emails triggered by routines or bulk actions should be clearly visible in a queue with status indicators (e.g., Pending, Processing, Sent).

Cancel in Progress: Ability to cancel or remove emails that are still in the queue and have not yet been picked up for delivery.

Admin Controls: Option for internal teams to intervene and stop queued emails upon customer request.
Solution: At this time this cannot be customized; however, it would be great for testing customization or just having a quick kill-switch when customizations may go awry.

Title: Work Order Improvement - SMS Notification for Assigned Work Orders
Description:
Summary:
Send an SMS notification to a user when a new work order is assigned to them.

Use Case:
Field technicians and staff often rely on real-time alerts to manage their day. Email notifications can be delayed or overlooked, especially while on the road or at a job site. SMS ensures immediate visibility when a new work order is assigned.

Desired Functionality:

Trigger: A new work order is assigned to a user (via dropdown or assigned user field).

Action: Automatically send an SMS to the user’s mobile number on file.

Message Template (example):
'Hi [FirstName], you’ve been assigned a new work order: [WorkOrderName] scheduled for [ScheduledDateTime]. Please check Method for full details.'

Optional: Allow customization of SMS content and opt-in/out at the user level.

Impact:

Faster response time from techs

Reduced missed assignments

Improved communication between dispatch and field teams
Solution:
Relatively small;

1.) Create a button / button menu option
2.) Generate url
3.) Notify the user 

(Optional) on the user table keep track of their cell phone number

Title: Cases App Improvement  - Inbox Monitoring & Auto Case Creation for the Cases App
Description:
Use Case: Aligning Method’s Cases App functionality with modern support workflows (e.g., Zendesk-style ticket automation)

Summary
Customers managing support through email want the ability for Method CRM to monitor a shared inbox (e.g., support@company.com) and automatically:

Create a new Case when a new support email arrives.

Attach replies to the existing Case based on email thread/subject.

Optionally, assign the Case based on routing rules (keywords, customer, etc.).

This is a key feature in Zendesk and similar platforms, and it allows teams to manage support more efficiently without manual entry or toggling between email and CRM.

Current Limitation
There is no native integration between email inboxes (e.g., Gmail, Outlook) and the Method Cases App.

Support staff must manually create Cases from emails, risking delays or lost tickets.

There’s no automated way to thread conversations or track cases based on email responses.

Proposed Functionality
Inbox Monitoring Integration:

Connect to a specified shared inbox (Gmail, Outlook, etc.).

Monitor for new inbound emails (IMAP or API-based connection).

Automated Case Creation:

Create a new Case in Method upon receipt of a new email.

Auto-populate fields:

Subject → Case Title

Email Body → Case Description

Sender’s Email → Linked Contact/Customer

Attachments → Stored in Case record

Threading / Updates:

Recognize replies (via email headers or subject line tracking).

Add reply content as comments or updates to the existing Case.

Optional Routing Rules (Phase 2):

Auto-assign Cases based on:

Customer type

Subject keywords

SLAs or time-of-day triggers

Why This Matters
Creates a seamless support workflow, reducing the need for manual case creation.

Prevents dropped support issues and ensures case accountability.

Matches industry standards set by Zendesk, Freshdesk, etc.—key in competitive sales conversations.

Impact Potential
High relevance for customers offering support services.

Adds significant value to the Cases App, making it a more complete solution.

Could unlock future enhancements around automated SLAs, email templates, and response analytics.
Solution: At this time this cannot be customized.

Title: API Integration - MRPEasy Integration with Method:CRM
Description:
Summary:
Request to integrate MRPEasy, a cloud-based MRP software for small manufacturers, with Method:CRM to support seamless operations for manufacturing-focused customers using QuickBooks.

Use Case:
Many Method:CRM users in the manufacturing sector rely on MRPEasy for production planning, inventory control, and shop floor management. These users would benefit from a native or API-based integration that allows the following:

Desired Features:

Sync key records: Customers, inventory items, sales orders, and purchase orders between MRPEasy and Method.

Two-way sync for production status updates: View job status, material availability, and work orders in Method.

Integrated quoting & planning: Initiate production quotes in Method that flow into MRPEasy’s BOM and planning modules.

Sync completed jobs to QuickBooks via Method: Close the loop between MRPEasy → Method → QuickBooks.

Benefit:
Improves operational efficiency by giving sales and ops teams full visibility across CRM, MRP, and accounting workflows—removing the need for double entry and manual updates.
Solution: Advanced Scoping and Project Review Required.

Title: API Integration - AmpTab Integration with Method:CRM
Description:
Summary:
Request to integrate AmpTab, an omni-channel B2B/B2C sales and marketing platform, with Method:CRM to unify customer engagement, lead tracking, and transactional data across platforms.

Use Case:
AmpTab users manage customer outreach, digital catalogs, and order capture through tablets, kiosks, and mobile channels. Method users want to centralize this activity to gain a full 360° view of the customer journey and streamline lead-to-sale workflows.

Desired Features:

Lead & Contact Sync: Sync leads and customer records from AmpTab into Method's Contacts and Opportunities tables.

Order Import: Automatically import B2B/B2C orders placed through AmpTab into Method as Sales Orders or Invoices.

Activity Timeline Integration: Sync marketing and sales touchpoints from AmpTab (e.g., presentations viewed, catalogs browsed, reps assigned) into Method Activities.

Multi-channel Attribution: Track which channel (in-person, kiosk, digital catalog, etc.) originated or influenced a deal inside Method.

Catalog Sync (Optional): Pull or push product data from Method to AmpTab to keep both systems aligned.

Benefit:
This integration bridges the gap between in-person and digital selling, giving sales and operations teams complete visibility into prospect engagement and order activity—within the CRM they already use to manage pipeline and post-sale workflows.
Solution:
Advanced Scoping is Required. 

Any ecommerce integration can be complex in nature with a lot of moving parts.

Title: Email Campaigns - Add Contact Record ID Column to Email Campaign Analytics Grid
Description:
It would be so benefical to have Contact’s record id in this grid as this way customer can easily export and then Import them back with any tags he needed. 

Example - customer wanted to export all “blocked” emails on Email campaign Record id- 255 and tag them to resend the email campaign.
Solution:
Should be a relatively simple customization; however, remember that blocked contacts are generally blocked for 2 key reasons:

1.) Unable to deliver to that inbox
2.) Repeated bounces

Title: Gmail Custom Server - View Sent Emails - Events does not show any status for CC/BCC recipients
Description:
Example- If we are using Gmail server and send out emails, I know we have limited visibility and can only see status like “sent” but it only shows for “To” email. 

It does not show anything for BCC or CC emails. It would be good to have that show up for us to troubleshoot further and advise the customers if emails were sent or not.
Solution: Cannot be customized

Title: Work Order List - Changing the Default View - Date Period - Date Range
Description:
Would it be possible to introduce a customizable option for users to select their default date range on Work Orders—similar to how we can star a default view?

Many businesses do not operate on a daily basis, and requiring an extra click to update the date range every time they access the system adds unnecessary friction. 

This enhancement would go a long way in improving user experience for those with weekly, biweekly, or monthly operational cadences.
Solution:
This could be customized; where each user could have their own preference for the date view. 

On Screen load we can make sure this view is set. 
That said this is a bit complicated as it's not a traditional view but a dropdown that is driving behavior in a related grid.

So some customization may be required to make this predictable by user.

Title: Sidebar Improvement  - Ability to Customize Sidebar Color Scheme for Visual Differentiation Between Method Accounts
Description:
This customer manages two separate Method accounts is looking for a way to visually differentiate between them to reduce confusion among users. Currently, both accounts have the same sidebar color scheme, which includes the menu that displays items like Dashboard, Invoices, Work Orders, etc.

The customer’s goal is to quickly identify which account they're in by visually customizing the sidebar or menu color scheme.
Solution: Cannot be Customized.

Title: Editable Grid Improvement - Make formulas available on Report Designer (Interoperability)
Description:
Ideally, it would be great if Editable Grid Formulas - were some how interoperable with Web Report Designer. 

Otherwise, we're basically trying to use two different formula builders to generate the same results in multiple locations.
Solution:
This cannot be customized at this time; the purpose of the formula builder is to provide quick calculations on the fly that could be exported using the Grid Export. 

However, for a better experience and interoperability you have two options:

1.) Re-create the formulas using a calculated field
2.) Create a custom field and store the information to the table; rather than just calculating it on the fly in a Grid.

Title: Editable Grid Improvement - The ability to nest formulas within eachother (Multiple Formulas)
Description:
I would like the ability to be able to use formula created fields on editable grid within other formulas. 

For example:
I want to calculate the change in two field:

 Formula Column C = Field A - Field B 

I cannot use Formula Column C, to then determine Formula Column D. 

 Formula Column D = Formula Column C * 12
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time; each formula column would need to have the full calculation required.

Title: Subscription Improvement - Subscription amount due message/banner on user screen
Description:
Currently, when there is an overdue payment on an account, all users see a banner message on their Method account. The admin would prefer that this message be visible only to the billing contact or admin users.

Can this be customized to restrict visibility to just those specific users.
Solution:
This cannot be customized; however, it's important to understand that not all billing contacts login to Method on a regular basis. 

Having this appear as a banner, ensure that users that are using the system are aware and can take action. 

Furthermore, all billing contacts - also receive an email notification that a payment could not be processed.

Title: Status.method.me Updates - Subscription or Notification Users via Email or Push Notification During System Issues or Outages
Description: A customer has suggested that Method should proactively notify users via email or push notification when the platform is experiencing issues or outages. This would help users stay informed without having to contact support or check the system status manually.
Solution: This cannot be customized; however, we should consider a subscription flow for our status.method.me page. To ensure users are always up to date on outages or incidents on the platform.

Title: App Routine Improvement - Ability to view action set from Revision History
Description:
Currently, when reviewing the revision history of an app routine, there are limited options:
1.) Publish

Ideally, we would like a view-only version of revisions to access action sets without creating new drafts to review the action set. 

Similar to the read-only view of action sets on screens - when a screen has been published / archived.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Email Improvement - Auto-Convert Large Email Attachments to URL
Description:
Summary:
When sending emails with attachments through Method, if the file size exceeds the allowable limit, the email currently fails or sends without the attachment—without any warning to the user. This creates confusion and a poor experience for both sender and recipient.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a fallback mechanism where, if an attachment exceeds the file size limit:

The attachment is automatically uploaded to a secure, temporary file-hosting URL (e.g., via Method file storage or integrated cloud storage).

The email body is updated to include a secure link to the file (e.g., “View file here: [link]”).

The user receives a confirmation that the file was sent via link instead of attachment.

Additional Enhancements:

Display a warning or file size indicator when uploading large files.

Option to always send files via link, regardless of size.

Benefits:

Prevents silent failures or missing attachments.

Enhances reliability and user confidence.

Improves recipient experience, especially for large or critical documents.
Solution:
This can be customized:

1.) Assign a value to action result (Total Attachment Size)
2.) Loop through attachments that are set to email
3.) Sum all the sizes together
4.) If greater than 15 - 20MB warn the user it's too big. 

Alternatively, invidual files that exceed 10MB can also trigger a warning for example.

Title: Cases app - Automatically Notify the Contact on any updates
Description: The customer wants the ability to automatically notify the contact whenever there are updates or changes to the case details.
Solution:
Yes, this is quite simple:

1.) Enable notify contact by default on cases.
2.) Make all activities, linked to cases default to 'external' and notify contact when an activity linked to a case is created or updated.

Title: API Integration - Integration with Korona POS System
Description:
Objective:
Create a native integration between Method:CRM and Korona POS to allow sales data and customer information to flow directly into Method. This will enable cleaner and more accurate data to be pushed to QuickBooks from Method, improving the reliability of the overall workflow.

Problem:
Currently, Korona POS only supports a one-way integration to QuickBooks. However, this direct sync often results in incomplete or inaccurate customer records. Additionally, businesses lack visibility into sales data within their CRM and are unable to utilize Method’s customization, automation, and reporting capabilities on POS transactions.

Proposed Solution:
Develop a Method-Korona POS integration that:

Pulls sales data from Korona into Method in near real-time or at scheduled intervals.

Maps customer information correctly, ensuring deduplication and clean record creation or updates.

Enables users to view, report, and act on Korona sales data directly from Method.

Pushes finalized transactions to QuickBooks from Method with full context and integrity.

Benefits:

Improves the accuracy of customer data in QuickBooks.

Enables better CRM workflows and automations (e.g., follow-ups, reporting, segmentation).

Reduces manual reconciliation or API custom builds, saving time and reducing errors.

Centralizes sales and customer activity for better decision-making.

Current Workaround:
A custom API connection is required to bridge this gap, typically quoted at 10–15 hours of development time. This results in higher onboarding friction and additional support overhead.

.
Solution: A custom API connection is required to bridge this gap, typically quoted at 10–15 hours of development time. This results in higher onboarding friction and additional support overhead.

Title: Contacts Management Improvement - Ability to Assign Users to Contacts
Description:
Objective:
Enable CRM users to assign specific sales reps to individual contacts (not just entities/customers) in Method:CRM to support account structures with multiple stakeholders or buyers.

Use Case:
A customer in the procurement industry, focused on supporting government clients, often works with multiple authorized buyers under a single customer account. To streamline communications and manage relationships effectively, they need to assign dedicated sales reps to each contact within an entity.

Proposed Workflow Customization:

Add a Sales Rep (User) field to the Contacts table.

Build UI elements (dropdown, assignment screen, filter views) to allow for:

Manual assignment of reps to contacts.

Visibility of which rep owns which contact.

Optional: Trigger automated workflows or follow-ups based on rep-contact assignment.

Reporting views that show rep activity or pipelines by assigned contact, not just entity.

Benefits:

Supports more granular account management in multi-contact accounts.

Increases accountability and rep visibility into their contact-specific pipelines.

Improves team alignment on which buyers they’re responsible for.

Essential for industries (like government procurement) with layered purchasing structures.

Current Limitation:
Method’s current user assignment logic is at the entity level. Contacts are not individually assignable without a custom table or workaround.
Solution:
This should be a pretty simple customization as most of the CRM and Configuration already revolves around contacts.

It is important to note that changes will need to be applied to view filters to ensure compatibility with permissions.

Title: System Page Improvement - API Improvement - User Interface for Managing REST API Requests and Limits
Description:
Request Summary:
Customers experiencing rate limiting on Method’s REST API have no proactive visibility or control over their API usage. One such customer’s website (managed by Sitecrafting) has been down since 1PM PST due to hitting Method’s API rate limits. This disruption has impacted their operations despite being a long-time customer with relatively stable traffic.

Current Challenge:

No user-facing interface to monitor API usage.

Customers are unable to anticipate or mitigate rate-limiting issues.

The rate limit thresholds are not transparent.

No automated alerts when approaching API limits.

Proposed Solution:
Build a dedicated API Usage Dashboard within Method, accessible to Admin users, with the following features:

Real-time usage tracking: Show total API calls made in the current rate limit window.

Rate limit thresholds: Display the current thresholds (e.g., 5000 calls/hour).

Historical usage graphs: Let users review call volumes over time to identify trends or spikes.

Webhook alerts or email notifications: Allow users to receive alerts when usage approaches 80%/90%/100%.

Allowlist management: Enable advanced users to request temporary limit increases or whitelist trusted services (subject to Method approval).

Benefits:

Proactive issue prevention for customers.

Reduced support burden due to fewer emergency tickets related to rate limits.

Increased transparency and trust for technical teams and developers integrating with Method.

Supporting Details:

Sitecrafting (customer’s dev team) is available to collaborate on this issue.

Traffic has increased, but the issue has occurred during standard usage as well.
Solution: At this time this cannot be customized; however, the API Header does report limits and reset times.

Title: Date Function Improvement - A new date format to be added to the current list of display options.
Description:
Raschelle has requested a new date format to be added to the current list of display options. She is specifically looking for the format:

'July 8, 2025' (MMMM DD, YYYY)

Currently, the closest available options are:

'Long Date': 'Tuesday, July 8, 2025'

However, none of these allow for the full date to be displayed without the day of the week, which is what the user is looking for.
Solution:
In theory, this could be manually customized and put together as a character function.

That said,  further flexibility on the date function would be ideal.

Title: Create invoice from multiple Purchase Orders (batch)
Description:
Client Context:
Customer operates as a broker within the mercantile industry. Their business model involves facilitating product purchases between suppliers and buyers. They do not fulfill orders directly but instead broker deals and earn commissions based on purchase orders they help bridge.

Requested Enhancement:
The customer is seeking a way to track and manage brokered purchase orders and generate commission-based invoices summarizing their involvement. The goal is to:

Aggregate multiple purchase orders they have brokered.

Create a batch invoice to the end customer reflecting commission amounts due.

Track which POs have already been included in past commission invoices to avoid duplication.


Commission Invoice Output:

Commission invoice template clearly itemizing each PO, supplier, total amount, and the calculated commission.


Reporting Add-on (Optional):

Broker commission report tracking total commissions earned, outstanding commissions, and invoice history.
Solution:
Certainly possible; however, there will need to be a customer designation on each PO or PO Line. (Small)

Everything else could flow and be combined into a single invoice per customer.
Commissions and additional work around POs will need to be scoped as the nature of this project seems large.

Title: Date Picker Improvement - Filter on Date Picker - Ability to Grey Out Specific Dates on the Date Selection Object
Description: The customer would like the ability to disable or grey out specific dates within the Date Selection Object in Method. This feature would help prevent users from selecting unavailable or restricted dates (e.g., holidays, blackout periods, or system maintenance days).
Solution: Great feature request, at this time this is not possible; however, on change you can reset the date and provide the user with a message that the date is invalid.

Title: Payment Gateway Improvement - Setting Up Primary and Backup Payment Gateways
Description:
Customer is looking to connect two payment gateways. 

Can we set one as the primary and the other as a backup? Essentially, if the primary gateway fails, the payment should automatically route through the backup gateway.
Solution:
This cannot be customized; however, with Method Pay this might be something that's possible:

1.) Setup to use Method Pay
2.) If Method Pay fails, roll back to Shuttle. 

Thanks for submitting this we will review it.

Title: Google Calendar Improvement - Integration with RSVP Tracking
Description:
Cameron would like the ability to send Google Calendar meeting invitations directly from Method, which would:

Connect to their Google Calendar

Allow them to send event invitations to clients from within Method

Let clients accept the invite and automatically add it to their own calendars (without requiring the user to log in to Google)

Allow the user to track RSVP responses (accepted/declined status) from within Method


Use Case:
This feature would streamline the scheduling process by keeping everything centralized in Method, eliminating the need to switch platforms. It would also help users track client engagement and attendance more efficiently.
Solution:
This cannot be customized; however: 


1.)Invite Client Without Google Login Requirement
- Clients receive a standard Google Calendar invite by email (ICS format compatible).
- Clients can RSVP (Yes / No / Maybe) without needing a Google account.

2.) RSVP Tracking
