Title: Improved  Web to Lead Form Compatibility with Squarespace
Description:
This customer attempted to embed a Method Web to Lead form on their Squarespace website using the steps outlined in the Help Center article.

They placed the  in a Codeblock object, and the preview appeared correct, but the live screen had the Codeblock 'hiding' the footer elements of the page, and wasn't responsive to mobile formatting.
Solution:
This isn't a limit of Squarespace, but a limitation inherent to iFrames. An iframe loads content from another source, but isn't aware of the content itself, so if you set the height to 100% it becomes very short. You could try using a VW (viewport width) value for the height rather than PX, which may make it more responsive, but only to the screen width rather than the content.


https://forum.squarespace.com/topic/328148-iframe-how-do-make-the-content-responsive/

Title: API Integration between Method:CRM and Basecamp
Description:
Many teams use Method to manage customer relationships and Basecamp to manage internal and client-facing projects. Currently, there is no direct integration between the two platforms. This results in:

Duplicated efforts: tasks created in Method need to be manually recreated in Basecamp.
Miscommunication: project and task updates are siloed across tools.
Reduced efficiency: no centralized view of customer/project activity.

✅ Proposed Solution
Enable a native or third-party-powered integration between Method:CRM and Basecamp to sync:
Contacts/Customers from Method → Clients in Basecamp (or linked projects).
Opportunities or Work Orders in Method → Basecamp projects.
Activities or Tasks in Method → To-dos in Basecamp (with status sync).
Comments/Notes in Method → Basecamp discussions or message threads.
Optionally, pull Basecamp updates back into Method for visibility within the CRM.

🔗 Use Cases
A CSM closes a deal in Method → automatically creates a project in Basecamp with predefined milestones.
A sales rep wants to track project delivery progress post-sale → can view Basecamp updates within Method.
A customer requests changes via Basecamp → logged back in Method as a customer interaction or task.

⚙️ Technical Considerations
Basecamp 3 and 4 both offer APIs.
Method's REST API can be used to sync objects bi-directionally.
Webhooks can enable real-time sync, or a periodic sync (e.g., hourly) can be used for simplicity.

💡 Benefits
Streamlined workflows between CRM and project management.
Greater visibility into post-sale customer engagement.
Improved internal communication and reduced duplication of work.
Better customer experience through faster handoffs and task accountability.
Solution:
This could be a relatively large integration depending on the scope. 
Best to recommend DEP and Project hours for accurate scoping, development, and support.

Title: Gravity Forms – Web-to-Lead Integration with Method (via Zapier or Native Enhancements)
Description:
One of our prospects is currently using a web form built on Gravity Forms and wants to replicate the same dynamic, multi-step experience as part of their Method CRM lead intake process. Their existing Gravity Form includes conditional logic where different questions and sections appear based on prior selections, offering a highly interactive and customized intake workflow.

Request:
Can we create a web-to-lead flow in Method CRM that mimics the dynamic conditional logic of their existing Gravity Forms setup?

Goal:
Enable a branded, interactive lead capture experience that sends data to Method CRM, potentially triggering downstream actions like opportunity and estimate creation.
Solution:
Two Integration Options
🔧 Option 1: Zapier + Gravity Forms
If visual branding and conditional logic are a priority, we recommend using the Gravity Forms + Zapier route.

Flow:

Form submission in Gravity Forms (with conditional logic)

Zapier catches submission and:

Creates a Lead in Method

Creates an Opportunity with supporting data

Optionally, creates an Estimate based on the form data

Pros:

Preserves the branded user experience

Fast to deploy

Minimal configuration inside Method

Cons:

External dependency (Zapier)

Less visibility inside Method until after submission

🔨 Option 2: Native Method Web-to-Lead Page (Custom Build)
Method can replicate much of the dynamic behavior using Show/Hide actions within the Web-to-Lead page.

Flow:

Form dynamically reveals sections based on user input

Data maps to:

Web-to-Lead table

Opportunity table

Estimate table (if applicable)

Pros:

Fully native to Method

All data visible from the start

Can trigger native workflows (e.g., follow-up reminders, status changes)

Cons:

Requires custom work to handle conditional logic

Complex forms may be harder to maintain

Branding/custom styling is more limited

Recommendation:
If the customer’s current Gravity Form is critical to their branding and conversion funnel, Zapier is the faster path with full flexibility. We can support automatic Estimate creation and Opportunity management through the Zapier integration.

If long-term ownership and tighter integration with Method is preferred, we can scope a custom Web-to-Lead form with conditional logic and native record creation.

Title: Watch - Subscribe to updates - Ability to Share Activities and Add Multiple Watchers - Ability to Watch Transactions, Activities

Activities can only be assigned to one user at a time.

There is no built-in support for activity “watchers” or shared ownership.
Solution:
Add support for multiple assignees or watchers on an activity, allowing users to receive notifications and track updates.

Introduce a “shared with” field or watcher list on the activity screen for visibility.

Permissions model to control edit rights vs. view-only access for watchers.

Notification settings so each user can choose if and how they want to be notified of changes to shared activities.

Title: KPI Reporting - Revenue Churn & Revenue Retention KPI Tracking via QuickBooks Invoicing
Description:
Summary:
We are requesting a new feature within Method to track Revenue Churn and Revenue Retention as key performance indicators (KPIs), based on invoice data synced from QuickBooks.

Problem:
Currently, Method does not offer a built-in way to measure customer-level revenue changes over time. This limits our ability to track expansion, contraction, and churn at a revenue level. While we can see individual invoices, there's no automated mechanism to summarize monthly recurring revenue (MRR), lost revenue, or retained revenue based on historical invoicing data.

Proposed Solution:
Enhance Method’s reporting and dashboarding features to calculate and display the following KPIs, leveraging synced QuickBooks invoice data:

Revenue Retention Rate (Gross and Net)

Based on revenue from existing customers across selected time periods

Gross: excludes expansion

Net: includes upsells/expansion revenue

Revenue Churn Rate

Revenue lost from existing customers who stop purchasing or reduce their spend

Identifiable by comparing invoiced amounts from one month to the next

Customer Cohort Revenue Tracking

Group customers by the month of first invoice or onboarding

Track how their revenue changes over time

Optional: MRR/ARR Trend Analysis

Aggregate monthly recurring revenue (if appropriate for business model)

Visualize changes due to churn, contraction, and expansion

Technical Notes:

All calculations should rely on historical invoice records synced from QuickBooks.

Ability to filter by date range, customer class, rep, or product/service category would enhance utility.

Ideally, provide both table and chart visualizations.

Use Case:
This feature would allow Customer Success, Sales, and Finance teams to proactively identify accounts at risk, measure the financial impact of retention efforts, and optimize customer lifecycle strategies.
Solution:
A project of this nature is pretty large and should be broken down into key blocks that are digestible. Here are the 5 blocks I recommend before visualizing as a chart / dashboard. 


Block 1: Data Foundation – Sync & Structure
Goal: Ensure that all necessary QuickBooks invoicing data is available and query-ready in Method.

✅ Sync historical and ongoing invoice data from QuickBooks (including customer, date, line items, amounts, and payment status).

✅ Normalize data format for consistent use in reporting (e.g., recurring vs. one-time invoices).

✅ Add support for tagging/classifying customers (e.g., industry, rep, subscription status).

Block 2: Monthly Revenue Snapshot Engine
Goal: Build a reliable mechanism to calculate monthly revenue per customer.

📆 Create logic to group and sum invoices by:

Customer

Month

Class/Rep (if applicable)

🔄 Store snapshots for each month to enable comparison (e.g., April vs. May).

Block 3: Churn & Retention Calculations
Goal: Calculate revenue churn and retention metrics at a customer and cohort level.

📉 Revenue Churn Rate = Revenue lost from existing customers / Total revenue from previous period

📈 Revenue Retention Rate = Revenue from existing customers / Total revenue from previous period

📊 Support both:

Gross Revenue Retention (ignores upsells)

Net Revenue Retention (includes upsells)

Block 4: Cohort Tracking & Segmentation
Goal: Enable tracking revenue changes over time by customer groups.

👥 Group customers by onboarding date (first invoice or CRM entry)

📈 Monitor how revenue from each cohort evolves over 3/6/12 months

🔍 Segment filters (industry, rep, source, etc.)

Title: Transaction Improvement - Commission Calculation - Automated Sales Rep Credit Assignment

Ensure sales reports and dashboards reflect accurate, shared attribution without additional manual adjustments.

Reduces administrative overhead, ensures fairness and accuracy in sales performance tracking, and provides clear visibility into contributions in collaborative deals.

Customizing this is quite simple we would effectively need to track what sales reps are involved in a deal.

Title: Contact Dropdown Improvement - Contacts Improvement - Enhanced Contact Filtering for Activities and Opportunities

Overview:
Customers need a more intuitive and streamlined experience when selecting contacts for Activities and Opportunities. Currently, the contact dropdown includes both parent customers and subcustomers/jobs, making it difficult to quickly identify the appropriate contact—particularly when subcustomers are not relevant to the workflow.

The current contact list shows both customers and subcustomers, causing confusion.

Attempts to exclude subcustomers via grid settings or list filtering have not worked.

Advanced Search and Companies App were proposed as workarounds but are not optimal for all use cases.

A customer is willing to explore customization but would prefer native functionality.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a configurable filter setting for the contact dropdown used in Activities and Opportunities, allowing users to:

Toggle visibility between parent customers, subcustomers, or both.

Apply default filters based on record type or customer hierarchy.

Easily customize the filter logic via UI without scripting.

Impact:
This improvement would:

Reduce user confusion during contact selection.

Increase data accuracy by minimizing incorrect associations.

Streamline workflows for users dealing primarily with parent customers.

Customer Engagement:
A free customization hour was offered to the customer to explore a temporary solution, but a native feature would improve usability for all users without requiring custom work.
Solution:
Customization of this is quite simple. 

1.) Customize the screen and select the dropdown
2.) Add a filter for is ParentFullname = Empty


That said this is applicable if the customer only wants to see the Top Parent in a Sub-customer chain.

Title: Date Function Improvement - First Day of the Week
Description: Currently, the “First Day of the Week” function considers Monday as the first day. The customer would like the option to set Sunday as the first day of the week, or for Sunday to be the default.

Both of these work arounds feel sufficient.

Title: Customer Onboarding App -Integrated Customer Onboarding Workflow
Description:
Summary
Add a dedicated onboarding module within the CRM to guide new customers through a step-by-step process, track progress, and ensure all required actions are completed for a smooth implementation. The onboarding process should be configurable by the business, fully integrated with CRM data, and visible to both internal teams and (optionally) the customer via a portal.

Problem / Opportunity
Currently, onboarding is handled manually through emails, spreadsheets, or ad-hoc CRM tasks. This results in:

Missed or delayed onboarding steps.

Limited visibility for sales, customer success, and implementation teams.

No consistent measurement of onboarding completion time or bottlenecks.

Customers not having a clear view of their own progress, leading to uncertainty and more support inquiries.

Objectives
Standardize the onboarding experience across customers.

Increase visibility for all stakeholders (internal & external).

Automate reminders and task assignments.

Measure onboarding efficiency with analytics.

Core Design in CRM
1. Onboarding Templates

Admins can create predefined onboarding sequences (e.g., “Small Business,” “Enterprise,” “Custom Implementation”).

Each sequence contains steps with metadata:

Task title & description

Assigned owner (internal role or customer contact)

Due date offset (relative to start date)

Dependencies (step B starts only after step A completes)

Required/optional status

2. Onboarding Tracker

A dedicated tab in the customer record showing:

Step list with completion status

Responsible party

Due dates & overdue flags

Notes & file attachments per step

Visual progress bar for percentage completion.

3. Internal & Customer Views

Internal CRM users see all steps, notes, and assignments.

Optional Customer Portal View:

Branded portal access for customers to track their onboarding progress.

Customers can mark steps as done, upload files, or leave comments.

4. Automation & Notifications

Trigger onboarding automatically upon deal closure or status change.

Automated reminders:

Internal: Slack/CRM task notifications for assigned staff.

Customer: Email or portal notifications.

Conditional branching (e.g., if a customer chooses a certain add-on, add specific steps).

5. Reporting & Analytics

Dashboard for:

Average time-to-onboard

Steps most often delayed

Onboarding completion rate

Filter by customer segment, template, or account manager.

Example Workflow in CRM
Deal Closed-Won → triggers “Standard Onboarding” template.

CRM auto-creates all onboarding tasks tied to the customer record.

CSM logs into CRM, sees “Onboarding” tab with tasks assigned to self and customer contacts.

Customer gets portal invite showing their required steps.

CRM sends reminders before each due date and alerts when overdue.

Onboarding dashboard shows real-time progress, allowing management to spot bottlenecks.

Benefits
Faster onboarding → quicker time to value for customers.

Fewer missed steps → higher customer satisfaction.

Data-driven insights into onboarding efficiency.

Stronger alignment between Sales, CS, and Implementation teams.
Solution:
Certainly possible; depending on the level of configuration the end user needs the scope of this project could be reduced. 

As a pre-requisite it would be ideal to have what communications need to go out, what are the steps required in the journey, and how can we follow-up.

A light-weight version of this could be designed simply using Opportunity Stages together with some customization to trigger the next step / setup the next followup.

Title: AI Improvement - AI-Powered Email Composition

Title: How to remove/merge customers in Method

Title: Get Notified, View, and Manage Failed Emails

⸻

⸻

Title: Ability to track end customers on a distributor invoice
Description:
Enable businesses who sell through a single distributor (e.g., Manitoba Liquor & Lotteries) to track and manage relationships with end customers (bars, restaurants, retailers) inside MethodCRM, while maintaining the distributor as the primary QuickBooks customer.

Some industries (breweries, distilleries, wholesalers, etc.) have only one billing customer (the distributor).

However, the real customer relationships — the ones driving recurring sales — are with the end customers who order through the distributor.

They would like the ability so that when entering r sales/orders under MBLL, they are able to track which end customers received which items (ex. “4 kegs → split across 3 pubs”),

From there, build purchase histories & workflows around those end customers (so you know who reorders what, and when to follow up).
Solution:
Yes — Method can be configured to handle this scenario, but it requires some customization because out-of-the-box QuickBooks + Method is built around a one-to-one customer/order relationship. Here’s how it could work:

1. Keep Distributor as the Billing Customer

In QuickBooks, Manitoba Liquor & Lotteries (MBLL) remains the only billing customer.

All invoices, payments, and accounting stay tied to MBLL, so your books stay clean.

2. Create End-Customer Records Inside Method

In Method, you’d add end-customer profiles (bars, restaurants, pubs, retailers).

These would not sync back to QuickBooks as 'customers' — they’d live only in Method CRM.

You’d be able to track contacts, addresses, preferences, notes, reorder reminders, etc.

3. Split Orders Across End Customers

When you create a sales order or invoice for MBLL in Method, you’d add a custom table/field to specify how that order was split among end customers.

Example: Invoice to MBLL = 4 kegs. Inside Method, you record “2 → Pub A, 1 → Pub B, 1 → Pub C.”

This can be implemented as a line-item allocation grid or a sub-record linked to the invoice.

4. Build Histories & Workflows

Over time, each end-customer record will have a purchase history (even though they’re not paying you directly).

That history can drive:

Reorder reminders (Pub A usually reorders 2 kegs every 3 weeks).

Follow-ups & campaigns (automated emails/calls before they’re due to run out).

Reports by end-customer (sales by pub, reorder frequency, product mix, etc.).

5. Benefits

Sales team can manage true relationships (end pubs/restaurants) without breaking QuickBooks’ accounting rules.

Distributor (MBLL) remains the sole “financial customer,” but Method gives you a CRM view of the real demand drivers.

This setup can scale to breweries, distilleries, wholesalers, or any industry with a distributor model

Strengthens outbound reporting (connects “source of lead” to revenue impact).

Title: CCPA Compliance Enhancements – Transparency & Customer Controls

Rights and obligations for customers and their end users.

Subprocessor list and data storage location transparency.

Generate a compliance report confirming fulfillment.

Title: Budget Tracking Integration for Invoices - Combine Invoices with Customer Budget Tracking

Title: API Integration - Close.io + Method:CRM Integration — Seamless Sync Between Sales Pipeline, Invoices, and Commissions
Description:
Summary:

Integrate Close.io (for CRM, pipeline, and deal tracking) with Method:CRM (for invoicing, QuickBooks sync, and commission management), enabling end-to-end visibility from sales opportunity → closed deal → invoiced revenue → commission payout.


Problem Statement:

Sales teams using Close.io love its simplicity and calling/email workflows, but once a deal closes, there’s no built-in way to track invoicing, payments, or commissions.

Meanwhile, Method:CRM is deeply connected to QuickBooks, handling the financial and operational side — but lacks native CRM depth for sales engagement workflows.

Companies that use both tools currently have to export deal data manually, reconcile invoices and commissions offline, and then reimport results into QuickBooks or spreadsheets — a repetitive and error-prone process.
Solution:
Create a native or connector-based integration between Close.io and Method:CRM to automate post-sale workflows.

Key features:

Deal-to-Invoice Sync:

Automatically generate a Method:CRM Invoice when a Close.io Opportunity/Deal is marked “Closed Won.”

Map Close.io deal fields (Amount, Customer, Product, Close Date) to Method invoice fields.

Two-way sync for invoice status (e.g., “Paid” updates Close.io deal record).

Commission Tracking:

Allow Method:CRM to calculate commissions based on Close.io deal data.

Commission rules configurable by rep, product, or margin.

Push commission summaries back to Close.io as notes or custom fields.

Budget & Revenue Reporting:

Roll-up of total invoiced revenue per rep or team, with QuickBooks-synced accuracy.

Real-time visibility into earned vs. paid commissions.

Option to track spend vs. customer or project budgets.

QuickBooks Integration (via Method):

Invoices created from Close.io sync automatically with QuickBooks through Method.

Reduces double data entry and keeps accounting aligned with sales.

Title: Enhanced Reporting Permissions — Allow Admins to Control Which Reports End Users Can See
Description:
Summary:

Give administrators fine-grained control over which reporting modules (e.g., Estimates, Invoices, Opportunities, Activities) are visible to each user or role.
By default, reporting access should be restricted, with admins explicitly granting visibility where needed.

Problem Statement:

Currently, reporting access in Method:CRM is broad — meaning if a user has permission to view data, they can often access related reports that expose sensitive financial or operational metrics (e.g., total invoice values, customer estimates, revenue by rep).

For many organizations, especially those with sales reps, contractors, or role-based data separation, this poses a data governance and confidentiality issue.
Admins need a way to limit what non-admin users can view within reporting dashboards and modules.
Solution:
Proposed Solution:

Introduce Admin-Configurable Reporting Visibility Controls that allow administrators to define which reports or report categories are visible to which users or roles.

Key capabilities:

Report-Level Permissions:

Ability to toggle visibility of individual reports (Invoices, Estimates, Opportunities, etc.) by role or user.

Default state: Hidden for all non-admins.

Category-Based Access:

Group reports by type (Sales, Financial, Activities, etc.) for simpler permission management.

Allow admins to bulk grant or restrict access.

Custom Role Profiles:

Admins can assign predefined permission templates (e.g., “Sales Rep,” “Consultant,” “Manager”) controlling report visibility.

Audit Trail:

Track when reporting permissions are changed and by whom.

Title: API Integration - Method:CRM + MessageDesk Integration — Unified Messaging for Invoices, Estimates, and Customer Updates
Description:
Summary:

Integrate MessageDesk with Method:CRM to allow users to send and receive SMS and email communications directly tied to customer records, invoices, and estimates.

This connection enables Method users to send automated messages (like payment reminders, appointment confirmations, or job status updates) while maintaining full sync with Method’s CRM and QuickBooks data.

Problem Statement:

Currently, Method:CRM users rely on manual communication through email or external tools. While Method handles customer data, estimates, and invoices, it lacks a native or integrated SMS communication channel for quick, transactional updates.

Users often need to remind customers about outstanding invoices, send estimates for approval, or confirm service appointments — all tasks that are perfect for MessageDesk’s texting automation.

Without an integration, these tasks require manual updates in both systems, leading to data silos and inconsistent communication logs.
Solution:
Create a two-way integration between MessageDesk and Method:CRM to unify CRM and messaging workflows.

Key Features:

Contact Sync:

Sync Method contacts (Customers, Leads, Vendors) with MessageDesk contact lists automatically.

Support custom fields (e.g., entity, project, job site, balance due).

Automated Messaging Triggers:

Send automated SMS or emails from MessageDesk when certain actions occur in Method (e.g., Estimate Created, Invoice Overdue, Payment Received).

Admins can define templates and triggers.

Two-Way Communication Log:

Messages sent or received in MessageDesk appear in Method’s Activity or Communication History for full visibility.

Option to attach message history to customer records.

Bulk Messaging:

Send broadcast messages to filtered Method lists (e.g., active customers, open invoices).

Ideal for service updates, promotions, or policy changes.

Template Library:

Predefined message templates for invoice reminders, job confirmations, and feedback requests.

Merge tags for personalization (e.g., Customer Name, Invoice Total, Due Date).

Title: Invoice Reporting Improvement - Invoice Report with Line Item Details
Description:
Summary:

Enable users to view and download a monthly invoice line item report directly within the Invoice Reports or Invoices section. This report should display all invoices with their detailed line items (item name, quantity, rate, amount, etc.) for a selected month and allow export to Excel.

Customer Description (verbatim):

“I want to add a custom report I created called Invoice Detail per Month (Line Item Report) in a fashion so I can click it on the invoice report section and it renders a list of ALL invoices with the line items in a certain month and I can download it (not sure if the report is correct).

OR it could also be another View on the Invoices section called for example Shared Invoices Detail, which would then list all line items.

Bottom line: I do not want just the total of the invoice but the line items which detail item, quantity etc.

It needs to be downloadable as an Excel.”

Problem / Current Limitation:

Currently, users can only access invoice totals or limited detail reports. There is no built-in option to display or export all line items for invoices within a specified date range (e.g., per month). This makes it difficult for users to analyze sales performance, item usage, or reconcile invoice-level data externally.
Solution:
Proposed Solution:

Add a new report or view option within the Invoices module that includes:
A “Line Item Detail” report accessible under the Invoice Reports section.

OR

A new “Shared Invoices Detail” view under the Invoices tab.

Both options should:
Allow filtering by month or custom date range.
Display invoice line items (Item, Description, Quantity, Rate, Amount, Tax, etc.).
Support Excel download/export directly from the interface.

Title: Vendor Management — Track Inventory Levels
Description:
Summary

Enable vendors to securely log in to Method CRM and view inventory levels relevant to them — including current stock, reorder points, and pending purchase orders — without granting full system access.

Problem / Why

Vendors currently rely on email or phone communication to check stock or replenishment needs.

This leads to delays, miscommunication, and inefficient order fulfillment.

Businesses that manage multiple vendors or just-in-time inventory need vendors to proactively monitor inventory levels and plan production or delivery accordingly.
Solution:
Create a Vendor Portal or extend the existing Customer Portal framework to:

Allow vendor logins with role-based permissions.

Display key inventory data filtered by vendor (e.g., only items supplied by that vendor).

Include:

Current on-hand quantities

Reorder points and reorder quantities

Open POs and expected delivery dates

Downloadable/exportable reports (CSV/Excel)

Optional: Enable vendors to confirm upcoming shipments or restock plans directly from the portal.

Title: Field Crew Improvement -  Terms and Conditions Input

Title: Google Calendar Improvement - Utilizing Activity Address Fields for Google Calendar Intergration.
Description:
Currently, when use the google calendar integration the location will be based on the contacts billing / shipping address. Since we now have job sites on work orders (by extension activities) we should be leveraging that for specific location address.

Title: Global Search Improvement - Add Transactions as a Search option

Title: Customer List Improvement - Ability to show Customer Tax Exemption

miscalculation of taxes is a major friction point for many customers and making sure that users are well aware when No tax calculation are due to customer being tax exempt.

Title: Multi-Entity Improvement - Have a Shared Preferences table
Description:
Issue: When managing multiple sub-entities, there are specific templates we'd like to use. If we use the PreferencesMethod table; we have to ensure that every single record across all sub-entities need to be populated. This means if even one sub-entity did not update a particular field, they'll face issues.

Resolution:  If we have a shared preferences table with a blank TenantID, we can debug more accurately if there's an issue.

Impact: By allowing for a shared preferences table, we apply it to more parts of Method where standardization needs to be applied and where only corporate (HQ) can control the settings. For example, if we need franchises to send out an SMS for a Google Review and we want to standardize the template, corporate should be able to standardize that and use it throughout all sub-entities without having to update every preferencesmethod record.
Solution:
While this can be customized by blanking out tenantID.

Probably would be better to be system managed.

Title: Invoice Improvement - Automatic PO Creation to Vendor When Customer Payment is Received
Description:
When a customer payment is received and applied to an invoice, automatically create a Purchase Order (PO) to the vendor captured on that invoice, following predefined rules. This reduces manual work, ensures vendors are paid/ordered from on time, and tightens the link between AR and AP/fulfillment.

Problem / Pain
Today, when a customer pays an invoice, the next step of creating a PO to the vendor is manual.

This leads to:
Delays in creating POs and fulfilling the order.
Human error (POs missed or created for the wrong vendor/amount).
Extra admin time spent copying information from invoices to POs.
Users want a way to automate the PO creation step when payment comes in, so the process is consistent and less reliant on memory.
Solution:
High-Level Behaviour

Trigger Event
Trigger fires when:
A payment is recorded, and
That payment is applied to an invoice (fully or partially, depending on configuration).

Vendor Identification
The automation looks up the vendor associated with the invoice. This could be:
A vendor field on the invoice header, or
Vendor(s) tied to the line items (if supported).

PO Creation
System automatically creates a new Purchase Order record:
Vendor = vendor captured on the invoice.

Line items = configurable:
Option A: Mirror invoice line items.
Option B: Use a default PO template (e.g., single line item “Fulfillment for Invoice #1234”).

Reference:
PO should reference the source Invoice ID/number and Payment ID/number.
Include link or reference field in PO pointing back to the invoice.

Status & Notifications
Default PO status (e.g., “Draft”, “Pending Approval”, or “Open”) should be configurable.
Optional: Notify a user or team (email, in-app notification) when the PO is created automatically.

Title: Migrations - ACT!  to Method - CRM Migration from ACT to Method
Description:
1.) There are a number of custom fields and information they want to capture
2.) Most of their data lives in QuickBooks; however, some importing is required.
Solution:
Expectations should be set that migrating to any new system; will never be seamless and will take time and energy from all parties. 

While this estimate is high, the objective is to educate the user on the process and why it takes time. In particular the impact it can have on their operations since we're so tightly integrated with QuickBooks.

Technically, 

First and foremost, the schema needs to be setup. 
- What information is coming from Salesforce
- What format
- How much of it is duplicated by information in QuickBooks and is it necessary

Importing is generally laborious because 
- Multiple imports to link records
- Analysis to remove duplicates

Once the import and data transfer occurs we have to explore how the data will be suraced up to the user.
- Conditionally displaying fields depending on other values
- Special permission considerations

Training on new features and integrations

Title: Payment Widget Improvement -  Ability to Pass Through Bank Statement Info
Description:
Issue: We need to be able to enter Purchase Order # onto the bank statement when a customer pays using the payment widget.

Reasoning: Mobility City has a contract with VA's and they need the Work Order # or the PO # associated with the payment on their bank statements. This way, they don't need a unique workflow for just Mobility City when the VA are reconciling their payments.

Impact: This will allow for our customer's customer (specifically in B2B businesses) to have an easier time reconciling their data.
Solution: Cannot be customized today.
Note from Product: Nov 24, 2025 (Phil) - Not possible via Shuttle today but will see if this is something we can implement and have possible with Method Pay.

Title: Global Sign Out All Users / Forced Logout Control
Description:
Problem / Why This Matters

Right now, if we need all users to be logged out (for example: security concerns, scheduled maintenance, policy changes, or device loss), there is no simple way to force a global sign-out from the system.

This creates a few issues:

Security risk: If a device is lost, shared, or compromised, user sessions might remain active until they time out or the user manually logs off.

Operational friction: For system-wide maintenance or permission changes, we may need everyone to log out and back in to ensure new settings apply, but there’s no way to enforce this.

Poor admin experience: Admins have no centralized control over active sessions and can’t quickly react to urgent scenarios.
Solution: This cannot be customized; however, upon request - this may be something our development team will be able to aid and assist with.

Title: API Integration - Clickup / Click up
Description:
Many customers use ClickUp for project and task management, while using Method for CRM, sales, and billing.

Today, teams are duplicating data between Method and ClickUp (customers, jobs, tasks, statuses), which leads to:

Manual data entry and human error
Out-of-date project status in Method
Poor visibility for sales/CS into delivery progress

Customers want a single source of truth for financials and customer data (Method/QuickBooks), while keeping their preferred PM tool (ClickUp).
Solution: The scope should be further evaluated with a consultant; however, with the open api they're definitely able to connect the systems.

Title: OCR Integration -  to Scan Documents or Images into Method
Description:
Use Case; Customer works with vehicles and there are many errors when manually copying VIN numbers or other documents into method (user error). They are looking for a solution to take a photo of a car's VIN and have that map to a Method Field. 

Additional Use cases - This would help solve two issues; 
1. Allow for field crew users to take notes on pen and paper where internet is limited then map fields back into method once service returns. 
2. Allow for larger data entry back into method for custom or stock solutions.
Solution: Recommendation: Explore feasibility of OCR + field mapping + trigger workflow capabilities via: 3rd party OCR integrations (e.g., Microsoft Azure Form Recognizer, Google Cloud Vision) Custom parser logic to convert scan → JSON → Method record UI configuration to allow for manual override/review before approval Priority: Medium-High (High if similar field-based prospects emerge) Next Steps with Lead: Advised prospect to trial Method + sync with QB Desktop if interested in further evaluation. Prospect agreed to “do some digging” and may revisit if a workaround or future solution is possible.

Title: Entity Tag Improvement - Have tags either applied downwards to contacts or summarized upwards to the entity

There are also conditions were we wouldn't want some tags to flow beyond stages.

Update the percentage field on screen with this calculation, you may need to use 5 decimal places so that the final transaction amount is accurate.

Title: Automated Email-to-Activity Creation for RFQs
Description:
Summary:
Customers would like the ability for incoming emails from a designated sales inbox (e.g., requests for quotes) to be automatically logged in Method CRM as Activities — specifically into a custom “Open RFQ Log.”

Customer Need / Use Case:

A customer wants incoming emails that arrive at their main sales email address to automatically generate an Activity record in Method without any manual action.

These emails often contain RFQs (Requests for Quote).

The user currently needs to manually trigger the “Create Activity from Email” workflow via the Method Outlook add-in, which slows down their process.

Their goal is full automation:

Detect incoming email →

Create new Activity in Method →

Assign it to “Open RFQ Log” →

Optionally attach the email body or PDF attachments.

Title: Detect User Dark Mode Preference for UI Logic & Styling
Description:
Provide a way within Method (including Web-to-Lead forms and customer-facing portals) to detect whether a user’s browser or OS is using Dark Mode, so UI elements (such as images, logos, and styling) can dynamically adapt for better usability and accessibility.

Introduce Dark Mode detection within Method that:

Exposes this as a usable condition within Method logic and workflows

Allows customers to:

Conditionally display different images (logos/icons)

Adjust CSS/styling

Improve accessibility and brand consistency


Business & UX Impact:

Improves end-user experience and accessibility

Prevents branding issues caused by poor contrast

Aligns Method with modern web standards and user expectations

Reduces custom CSS/JS workarounds for customers

Title: Action Set For Grid Filter Change
Description: We are looking for a way to either refer to the current view filter of a grid the same way we would a radio button or a dropdown, or have an action set associated with a grid view filter change.

Title: Import - Sales Orders from a CSV

He wants the ability to take this custom worksheet, and import it directly into QuickBooks.

Since an import requires two levels:
Sales Order & Sales Order Line

Solution:
The solution is a little complex:

1.) Create a custom table that matches the same format as the CSV and plays nicely with QuickBooks (items, customers etc.)
2.) Build a custom screen to process the records in that table and combine (items) for a single customer into a unique order for that customer vs. having different lines. 
3.) Depending on how many steps the user want's in between to verify / approve the order the solution can be quite simple or difficult. 

5-10 hours is a starting point for this customization.

Title: Companies Instead of Contacts App
Description: This app is for users who want to separate their Companies from their Jobs. The Companies List screen displays a list of only parent Customer records. There is also a View Company screen included which has a list of Contacts and a list of Jobs for the active Company (Customer) record. Each of these grids has a row click into the Contacts app.
Solution: Solution Available in the Customization Catalog

Title: Convert a Text field into a DropDown
Description:
The “Group ID”, field is currently a text field, want to make this a dropdown field where you choose an option from a provided list instead of this being a manual entry field. (See attachment)

This will keep the group # consistent across all items and prevent the same group #’s from being entered in numerous different ways (for example Group 11A / Group 11-A / 11A / group 11a etc.) 

It should have a screen to create groups for this field as necessary.
Solution:
1. You need to export the table that the field is contained within. 
2. Extract the DISTINCT values
3. Hide the regular text fields on all screens where it is found
4. Create a new dropdown field which contains the DISTINCT values extract
5. Put the newly-created dropdown in the text fields former position
6. On dropdown.change add logic to populate hidden text field with the selected value to all pertaining screens
7. Create screen to manage dropdown values
8. (Optional) Repurpose logic that was previously updating the text field, if any. 

This can turn to Medium depending on how many screens/logic required to repurpose the text field

Title: Importing - Invoice Import (Transaction Import)
Description:
Attached is a data sheet that needs to be imported to Inovices  

- They get a report like this every month that they want to import into the CRM
- So along with this import, also need to customize the ability to import on a regular basis

OPTION 1 - Import for Invoice Custom Fields only (5 - 6 Hours)

-Addition of fields to New/Edit Invoice screen, screen changes (1-2 Hours)

Title: App Routine - Automate / Simplify Batch Merging Customers / Contacts
Description:
1) Wants to build an app routine to run daily to merge duplicate entities 

- They run into this issue constantly as they have different offices around USA and some work within Method, and others solely in QB

2) Email notification to a user Julie, when a SO is created by certain Sales Reps

- Sales  reps will need to be defined by territory, and only need the email notification to be sent out when some of these territories are in question.
Solution:
This is a pretty simple solution in theory:

1.) I would probably avoid an app routine to automatically merge 
2.) I would probably first create a list and show a report to the end user of duplicate entities.
3.) Based off this list she would then be able to process the duplicates herself manually using a merge screen. 

If we did do this through app routines, remember there is no going back so we would need to make sure we put enough conditions or rules that don't result in accidental data loss.

Title: Opportunities - Creating Different Opportunity Flows (Different Sets of Stages) with Activities
Description:
Customer has two major opportunity flows:

1.) The sales of the equipment flow
2.) The sales of the installation and services flow

Both have different stages and needs that need to be tracked sometimes separately, sometimes together.
Solution:
This is rather an easy customization. We can probably fit it in small category as well totaly between ( 3 - 6 hours) , here are the main parts that needs to be set: 

1. Add screen to relate Stages to Opportunity type & customize New/Edit opportunity screen to filter dropdown accordingly. This way it wont be limited to only 2 types, if in future they need to increase the opportunity types they don't need to come back to us.   (~ 1 - 2 hours)

2. Do we need to link activities to each stage? What if the previous activity is not completed yet? do we want to complete previous activities and create another one? Should it be for the same date or they want to follow up in few days? Depending on the answers we can plan the best way to move forward.  (2 - 4  hours)

Title: Lead Scoring
Description:
Customer is looking for a way to score leads.
Is this something we're able to customize?
Solution:
TL;DR - Scoring is possible, it's all about what criteria the customer wants to use to score. 

Scoring a lead is certainly possible.
The scope of the project could be very small or very large.

It all depends on variables that the customer will need to set.
For example,

1.) Have they purchased before?
2.) What industry are they in?
3.) What is the company size?

Etc. depending on the answers to these questions, we can setup a scoring system that will update dynamically based on actions we take.

For example, if they're a strong lead based off industry but have never had a meeting with us, we could score lower. Also as the lead ages, we could also score the lead lower.

QuickBooks is able to do this inline when creating a new transaction.
Solution:
I would add a new button close to the Estimate Lines. This would then open a new tab for the New Item Type (where you select the Item Type before creating the Item it's self). Once you add the Item, it can then be used in the Estimate Lines.

If they use Mobile, I would add this button on the Add Item screen as well.

Title: Assign a preferred Vendor to Donation Class / Class
Description:
Nonprofit customer has many sub-programs their donors can donate directly to.
Potentially a custom dropdown field in the Classes app to pick a vendor from their vendor list.

Each program has a vendor and they would like to build a vendor notification email automation so that whenever a donation goes into a program, the 'preferred Vendor' received an email notification.

Further, if the preferred vendor is possible, they would like to use our bills app to automate a bill of the donation amount to the preferred vendor.
Solution:
This is three separate asks but all should be doable within 10 hours assuming there aren't any other details that we would need to consider.

Creating a custom dropdown will take one minute. Adding it to all the relevant txn screens and/or preferences screens will take about an hour.

Creating a scheduled app routine to check if the Vendor has already been emailed, if not, send the email. This will take about upwards of 4 hours to implement and perform basic tests. We would need to call this app routine from the Donate Now page as well as the New/Edit Donation screen. Since it's a scheduled daily app routine, that means and donations coming into Method from outside of Method will also trigger the emails to be sent to Vendors, however, that will happen the next calendar day (meaning a delay of 1 day).

Creating Bills for Vendors can also be automated via app routine if needed. This may take several hours (3-5 hours) and require specific rules and checks, for example:

a) Has a Bill already been created for this specific Donation?

b) Should we think about Donations that have been edited since the Bill was created and now update the Bill or create a new bill?

c) Will Bill's ever be created outside of Method? If so, we need to think about that and come up with a way of locating them so as not to duplicate Bills.

Michael Melo - Sept 20, 2024

Title: Tables & Fields Improvement - The ability to 'duplicate' a table
Description:
Duplicating a table is really helpful for a couple of reasons:

1.) It enables me to create a table that matches the schema of another table for importing.
- For example, importing into the activity table - Ideally I would just like to copy the activity table
- Convert all the fields to “plain text” so I can import into this “Staging Table”

Then use actions to import the data.

2.) In general this does help us create apps / tables that share similar structures but keep the data separate. 
- For example, an Inspection List vs. an Installation List. 

The same information is captured; however, for a number of reasons, it’s easier to manage this information in two separate places vs. reporting on it all in the same place. 

3.) Keeping records of historical changes
- For example, a packing slip on a Sales Order
- As things are shipped out, we may want to keep track of items that are shipping.

QB enables us to keep track of how many are shipping up until today.
However, we might want to keep a log of shipping records, this is especially helpful for partial shipments. 

Saves customizers hours of creating tables and schemas.

Title: Tables & Fields Improvement - Allow me to set a default value when creating a new field

Then i need to:

Would save me 20 minutes of work updating, creating screens, or importing values. 
Over the course of 5-6 new tables.'

Title: Xero Improvement - Support Contact Groups - Sync with Custom Contact Groups
Description:
Default contact groups
Xero sorts your contacts when you first enter an invoice, bill, or credit note transaction for them. They’ll display in the All group, which shows all your contacts, as well as either the Customers or Suppliers group.

Customers are added when you create a sales invoice or customer credit note.
Suppliers are added when you create a bill or supplier credit note.
You can't manually add contacts as Customers or Suppliers.
You also can't remove a contact from the All, Customers or Suppliers groups. 
Contacts won’t be assigned to the Customers or Suppliers groups when you create a spend or receive money transaction. 
If you void the first transaction where a contact appears in Xero, the contact doesn't display in the Customers or Suppliers groups, but it does display in All.

Custom contact groups
You can create custom groups to organise your contacts to suit your organisation’s needs.
Use contact groups to create invoices for multiple contacts at once.
Filter sales and purchases reports by contact groups.
If you want to group contacts you're expecting to become customers or suppliers before adding any transactions, you can create temporary contact groups.
Removing a contact from a group doesn't delete the contact from your organisation.
Solution: At this time cannot be customized.

Title: Customization Improvement - Allow Duplicating Sections
Description:
Request: Similar to how we're able to duplicate objects such as Charts, Grids, Controls; it would be nice to be able to duplicate sections.

Use Case: When customizing, there may be a section that has padding on specific cells, or specific sections within the section. Sometimes, we need that same section with the same formatting to be on another section.

Benefit: It would reduce time customizing and having to remember/go through each cell/section that has formatting to recreate it.
Solution: Great request.

Title: Generate Timesheets from Time Tracking Entries
Description:
Summary:
Enable users to generate structured timesheets based on time-tracking entries in Method CRM, streamlining payroll and billing processes.

Problem Statement:
Currently, users track time in Method CRM, but there is no built-in way to generate a summarized timesheet based on those entries. This results in a manual and time-consuming process for businesses that need to review, approve, and process time entries for payroll or invoicing.

Proposed Solution:
Introduce a Timesheet Generation feature that allows users to:

Select a Date Range – Filter time entries by week, bi-weekly, or custom date ranges.
Group by User/Project/Job – Organize entries based on team members, customers, or work orders.
Summarize Totals – Automatically calculate total hours worked per category.
Export & Share – Download timesheets in CSV/PDF format for payroll or invoicing.
Approval Workflow (Optional) – Allow managers to review and approve timesheets before finalizing them.
Business Impact:

Saves time by reducing manual data entry and calculations.
Improves accuracy by ensuring time entries are correctly grouped and totaled.
Enhances reporting by providing a clear breakdown of work done over a period.
Supports billing for service-based businesses that invoice based on time worked.
Additional Considerations:

Should this integrate with QuickBooks Payroll or other payroll systems?
Should there be permission controls for who can generate/export timesheets?
Solution:
In general, this is a pretty complex customization.

QuickBooks Desktop offers this functionality out of the box; QBO less so but solutions are available.

Title: API Integration - Weather
Description:
Summary:
Integrate a Weather API into the system to provide real-time weather data that can enhance decision-making, scheduling, and customer engagement.

Use Case Scenarios:

Logistics & Shipping – Companies can anticipate weather conditions affecting shipping routes and delivery times.

Field Service Management – Businesses with outdoor workforces (e.g., landscaping, construction) can adjust schedules based on forecasts.
Solution:
Basic Steps:
Obtain an API Key

Register for a weather API (e.g., OpenWeatherMap, WeatherAPI, or WeatherStack).
Get an API key for authentication.
Convert Address to Coordinates (if required)

If the weather API requires latitude/longitude instead of an address, you need a geocoding API (e.g., Google Maps API or OpenCage).
Call the geocoding API with the address to get latitude and longitude.
Call the Weather API

Use the latitude and longitude (or directly use the address if supported) to fetch weather data.
Make an HTTP request (GET) to the weather API endpoint.
Process and Display the Data

Parse the JSON response.
Extract relevant information (temperature, humidity, forecast, etc.).
Format the data for display in your application.

Title: API Integration - TMS Asset Management
Description:
Summary:
Develop an integration between Method:CRM and Accruent TMS, a leading asset management system, to enable seamless synchronization of asset data, work orders, and maintenance schedules.

Problem Statement:
Many businesses using Method:CRM also rely on Accruent TMS for asset tracking and facility management. Currently, there is no direct integration, leading to manual data entry, inefficiencies, and potential errors. An API integration would streamline operations, improve accuracy, and enhance productivity.

Proposed Solution:
Leverage Accruent TMS’s API to create a bi-directional sync between Method:CRM and TMS. The integration should include:

Asset Sync: Automatically pull asset records from TMS into Method:CRM.
Work Order Sync: Allow users to create, update, and track work orders in Method that reflect in TMS.
Maintenance Scheduling: Sync scheduled maintenance and service history to keep records up to date in both platforms.
Status Updates: Ensure asset conditions and service logs update in real-time between both systems.
Use Cases:

A company using Accruent TMS for facility maintenance can track asset conditions and schedule repairs directly within Method:CRM.
Sales and customer service teams can access asset status within Method without switching between platforms.
Automated alerts in Method can notify customers about upcoming maintenance or required actions based on TMS data.
Expected Benefits:

Reduce duplicate data entry.
Improve data accuracy and efficiency.
Provide a centralized view of asset status within Method.
Enhance customer service by ensuring up-to-date asset and service history is available in CRM.
Solution:
Integrations of this magnitude are quite large. 

I would explore the equipment app catalog as a mechanism to manage assets. 

It's a great starting point for the app.

Title: Proposals Improvement – Better Integration with Work Orders
Description:
Summary
Customers using Method Proposals for Work Order authorization want an automated workflow where a Proposal is marked as 'Completed' and charged using the credit card on file when the related Work Order is completed.

Use Case
Currently, when a Work Order is completed, the corresponding Proposal remains open, requiring manual intervention to close it and process payment. This additional step creates workflow inefficiencies, especially for businesses handling high volumes of work orders.

By automating Proposal completion and payment processing, businesses can:

Reduce administrative overhead (no manual tracking and closing)
Ensure faster payments (auto-charge eliminates delays in invoicing)
Improve customer experience (streamlined process from approval to job completion)
Solution:
Proposed Solution
Auto-Mark Proposal as 'Completed'

When a Work Order is marked as 'Completed', the linked Proposal automatically updates its status to 'Completed'.

Automatic Payment Processing

If a credit card is on file, automatically charge the card upon Proposal completion.
If no card is on file, trigger a payment request email to the customer.

Configuration Options (to meet different business needs)

Admins can enable/disable Auto-Completion and Auto-Charging in Proposal Settings.
Option to set a delay (X days) before charging to allow review time.

Title: API Integration - Salesforce

Improved operational efficiency and reduced administrative overhead.

Real-time visibility into financial data within Salesforce, enabling quicker decision-making and streamlined sales processes.

Greater flexibility and customization through Method:CRM’s open API.

Develop or document existing endpoints within Method:CRM’s open API to support Salesforce-specific integration scenarios.

Ensure compatibility with Salesforce’s latest API versions to facilitate smooth data exchange.

Provide detailed documentation and support resources to assist customers with custom integration efforts.

Evaluate feasibility and scope of work.

Explore existing API capabilities and determine required development resources.

Schedule a customer meeting to gather further requirements and clarify use-cases.

Title: API Integration - True Commerce

Title: Activity App Improvement - Filter Opportunity List better / provide recommendations / ai suggestions

Title: Activity Improvement - Contact-Level Activity Tracking (Next/Last Activity)
Description:
Summary:
Current activity tracking is based on the entity/account level, meaning it aggregates all activities related to the company, regardless of which contact is involved. This limits visibility when users are engaging with specific individuals within an organization.

Current Limitation:

“Next Activity” and “Last Activity” fields only reflect data at the entity level.

Users working with tracked contacts or specific stakeholders cannot see the actual recency or upcoming engagement for that contact.

Proposed Enhancement:

Introduce Contact-Level Activity fields:

“Next Activity (Contact)”

“Last Activity (Contact)”

These fields should reflect only the activities logged with that specific contact.

Benefits:

Improves accountability and follow-up for relationship owners.

Reduces confusion when multiple departments (e.g., support, sales) are engaging the same client for different reasons.

Enables more personalized and timely outreach.

Use Case:
A sales rep may be assigned to Jane Doe at ACME Corp. Support is actively working with John Smith at the same company, so the current “Last Activity” shows “yesterday.” In reality, the rep hasn’t followed up with Jane in over a month.
Solution:
This is something that can be customized; however, given the use of contacts throughout the app - this is better suited for a stock app change. 

In theory, we'd just need an additional field for Last Activity and Next Activity on the contact that reports on the specific contact vs. the entity.

Title: Activity Improvement - User-Specific Next Activity Summary (Per-User View)
Description:
Summary:
Today, users can view the “Next Activity” for a client, but it shows the next activity assigned to any user. There is no way to easily see 'My Next Activity' for that account or contact, creating blind spots in individual pipelines.

Current Limitation:

“Next Activity” displays the soonest scheduled action, regardless of assignee.

Users cannot quickly see what their own next engagement is for a given customer or lead.

Proposed Enhancement:

Add a “My Next Activity” field to accounts and contacts, showing the next activity assigned to the logged-in user.

Make this field available on list views, reports, and dashboards.

Benefits:

Enables better personal follow-up and activity prioritization.

Helps users stay focused on their own tasks, without needing to dig into activity logs.

Avoids confusion when other departments are also active on the same account.

Use Case:
A CSM views a client profile and sees a “Next Activity” for a support follow-up tomorrow—but their own follow-up call scheduled for next week is hidden in the timeline. Having “My Next Activity” visible ensures they don't miss it.
Solution:
In sales this is critical for us. 

Because we can't depend on Next and Last Activity, we've wired up custom activities to make sure a followup is always planned and a next activity is ready to go. 

In reality this is a bit more difficult to customize as the information cannot be stored anywhere (eg. with 100 users, there is no way to maintain the last activity. 

I would in general recommend a LastSalesRepActivity and NextSalesRepActivity, this way owners of the customer or contact know their next reachouts seperated from general CRM activities.
